Name: Association of the United States Army - AUSA
Description:
The Association of the United States Army is a nonprofit educational and professional development association serving America’s Total Army, our Soldiers, Army civilians, and their families; our industry partners, and supporters of a strong national defense. AUSA provides a voice for the Army, supports the Soldier, and honors those who have served in order to advance the security of the nation.

AUSA educates its members, the public, industry, and Congress about the critical nature of land warfare and the Army’s central role in national defense.

AUSA informs its members, our communities, and Congress about issues affecting America’s Army and the Soldiers who serve in the Regular Army, Army National Guard, and Army Reserve.

AUSA connects the Army to the American people at the national, regional, and chapter levels.

Name: AUKUS Forum
Description:
The nuclear powered submarine project with AUKUS thrusts Australia into a whole new world where innovation and technology rule.

AUKUS will be much more than nuclear submarines and the opportunities will expand into infrastructure and energy too.

AUKUS is already expanding from nuclear with particular emphasis on cyber capabilities, artificial intelligence, quantum technologies and more.

Moderated by Michael Sharpe

See AUKUS Forum - https://aukusforum.com

Name: DGRIS - Direction générale des relations internationales et de la stratégie
Description:
La Direction générale des relations internationales et de la stratégie (DGRIS) contribue à la politique internationale coordonnée par le ministère de l'Europe et des Affaires étrangères et pilote l’action internationale du ministère des Armées.

Rattachée directement au ministre, la Direction générale des relations internationales et de la stratégie (DGRIS) pilote l’action internationale du ministère des Armées en y associant l’Etat-major des armées (EMA), la Direction générale de l’armement (DGA) et le Secrétariat général pour l’administration (SGA).

La DGRIS conduit également les travaux de prospective stratégique et coordonne, pour le ministère des Armées, ceux nécessaires à la préparation du Livre blanc sur la défense et la sécurité nationale et à son actualisation régulière. Elle veille, en liaison avec l’EMA, la DGA et le SGA, à l’articulation entre la stratégie de défense et la programmation militaire.

Elle représente le ministère auprès des autres départements ministériels pour les questions touchant à l’action internationale, à l’exception de la conduite des opérations des armées, des coopérations en matière d’armement et du soutien aux exportations pour lesquels elle apporte son appui et se coordonne avec l’état-major des armées et la direction générale de l’armement qui restent responsables de ces domaines. Elle définit la stratégie d’influence internationale du ministère des Armées et coordonne sa mise en œuvre.

Pour remplir ses missions, la DGRIS est composée d’une direction et de trois services.

La DGRIS pilote le réseau diplomatique de défense, qui est constitué des missions de défense (MdD) bilatérales ainsi que des représentations militaires et de la défense (RMD) et des conseillers militaires des représentants permanents auprès des organisations internationales (UE, OTAN, ONU, OSCE, Conférence du désarmement, OTICE, OIAC).

Name: Commissariat au Numérique de Défense - CND
Naval Special Warfare Center
Description: NSW Center is one of the eight major commands that comprise Naval Special Warfare Command, the Navy’s premier commando force and the maritime component of Special Operations Command. Strengths

- Highly trained and motivated personnel

- Strong international partnerships
- Unwavering commitment to national security
- Selfless service to the nation and its people

Connect

Join LinkedIn to stay updated on latest news, achievements of PAF. Let's work together to build a stronger and more prosperous Pakistan.

Name: Office of the Secretary of the Navy

The principal officials of the Secretariat include the under secretary of the Navy (the secretary's principal civilian deputy), the assistant secretaries of the Navy (ASN), the general counsel of the Navy, the judge advocate general of the Navy (JAG), the Naval inspector general (NIG), the chief of Legislative Affairs, and the chief of naval research. Name: Small Wars Journal
Description:
The new Small Wars Journal, under the leadership of ASU’s Future Security Initiative, publishes serious, authentic voices from across the spectrum of small wars stakeholders. We define small wars as conflict and competition below the threshold of major interstate conventional wars. Irregular warfare, competitive statecraft, “grey-zone”, and liminal strategies and approaches are all part of this exploration. Our goal is to add richness, breadth, and depth to the active dialog that occurs in many cloistered or siloed venues be they academic, government, or private sector.  In this, we seek input from everyone ranging from the soldier and interagency practitioners of small wars to their leadership, to those that make and inform policy at the national and international level.  These are international issues, so we encourage international participation. We are non-partisan and do not support any political ideology. The views of published authors are their own. Likes, comments, & reposts ≠ official endorsements. 

We believe that responsive publishing and open dialog around well-formed ideas grounded in experience and/or deep study (ideally both) serves our community better than the protracted processes found in other venues to incrementally advance the rigor of a piece before its eventual exposure to light.  We want to publish viewpoints on today's issues today, rather than in months or years.

Visit our peer-reviewed sister-publication, Inter Populum: The Journal of Irregular Warfare and Special Operations.

Name: Ghana Military Academy
Description: The Ghana Military Academy is the Military Academy of the Ghana Armed Forces. It offers basic military training to officer cadets from the Ghana Army, Ghana Navy and the Ghana Air Force. The academy is based in Teshie in the Greater Accra Region.

Integrating the most powerful combat credible maritime forces on Earth into NATO operations, deterring adversaries & defending our territory & people

Name: NATO STABILITY POLICING CENTRE OF EXCELLENCE
Description:
The NATO Stability Policing Centre of Excellence is an international organization, funded by 8 countries.

We want to operate as a prime mover to increase contribution to the Alliance stability and reconstruction efforts in conflict and post-conflict scenarios and to provide an unique capability to Alliance, NATO Nations, NATO partners and International Organizations in the area of Stability Policing.

Name: NATO Innovation Hub
Description: The NATO Innovation Hub is no longer operational; activities have been incorporated into the Innovation Branch of NATO Allied Command Transformation.

Name: InterForce Danmark
Description:
InterForce er en organisation, der er tilknyttet Forsvaret.

Vi er sat i verden for at få et tæt samarbejde med det civile arbejdsmarked. Særligt med de mange virksomheder - både private og offentlige - som har medarbejdere ansat, der er en del af Reservestyrken.

Reservestyrken består af mænd og kvinder, der er medlemmer af Reserven. Men den består også af de mange frivillige i Hjemmeværnet, Beredskabsstyrelsen og Kystredningstjenesten.

Det er en belastning for enhver virksomhed at skulle undvære centrale medarbejdere i de dage, hvor han eller hun skal stille op i uniform. Enten for at efteruddanne sig eller for at hjælpe til i krisesituationer.

Vi ønsker at minimere disse gener så meget som muligt. I et tæt samarbejde med det civile arbejdsmarked.

Så Forsvaret kan trække på medlemmerne af Reservestyrken, når det spidser til. Og så det civile arbejdsmarked også føler. at de får noget igen.

Det er det, vi arbejder for i InterForce.

Over 3.000 danske virksomheder - private og offentlige - har indtil videre valgt at støtte op om InterForce. Tilsammen repræsenterer de over 1.000.000 medarbejdere. 

På den måde er de med til at støtte op om Danmarks sikkerhed i en usikker tid.

Name: MFO - Multinational Force & Observers
Description:
The Multinational Force & Observers (MFO), headquartered in Rome, is an independent international organization, created by agreement between the Arab Republic of Egypt and the State of Israel, with peacekeeping responsibilities in the Sinai.
 
The origins of the MFO lie in Annex I to the 1979 Treaty of Peace between Egypt and Israel, in which the parties undertook to request the United Nations to provide a force and observers to supervise the implementation of the Treaty. When it did not prove possible to obtain Security Council approval for the stationing of a UN peacekeeping force in the Sinai, the parties negotiated a Protocol in 1981 establishing the MFO “as an alternative” to the envisioned UN force.

Name: Nuclear Taskforce
Description:
Australia’s nuclear-powered submarine program will be the most transformative industrial endeavour in the nation's history – exceeding in scale, complexity, and economic significance the creation of an Australian automotive manufacturing sector and the construction of the Snowy Hydro Scheme.

Developing a pipeline of highly skilled STEM graduates and a talented skilled workforce will build Australia’s sovereign industry capabilities, playing a crucial role in delivering on our AUKUS commitments, and strengthening national security.

Name: SOFEX Jordan
Description:
Special Operations Forces and Homeland Security are doing more things in more places than ever before. They seek ways to tackle a range of security threats worldwide, shore up the resilience of their friends and allies against terrorist and criminal networks, and minimize the need for large-scale military interventions. The importance of Special Operations Forces and Homeland Security is growing spontaneously.

Founded in 1996 by His Majesty King Abdullah II, the Special Operations Forces Exhibition and Conference (SOFEX) continues to grow edition on edition, acquiring paramount ranking amongst the specialized defense exhibitions.

SOFEX provides an ideal platform to present and demonstrate new and innovative equipment and technologies in order to deliver swift, decisive and effective solutions that will help international Special Operations and Homeland Security decision makers and armed forces maintain the security and safety in their countries, regions and around the world. It is also considered a vital gathering for exchanging views and experiences while strengthening relationships with armed forces, security agencies, governments and key businesses from around the world.

Description: SOCNORTH plans, coordinates, and conducts special operations in collaboration with mission partners, to assure allies and partners, compete below the level of armed conflict, deter conventional and irregular threats, and set conditions to execute contingency operations to defend the United States and its interests.

Description: PEO C4I is the Navy’s first line to acquire the right communication and technology tools to deliver affordable, integrated and interoperable information warfare capabilities to the fleet. It includes eleven program offices responsible for acquiring fielding, and supporting C4I systems extending across Navy, joint, and coalition platforms. Name: Swiss Innovation Forces
Description:
Die Swiss Innovation Forces sind die Antwort der Schweizer Armee auf laufend ändernde Bedrohungen und neue technologische Möglichkeiten. 

Wir machen der Truppe innovative Lösungen schnell und wirkungsvoll verfügbar. Zudem befähigen wir die Menschen in der Armee selber Veränderungen anzustossen.

Name: ORSEM OFFICIERS DE RESERVE SPECIALISTES D'ETAT MAJOR
Description:
En 1899 se crée la Réunion des Officiers de Réserve du Service d’Etat-Major qui organise en son sein des séances de formation d’officiers de réserve aux techniques et travaux d’état-major. Cette initiative faisait suite au constat, partagé par le Haut commandement, de la faible efficacité des états-majors français lors de la guerre de 1870, face à un adversaire qui avait lui-même tiré les enseignements de ses défaites lors des guerres de l’Empire. L’institution militaire s’y intéresse très vite et crée un an plus tard, à partie de cette initiative, l’organisme aujourd’hui dénommé « Ecole Supérieure des Officiers de Réserve Spécialistes d’Etat-Major » (ESORSEM). Cette école est placée dans le giron de l’Ecole Supérieure de Guerre dès 1911. 

Ainsi, depuis sa naissance il y a plus d’un siècle, la filière de formation des ORSEM repose sur deux piliers indissociablement liés et travaillant de concert.

L’ESORSEM (Ecole supérieure des officiers de réserve spécialistes d’état-major) est aujourd’hui l’une des quatre écoles du Centre de Doctrine et d’Enseignement du Commandement (CDEC) de l’Armée de terre. Installée à l’Ecole militaire, elle est dédiée à la formation supérieure des officiers de réserve. Elle a pour mission principale de concevoir, organiser et conduire leur formation de cursus selon quatre niveaux : Stage d’initiation aux techniques d’état-major (SITEM), Stage de certification d’état-major (SCEM), Cours supérieur des officiers de réserve spécialistes d’état-major (CSORSEM, le cours historique) et Brevet technique d’études militaires générales (BTEMG). 

La Réunion des ORSEM, association reconnue d’utilité publique, structure le corps des ORSEM. Adossée à une histoire de plus de 120 ans, elle est colocalisée avec l’ESORSEM. Elle exerce deux missions principales, au cœur du lien armée-nation :
- la première est le soutien à l’ESORSEM dans des domaines aussi variés que le recrutement dans la filière, l’identification d’instructeurs pour les différents stages,

Name: elSnorkel.com
Description: This site was created in July 2000, having as founding spirit, to unite the friendship of the Latin American Submariner Community, a meeting point for professionals and amateurs, sharing experience and anecdotes, a place of debate and source of consultation on all topics related to the origins and evolution of submarines. If you wish to collaborate and/or have material that you think is interesting for the diving community, you are welcome to send it to: contenidos@elsnorkel.com This site has no relation with the Armed Forces of any country.

Name: F3EA, Inc.
Description: F3EA, Inc. is a certified Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB), established in 2013, and dedicated to delivering high quality training and management service solutions to U.S. Government customers primarily within the Special Operations and Intelligence communities. As a provider of mission support, security, and training services; F3EA’s core competency resides in providing full-spectrum support, development, and execution of interagency Emergency Deployment Readiness Exercises (EDREs) and Joint Full Mission Profile (FMP) exercises to enhance performance and improve operational capabilities both domestically and overseas. F3EA’s respected position in the USSOCOM community is built on realistic, demanding, and cutting-edge exercise delivery with the expertise and experience to meet and exceed the performance requirements identified to support Joint Forces Commanders.

Name: STEYR ARMS GmbH
Description:
Starting from the traditional location in Steyr, which also gave the company its name, STEYR ARMS has developed into an international group of companies with locations in Austria and the USA.

STEYR ARMS produces and sells high-quality outdoor products worldwide. In particular, these are hunting, sport and authority weapons, knives and accessories. 

The company redefines the overall hunting experience and transposes these values to other areas of life.

In the government sector, STEYR ARMS is world-renowned as a producer of weapons for the military and police. The proverbial precision and trend-setting developments from STEYR ARMS have always been in demand worldwide.

In direct contact with governments and authorities worldwide, customised solutions for the respective users are realised in cooperation on site.

Name: Commandement de la Gendarmerie pour les Missions Extérieures (CGMEx)
Australian Naval Institute
Description: The ANI was formed and incorporated in the ACT in 1975 as a self-supporting and non-profit making organisation to provide a forum for Naval Officers and others interested in maritime affairs. The main objectives of the Institute are to encourage and promote the advancement of knowledge related to the Navy and the maritime profession; and to provide a forum for the exchange of ideas concerning subjects related to the Navy and the maritime profession.

Name: NATO AWACS
Description:
Official LinkedIn Account of NATO AWACS. We are NATO’s own Airborne Early Warning and Control Force.

The NATO Airborne Early Warning & Control Force (NAEW&CF) is home to NATO’s ‘eyes in the sky’, the E-3A Airborne Warning & Control System (AWACS), which plays an important and unique role for the Alliance by protecting Allied airspace with their air and maritime surveillance capability.

The NAEW&CF has two units with operational capability: The NATO E-3A Component, and the former E-3D Component of the Royal Air Force (RAF) in the United Kingdom. RAF personnel crewed six E-3D aircraft until 2021. Additionally, the Mission Systems Engineering Centre (MSEC), which provides engineering services for the E-3A aircraft mission systems, is also part of the NATO Airborne Early Warning & Control Force. The NAEW&CF Headquarters is responsible for providing direction and guidance to the Force and oversees Force policies.

The NAEW&CF Headquarters, MSEC, and the E-3A Component are located on NATO Air Base Geilenkirchen in Germany. The Component also maintains three forward-operating bases at Konya in Türkiye, Aktion in Greece, Trapani in Italy, and a forward-operating location at Ørland in Norway.

Name: Sri Lanka Navy
Description: Full range of operations in which a nation’s naval forces may be involved is vast, ranging from high intensity war fighting at one end to humanitarian assistance and disaster relief operations at the other end. This broad continuum of operations can be broken down into distinct roles, each demanding a specific approach to the conduct of operations. Accordingly, the three main roles envisaged for the SLN are; Military, Diplomatic and Constabulary.

Name: ILERI Défense
Description:
Notre but ? 

Les membres d'ILERI Défense sont issus de toutes les promotions de l'Institut Libre d'Étude des Relations Internationales (ILERI). Localisée à Courbevoie au 1 rue Sainte-Marie, 92400, l'association a pour vocation de donner aux étudiants un accès à des connaissances approfondies inhérentes au monde de la défense, de la stratégie, de l'armement et de la sécurité internationale.

Nos activités ?

ILERI Défense est une association aux activités diverses : Gestion et simulation de crise et OTAN en passant par des visites de lieux en lien avec le monde de la Défense comme l’Île Longue en mars 2025, les plages du débarquement mais aussi des conférences avec d'éminents membres de nos armées et des experts (historiens, avocats et politiques) sur des thématiques de défense et de sécurité sont régulièrement organisées et des ateliers introduisant les élèves aux milieux de l'armement et de la stratégie sont préparés chaque année par nos membres.

Plus qu’une association ILERI Défense est un véritable acteur du monde de la défense !


Contactez-nous !
Mail : ileri.defense@gmail.com
Instagram : @ileridefense

Name: Afghanistan War Commission
Description:
On December 27, 2021, Section 1094 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 (P.L. 117-81) established the Afghanistan War Commission to:

(1) “examine the key strategic, diplomatic, and operational decisions that pertain to the war in Afghanistan during the relevant period, including decisions, assessments, and events that preceded the war in Afghanistan;” and 

(2) “to develop a series of lessons learned and recommendations for the way forward that will inform future decisions by Congress and policymakers throughout the United States Government.”

Name: Baltic Ghost Wing Center of Excellence
Description:
We are excited to announce the upcoming public launch of the Baltic Ghost Wing Center of Excellence (BGWCE), strategically located in Jõhvi, Estonia. Set to be officially unveiled on May 9, 2024, with a projected start in Fall 2024, BGWCE is poised to redefine the landscape of UAS training and tactical readiness. Drawing inspiration from the renowned "Top Gun" Navy Fighter Weapons School, our approach is based on proven aerial combat training techniques adapted for unmanned systems, ensuring a blend of technical proficiency and tactical acumen.

Why BGWCE? The Center of Excellence is more than just a training facility; it embodies the resilience and spirit of the "Ghost Wings"—civilians and defense leagues united in their dedication to protect their homeland. BGWCE integrates cutting-edge technology with practical tactical training to prepare individuals not just to face challenges but to excel in high-stakes environments.

Advanced Training and Curriculum: Our curriculum, meticulously designed over a 13-week course, focuses on FPV engineering, UAV operations, and tactical deployments. The program is rigorously structured to culminate in real-world applications, ensuring participants are tested and proven under actual field conditions.

Proving Grounds: Our state-of-the-art proving grounds in Ida-Virumaa are a cornerstone of our training methodology, replicating battlefield conditions to test and perfect drone operations. This environment allows for a comprehensive evaluation of UAV endurance, navigation capabilities, and tactical utility, preparing our graduates for any operational scenario.

Impact and Vision: The establishment of BGWCE will catalyze economic growth, drive technological advancements, and foster innovation within Ida-Virumaa. Our goal is to transform the region into a beacon of defense training excellence, influencing global standards in tactical education and operations.

Name: Strategic Systems Programs
Description: Strategic Systems Programs is the Navy command that provides cradle-to-grave lifecycle support for the sea-based leg of the U.S. nuclear triad.

Name: Naval Surface and Mine Warfighting Development Center (SMWDC)
Description: Mission: Increase the tactical proficiency and lethality of the Surface Force across all domains.

Name: Smart Defence
Description:
Smart Defence is a platform that aims to bring together in a single space the individuals and national and international entities that participate in the defence sector, such as the Ministry of Defence, members of the armed forces, diplomatic attachés, private companies, research centres, students, journalists, among others, as well as business opportunities.

The platform's mission is to foster business in the Defence ecosystem, providing visibility to registered entities and available opportunities, such as national and international public tenders. To speed up the search and identification process, a matchmaking system is used to connect user profiles with grants and tenders.

In order to mitigate the bureaucratic difficulties sometimes inherent to the application processes to public programmes, the Legislative Area was also created, which gathers the rules and conditions of the ongoing tenders. In order to contribute to and promote the Defence ecosystem, a Community hub has also been incorporated, for sharing and debating ideas. Finally, the suitability of the entities involved is guaranteed, since their registration is subject to validation by the platform administrator (idD).

idD Portugal Defence is an exclusively public owned company under the joint supervision of the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of Finance.

idD Portugal Defence aims to make the Defence Technological and Industrial Base a relevant international player within the Defence Economy, through the following axes:

Management of the Portuguese public holdings in companies in the Defence sector;

Management of the Technological Defence and Industrial Base (DTIB), including representation of the DTIB and the Portuguese State in the European Union, NATO, and international forums relevant to the Defence Economy.

Name: VIKI - Defence Innovation Research Institute of Hungary
Description:
Defence |  Innovation |  Research

VIKI Defence Innovation Research Institute, part of the Hungarian Ministry of Defence, is a leading Hungarian think tank committed to driving military innovation and development. Our research and development program leverages a cutting-edge test center network to connect end-users in the defense force with the most effective innovators. As the first point of contact for the local ecosystem towards EDF and NATO DIANA programs, we play a crucial role in connecting gaps between innovation and implementation.

Name: NATO Security Assistance and Training for Ukraine (NSATU)
Description:
NATO Security Assistance and Training for Ukraine (NSATU) is a NATO command that coordinates the provision of military equipment and training to Ukraine by NATO member and partner countries.

NSATU places security assistance to Ukraine on an enduring footing, ensuring enhanced, predictable and coherent support for the long term. It brings together much of the security support being provided by the international community to Ukraine under one overarching framework, improving coordination, accountability and burden-sharing among Allies. NSATU, which operates only on Allied territory, helps Ukraine uphold its right to self-defence, as enshrined in the UN Charter; it does not, under international law, make NATO a party to the conflict.

NSATU is nested within broader international efforts to support Ukraine, and works closely with key multinational organizations, such as EU’s Military Assistance Mission in support of Ukraine, and the Ukrainian Defence Contact Group, as well as directly with individual donour countries. It acts as a force multiplier for those generating the resources needed to empower Ukraine’s long-term force development, by providing a regular flow of data, and a better forecast of upcoming developments, in order to better inform their planning efforts.

NSATU is based in Wiesbaden, Germany with logistical hubs in the eastern part of the Alliance. It is reporting to NATO’s Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR) and involves around 350 personnel from NATO member and partner countries, including Ukraine.

Name: Drone Warfare Innovations

The Shift to Asymmetric Attrition
 
The modern battlespace has fundamentally changed. It is no longer defined by "Air Superiority" but by Asymmetric Attrition. The economics of warfare have inverted: cheap, software-defined consumables are defeating exquisite, industrial-age platforms.

Drone-Warfare.com was built to track this shift. 

We are not a news aggregator. We are an analytical engine designed to move the industry observer from Tactical Awareness (what happened) to Operational Understanding (why it matters) and Strategic Prediction (what comes next).

בפרקליטות הצבאית משרתים קצינים, בשירות חובה וקבע, בעלי הכשרה משפטית אקדמית שהם גם עורכי דין. ההצטרפות ליחידה נעשית בדרך כלל במסגרת מסלול העתודה האקדמית. עם זאת, בשורותיה גם קצינים ששרתו בעבר בתפקידים אחרים בצה"ל, בשטח ובמטה, וברבות השנים רכשו השכלה משפטית ונמצאו מתאימים להצטרף ליחידה.  

בוגרי היחידה משולבים במערכת המשפט הציבורית והפרטית במדינת ישראל, ובהם דמויות מפתח בעולם המשפטי הישראלי לדורותיו. ליחידה מערך מילואים רחב, עמו נמנים עורכי דין ותיקים ומנוסים בכלל ענפי המשפט, אשר מסייעים ליחידה למלא את תפקידיה בשגרה ובשעת חירום.

The Military Advocate General's Corps (MAG Corps) was founded prior to the establishment of the State of Israel as the "Legal Service" within "Haganah" (the quasi-military body of the Jewish community and Zionist Movement in the British mandate prior to the establishment of the State of Israel). The MAG Corps, as it is today, was established in 1948 concurrent with the IDF, as the "Military Prosecution". In 1950, the unit was renamed the Military Advocate General's Corps.

The MAG Corps is responsible for implementing the rule of law within the IDF. The unit's objectives include integrating the rule of law amongst IDF commanders and soldiers; providing commanders with the tools for the effective performance of their missions in accordance with the law; and helping the IDF achieve its goals on all legal fronts. Special emphasis is placed on the MAG Corps' ability to provide legal advice in emergencies and during warfare. The MAG Corps operates as the bridge between the legal and military contexts, and as such MAG Corps officers are proficient in both the law as well as the military's needs.

Name: !ntellibot

IntelliBot is an OSINT platform built to convert vast, fragmented public information into structured, analyzable intelligence. It aggregates and standardizes data from 100+ RSS feeds, covering all major countries, across critical intelligence domains including international and regional news, defense and military, security and cyber, think-tank analysis, and international organizations.

To move beyond headlines, IntelliBot integrates high-signal data layers from:

GDELT Project for global event and media monitoring

Metaculus for public prediction markets and probabilistic foresight

CoinGecko for cryptocurrency market intelligence

Frankfurter for real-time foreign exchange data

Designed for policy professionals, researchers, and analysts, IntelliBot reduces hours of manual monitoring into minutes by centralizing sources, revealing correlations, and supporting early-signal detection across geopolitical, economic, and security events.

Built for modern intelligence workflows, where credibility, context, and speed are non-negotiable.

Name: Space Strategies Center
Description:
World Famous Scholar and Space Expert Is Now Providing an Important Lecture on Space Warfare.
The internationally acclaimed expert on space strategies and space warfare, Paul Szymanski, is presenting an on-line lecture on how to fight and win space wars. You are cordially invited to participate in this seminar, and ask questions of Mr. Szymanski afterwards on his vast knowledge of this critical subject. At this unclassified lecture he will present the foundational issues of space warfare including doctrine, strategies and tactics, and provide a history of previous space wars since the 1960’s. He will also provide technical orbital descriptions of space attacks that occurred during the Ukrainian conflict. Finally, he will talk about his assessment of who is the primary space powers around the World and their political implications.
Mr. Szymanski has 50 years’ experience in space war policy, doctrine, strategy, simulations, surveillance, survivability, threat assessment, long-range strategic planning, and command and control. He has published or lectured 170 times in Aviation Week, the United States, Canada, Britain, Scotland, Netherlands, Denmark, Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Greece, Poland, Estonia, Ukraine, India, Taiwan, Australia, New Zealand, Cyprus, Malaysia, and Japan.

Our primary objective is to provide all Service personnel with access to snow and ice sports. Our secondary objective is to train and develop our winter sports athletes to compete and win across eight winter disciplines: Alpine Ski, Snowboard, Telemark, Nordic, Bobsleigh, Bob-Skeleton and Luge, with each having its own competitive teams and representation.

Each team competes in the RN Alpine Ski and Snowboard Championships - the Service's single largest participative sporting event, which hosts sessions for complete beginners, all the way up to those with an expert level of experience. It also offers potential selection for Service representation at the Inter-Service Snowsports Championships, an event which is itself a platform to represent in GB events, gain National selection and achieve ever further.

Name: STC sniper training and consultancy
Description:
STC is a specialist provider of world-class sniper training and defence consultancy services. We deliver comprehensive capability development solutions, from the design and execution of foundation sniper courses through to advanced marksmanship, precision shooting, and counter-terrorism programmes.
Our instructor team is comprised of highly experienced professionals; all of whom have served within the Special Forces Support Group (SFSG). They bring extensive operational experience from global deployments and have a proven record of delivering specialist sniper training in demanding and complex environments worldwide.
Using the Defence Systems Approach to Training (DSAT), STC designs, develops, and delivers tailored training packages that meet specific client requirements. Our programmes ensure measurable capability enhancement, skills progression, and sustainable through-life training solutions.
In addition to defence and government training services, STC delivers civilian firearms training packages, ranging from basic weapons safety and firearms awareness to advanced shooting courses. Training can be conducted at our dedicated range facilities or at approved locations that best suit the client’s needs.
For further information or to discuss how STC can support your training, capability development, or consultancy requirements, please contact us.

Description: Employer Support of the Guard and Reserve (ESGR) is a Department of War office established in 1972 to promote cooperation and understanding between Reserve Component Service members and their civilian employers and to assist in the resolution of conflicts arising from military service. The Minnesota Committee helps service members and employers understand their rights and responsibilities in navigating the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA).

Name: The Royal Jordanian Command and Staff College
Description:
Since its inception in 1954 in Az Zarqa as the Staff School for Jordan Infantry, other military services and brethren and friendly countries, the Command and Staff College (CSC) has evolved to meet the educational and operational needs of the Jordan Army. It has generally been known as the staff school.
The College educates officers from military services and from many countries. Since that time the CSC is focused on the program of Leader Development and Education, along with a number of other schools and centers whose missions are centered on leader development and education
In 1973 the college was renamed as “The Royal Jordanian Command and Staff College”.
In 1986 the college was relocated to its current location (Amman) and upgraded to have 150 students. 25-30% of slots were designated for international students.
In 1996 RJCSC was integrated with the Royal Air Command and staff college.

Name: Cold Relation
Description:
Cold Relation is a next-generation Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT) and research platform built For Collectors, By Collectors. Designed for defense, law enforcement, and investigative professionals, Cold Relation provides safe, anonymized, and compliant access to the world’s most restricted digital ecosystems, including deep visibility through our flagship China Suite.

The Cold Relation platform enables secure, REAL TIME social media and web collection across open, deep, and region-locked networks. 

Our China Suite delivers authentic access to PRC platforms such as Weibo, QQ, CNKI, and Tieba, offering analysts unparalleled insight into Chinese social and information environments. This capability supports both operational and academic missions requiring language, context, and authenticity at scale.

We actively collaborate with analysts, technologists, and policy professionals to promote ethical and responsible OSINT practices, maintaining transparency, privacy, and tradecraft integrity in every build.

Cold Relation is trusted across sectors, from U.S. defense and law enforcement to academic research and private intelligence to power safe, accurate, and global open-source collection.

Cold Relation — Safe Access. Global Insight. For Collectors, By Collectors.

Founded by two Senior Information Systems Executives with each having over 20-year Navy experience, LPM was built to solve a critical problem: the fragmentation of veteran resources. We replace outdated checklists with a secure, centralized digital command center.

Description:

Name: Fayetteville Technical Community College Military/Veterans Programs (MVP)
Description:
Fayetteville Technical Community College (FTCC) is a regionally accredited two-year college with its main campus in downtown Fayetteville, NC. FTCC offers open admissions, associate degrees, diplomas, and certificate programs in over 280 academic, professional, and technical fields. FTCC’s mission is to serve our community as a learning-centered institution and build a globally competitive workforce that supports economic development. Programs are offered on Fort Liberty (LTEC), Main Campus, Spring Lake, Pope Airfield, Elgin Air Force Base, and your unit’s Area of Operations. Unit classes are available for all units; by coordinating with FTCC’s Fort Liberty office, Command lets us know the course they would like the unit to take, and we provide the instructor directly to the unit’s Area of Operation. In addition to unit classes, FTCC simplifies the transfer of credits and residency requirements for military members and offers credit for Military Training. Students can send us their Joint Service Transcript (JST), or stop by the office, to have their MOS evaluated for credit once they have completed one curriculum course.

FTCC provides complete student services at Fort Bragg to guide Military service members, veterans, and their dependents step by step through the application process, registration, and financial aid (FASFA) and assists with course selection. FTCC participates in the My Career Advancement Account (MyCAA) Scholarship. Through the MyCAA Scholarship program, an eligible military spouse can receive up to $4,000 of tuition assistance over two years. FTCC is equipped with a transition Tech Program for soldiers leaving the military to provide industry-focused certificates and credentials. FTCC’s All-American Veterans Center, located on the main campus, is a space to honor our veterans and to provide a location where veterans can gather, find assistance, and receive the support necessary to ensure their success at FTCC and beyond.

With a focus on precision manufacturing and a commitment to excellence, Glemco serves as an approved U.S. government contractor, delivering OEM and hard-to-find obsolete parts to clients worldwide.

Mission Statement
At Glemco Parts LLC, our mission is to enhance the safety and functionality of defense vehicles and equipment by delivering precision-engineered parts that meet the exacting needs of defense operations. We are committed to supporting American military readiness through exceptional service, competitive pricing, and a global network of suppliers and dealers.

Name: NexuDoc LLC
Description:
Nexudoc is a mobile app that was developed to help service members take control of their own military medical records, which in turn, helps set them up for successful compensation and pension claims to get the benefits and compensation they deserve for an injury, illness or exposure caused by service.
Nexudoc accomplishes the following:

·      Places service members in charge of their own military medical records

·      Allows for immediate documentation of injury and self-monitoring of health every 3 months

·      Supports successful outcomes in compensation and pension claims

·      Has the ability to export military medical records focal to compensation and pension directly to 3rd parties of Veteran’s choice

·      Keeps Veterans’ records private and under their control until they are ready to share them.
It has been far too long that our service members have struggled to get the benefits they deserve and often through no fault of their own. While they are busy serving our country, they should not have to worry that when they are ready to file for benefits, they will have to fight for them or possibly face years of appeals. They take care of us during their time enlisted. We need to take care of them when they separate. They deserve better than what they are getting.

We recognize the personal sacrifices made by both the service members and the families supporting them.  We want to be part of the solution in helping Veterans receive the benefits they rightfully deserve by supporting their claims with the use of the Nexudoc app.

Description:
The Association of the United States Army is a nonprofit educational and professional development association serving America’s Army and supporters of a strong national defense. AUSA provides a voice for the Army, supports the Soldier, and honors those who have served in order to advance the security of the nation.


OUR MISSION

AUSA supports soldiers, their families and Army civilians, provides a voice for the Army, and honors those who have served. As the Army’s premier, nonprofit, educational and professional association, we: 

Educate those we serve (soldiers and their families, Army civilians, retired soldiers and veterans, and the businesses and industries that support the Army) with programs, products, resources and events…

Inform our members, Congress and the American public about issues affecting America’s Army – Regular Army, Army National Guard and Army Reserve, and… Connect America’s Total Army, its industry partners, like-minded associations and other supporters at the national, regional and chapter levels.

Name: Defport BV
Description:
Met meer dan dertig jaar ervaring is Defport een vertrouwde partner in de defensiemarkt. Als technische handelsonderneming leveren wij hoogwaardige producten én slimme maatwerkoplossingen die volledig aansluiten op de eisen van onze klanten.

Wat begon als een specialistische technische dienst is inmiddels uitgegroeid tot een veelzijdige organisatie met diepgaande kennis van engineering, inkoop en projectondersteuning binnen de defensiesector. Door onze langdurige samenwerking met Defensie zijn wij goed bekend met de geldende inkoopprocedures, leveringsvereisten en bijbehorende administratieve processen.
Wij combineren praktijkervaring met innovatie en flexibiliteit – precies wat nodig is in een veeleisende omgeving.

Expertise
•	Meer dan 30 jaar ervaring in defensiegerichte techniek en engineering
•	Technische handelsonderneming gespecialiseerd in defensie-oplossingen
•	Leverancier van robuuste en gecertificeerde componenten en systemen
•	Ontwikkeling en optimalisatie van mechanische én elektronische oplossingen
•	Kennis van militaire normen, specificaties en documentatie-eisen
•	Inzicht in defensie-inkoop- en leveringsprocessen door jarenlange samenwerking

Name: Welcome Home Military Heroes
Description:
Welcome Home Military Heroes is a veteran-founded nonprofit organization based on California’s Central Coast, dedicated to honoring and supporting those who’ve served in the U.S. Armed Forces. Since our founding in 2007, we’ve grown into a volunteer-powered movement driven by patriotism, gratitude, and community action.

Our mission is simple: to ensure no military hero is ever forgotten.
Whether it’s a warm welcome home from deployment, a final salute at a veteran’s funeral, or delivering holiday support to struggling military families, our commitment is to be there — in moments that matter most.

We operate through nine year-round programs:

Operation Welcome Home

Operation Flag Line

Operation Veteran in Need

Operation Holiday Tree

Operation Last Salute

Operation Care Packages

Operation Education (Scholarships)

Operation American Flag (Flag Replacement)

Operation Veteran Vehicle Salutes

Every mission is fueled by volunteers, local businesses, and community members who believe in giving back to those who gave everything.

100% of donations stay local and directly support veterans, active-duty service members, and their families in Santa Barbara and San Luis Obispo Counties.

We proudly collaborate with other veteran nonprofits, military bases, and civic organizations to make a greater impact — together.

 We don’t just thank our heroes. We show up for them.

Name: Royal Lao Airborne
Description:
The Royal Lao Airborne is a Foreign Government in Exile allied to the USA.

The mission of the Royal Lao Airborne is:

* Preserve the fame and glory of the Royal Lao Airborne
* Provide training assistance  (Raiders – Jungle – Academy)
* Provide humanitarian aid to Lao refugees
* Assist FEMA in disaster-related activities

It is also charged to maintain and strengthen the bonds of comradeship, which distinguish the members of the Royal Lao Airborne, to continue to develop the bond between current and past Airborne forces of the world, and to provide for the gathering and dissemination of information concerning those members.

Royal Lao Airborne (RLA) has a long and amazing history going back over 60 years.
The Royal Lao Airborne in the United States was founded on September 3, 2001.
From its beginnings in the 1950s through today, the RLA has been, and will continue to be, a group of professionals who exhibit military bearing, hold true to the core values, and who put others before themselves.

Royal Lao Parachute Wings are also on the DOD list of approved foreign awards. (FM 600-8-22, Appendix D, dated 18 OCT 2016.)

Name: Norfolk Advisory
Description:
enquiries@norfolk.au | norfolk.au | +61 400 538 816

Norfolk is an Australian strategic research, analysis, and legal advisory firm. We work at the intersections of law, geopolitics, and security. We focus on sovereign resilience and governance in Australia and the near regions. Our services include assessments, strategy, and policy. We are an incorporated legal practice which provides advisory services in public and international law. We also provide services to government clients in the intellectual, informational, and legal warfare domains.

We provide our clients with different approaches, thinking, and perspectives. Our advisory skills come from strong intellectual foundations, enquiring minds, and broad knowledge and experience. Name: ECHO Intelligence
Description:
ECHO Intelligence is an independent collective of security and intelligence professionals focusing on intelligence and analysis to tackle contemporary threats to global security. Founded by 'Bishop,' a former public-sector intelligence professional, ECHO provides insightful perspectives on key issues like geopolitics, terrorism, domestic violent extremism, and great power competition.

With a strong commitment to public and national security, ECHO leverages open-source intelligence (OSINT) and innovative analytical methods to sift through misinformation, identify emerging threats, and deliver precise, impactful assessments.

In a world characterized by rapid change and geopolitical uncertainty, ECHO Intelligence is a valuable resource for professionals and curious individuals, offering clarity and actionable insights in an increasingly complex landscape.

The 30th Medical Brigade falls under the 21st Theater Sustainment Command, which is the U.S. Army Europe and Africa Sustainment Command.

Name: AFA - Los Angeles Schriever Chapter
Description:
The General Bernard A. Schriever Los Angeles Chapter 147 was officially chartered 
1) to create and sustain a broad acquaintance with the thinkers, planners, and doers of the aerospace fraternity,
2) to support local youth organizations (e.g., AFROTC and AFJROTC) and the community objectives of Space and Missile Systems Center personnel in the surrounding neighborhood, 
3) to participate in recognition of achievements of Space and Missile Systems personnel, and 
4) to join in convivial activities honoring this prestigious Air Force organization. 

These activities include Chapter’s 147 Executive Forum and Salute to Space and Missile Systems Center Awards Banquet, the Executive Reception held in conjunction with the Air Force Ball, and spring and fall Mixers.

The SCHRIEVER EDUCATION FOUNDATION was established on 9 March 1999.  The Board is comprised of five Directors, who also serve as the Officers of the General Bernard A. Schriever Los Angeles Chapter 147.  Because aerospace education is concerned with communicating knowledge, developing skills, and formulating attitudes about America’s aerospace initiatives and the impact of air and space technologies and systems on American society, the Schriever Education Foundation is dedicated to educating the nation about significant aerospace activities.

Name: Western New York Council - Navy League of the United States
Description:
The Navy League of the United States, founded in 1902 with the support of President Theodore Roosevelt, is a nonprofit civilian, educational and advocacy organization that supports America’s sea services: the Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard and U.S.-flag Merchant Marine.

For over a century, the Navy League has been a voice for maritime strength, engaging the public and Congress on the vital role these services play in national security, economic prosperity, and global stability. Through membership, we unite individuals passionate about maritime heritage, national defense, and community support, fostering a network that uplifts those who serve.

Through membership, the Navy League unites individuals passionate about maritime heritage and service, creating a vibrant network that uplifts those who serve and promotes maritime excellence.

Name: NVM Consulting
Description:
NVM Consulting helps governments, international organizations, and private sector leaders navigate volatile environments through strategic advisory, risk and threat assessment, and research-driven policy evaluation. We work across Sub-Saharan Africa, Eastern Europe, and South Asia, bringing field-tested insight that connects analysis to execution.

Our practice tracks how the security landscape is changing—especially as emerging technologies reshape it. We assess how state and non-state actors apply AI across operations and influence campaigns, and what that means for asymmetric tactics, governance, and resilience. These insights feed directly into risk frameworks, program design, and decision support for clients facing complex geopolitical pressures.

Over the past eight years, we’ve advised donor governments including the EU, UK, US, and the Kingdom of the Netherlands, helping teams anticipate threats, allocate resources, and deliver measurable impact in conflict-affected settings.

1. Close Protection: Our specialized team conducts thorough threat assessments and develops customized risk mitigation strategies to protect high-profile individuals and assets.

3. Resource Sharing and Training: Our collaboration with DND provides access to cutting-edge technologies and specialized training that exceed civilian standards, equipping our team with the skills necessary to manage complex security scenarios.

QUI ADHÈRE À LA SMG ?
Les officiers n’étant pas encore membre sont ceux issus de nouvelles promotions. Il s’agit pour nous de les convaincre de la nécessité de se perfectionner, de cultiver la camaraderie interarmes, de regrouper tous ceux qui tiennent à un politique de sécurité crédible.

Pour ceux qui au fil des ans ne nous ont pas rejoint, il nous appartient de faire appel à leur sens de la solidarité. Name: Building Healthy Military Communities
Description:
Building Healthy Military Communities (BHMC) is a strategic pilot that is aligned with the larger Department of Defense (DoD) initiative, Total Force Fitness (TFF) to enhance recruitment, retention, readiness, and resiliency of the Total Force. TFF views health, wellness, and resilience as a holistic concept where optimal performance requires a connection among mind, body, spirit and family, and social relationships.

BHMC’s objective is to coordinate and integrate existing DoD, federal, state, regional, tribal, and local efforts in support of TFF across all branches in Active Duty and Total Reserve Components who are living in geographically dispersed areas.

BHMC initiatives enhance protective factors ahead of the point of crisis across eight (8) domains – Physical, Environmental, Medical/Dental, Nutritional, Spiritual, Psychological, Financial, and Social – which comprise the TFF framework. 

Each state is led by a State Coordinator (SC) who serves as a central hub to facilitate connections and awareness in their state between: 
- Collective DoD community 
- Non-governmental organizations
- Community partners 
- National, state, local, and tribal governments

Name: The Strategic Veteran
Description:
The Strategic Veteran is more than a podcast—it's a movement dedicated to transforming how veterans navigate their transition from military to civilian life. Founded by Adam Peters, a veteran who intimately understands the challenges of military transition, TSV has evolved into a multi-faceted platform that bridges the gap between military service and civilian success.

At our core, we share raw, unfiltered stories of veteran transitions—the victories, the struggles, and everything in between. Our flagship podcast features authentic conversations with veterans who've walked the path, offering real insights and actionable strategies for those facing their own transitions. We don't just scratch the surface; we dive deep into the emotional, professional, and personal aspects of leaving military service.

Through our podcast & live events, we create spaces where veterans can connect, share experiences, and build meaningful relationships. These events go beyond networking—they're about creating genuine connections and fostering a community of support and understanding.

What sets us apart is our commitment to authenticity. We reject the 'guru culture' and instead focus on real stories, real challenges, and real solutions. Our content strategy spans across platforms, with our LinkedIn presence serving as a daily source of inspiration and practical insights for transitioning service members.

Our mission extends beyond individual success stories. We're advocating for systemic change at the DoD level, working to improve transition programs and support systems for future veterans. Through our platform, we're building a bridge between military experience and civilian opportunity, one story at a time.

Whether through our podcast episodes, live events, social media content, or community initiatives, The Strategic Veteran is committed to one goal: ensuring no veteran faces transition alone. We believe in the power of shared experiences, authentic storytelling, and community

Name: USS Enterprise (CVN-65)
Description: USS Enterprise (CVN-65), formerly CVA(N)-65, is a decommissioned United States Navy aircraft carrier. In 1958, she was the first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier and the eighth United States naval vessel to bear the name. Like her predecessor of World War II fame, she is nicknamed "Big E". At 1,123 feet (342 m), she is the longest naval vessel ever built and the only ship of a class that was originally planned to have five other ships. Her 93,284-long-ton (94,781 t) displacement ranks her class as the third largest carrier class, after the Nimitz class and the Gerald R. Ford class. Enterprise had a crew of some 4,600 service members.

Our patented formula—T = α(SES) + β(PI) + γ(IE) + δ(SD) + ε(LE) + ζ(PSF) + η(EI)—integrates socio-economic, political, ideological, and psychological variables to assess terrorism risk with unmatched precision. Jonathan Denning, our Chief Technology Officer, leads our innovation arm with unmatched expertise in restoration security, a domain focused on reestablishing critical infrastructure, digital resilience, and socio-technical harmony after terrorist disruption. Jonathan's strategic coding, adaptive algorithms, and ethical digital architecture empower national and regional recovery with technological foresight.

Our mission? To safeguard global communities through transparency, compliance, and collaboration. Our work is grounded in academic rigor, humanitarian principles, and international legal standards.

Our vision? A world where counter-terrorism is intelligent, preventative, and human-centered.

Name: US Navy Fighter Weapons School (TOPGUN)
Description:
In 1968, Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Admiral Thomas Hinman Moorer ordered Captain Frank Ault to research the failings of the U.S. air-to-air missiles used in combat in the skies over North Vietnam. The Navy’s "Ault Report" concluded that the problem stemmed from inadequate aircrew training in air combat maneuvering (ACM). Among its wide-ranging recommendations to improve air combat performance, the Ault Report recommended that an "Advanced Fighter Weapons School" be established at Naval Air Station Miramar to revive and disseminate community fighter expertise throughout the fleet. CNO Moorer concurred.
The United States Navy Fighter Weapons School was established on 3 March 1969, at Naval Air Station Miramar, California. Today, the Navy Weapons School is headquartered at NAS Fallon, Nevada under the umbrella of the Naval Aviation Warfighting Development Center (NAWDC).

Name: theNavyCWO, LLC
Description:
TheNavyCWO.com: Honoring the Legacy, Empowering the Future 

Welcome aboard! TheNavyCWO.com is your go-to resource dedicated to celebrating the rich history and tradition of U.S. Navy Chief Warrant Officers (CWOs). Our mission is threefold:

1 Preserve History: We’re committed to safeguarding the legacy of CWOs and their invaluable contributions to the Navy.
2 Educate & Prepare: Offering resources, guidance, and tips to help aspiring CWOs and Limited Duty Officers (LDOs) navigate their journey.
3 Spread Awareness: Building a community that appreciates the dedication and expertise of Navy leaders, past and present.

 What We Do:
From in-depth articles and historical insights to a growing online store featuring Navy-themed swag, we aim to keep the CWO spirit alive. Our store supports the site’s operations while spreading pride in our unique heritage with slogans like "SINCE 1775," "SINCE 1893," and "SINCE 1948."

 Why We Exist:
We’re not here to get rich—just to keep the site afloat and share the passion for all things nautical and Navy. So whether you're an aspiring officer, a seasoned veteran, or just a fan of maritime history, there’s something here for you.

 Join us in preserving the traditions, humor, and excellence that make the Navy—and its Chief Warrant Officers—truly legendary.

 Visit us at TheNavyCWO.com and let’s sail together toward a better understanding of Navy leadership!

Description:

And now more than ever we need to strengthen in-person peer support and encourage positive physical and mental well-being within our community.

To mark the 2022 Queen's Platinum Jubilee, our community will launch the Walk + Talk initiative, making it simple and easy to connect, with the target of connecting 1000 Household Cavalry members and veterans this year.

Find out about walks in your area or sign up to lead a walk here: https://www.militaryapp.org/pages/household-cavalry-walk-and-talk

Peer support and engagement is one of the best ways to strengthen a community and build a proactive approach to supporting positive mental and physical health. And what better way to do this than getting outside in the fresh air with one or two Household Cavalry men, women and families.

Description:
Campania SHE FOR ROMANIA are ca scop creșterea reprezentării și participării femeilor în sistemul național de apărare, ordine publică și siguranță națională, dar și încurajarea acestora să ocupe poziții de lideri în organizațiile internaționale precum ONU, NATO și UE, în negocierile de pace și misiunile de menținere a păcii.

Destinată femeilor aflate la începutul carierei, campania oferă seminare, workshopuri și sesiuni de mentorat pentru dezvoltarea abilităților, schimbul de bune practici și crearea de rețele profesionale. Scopul este de a alinia traseele profesionale cu oportunitățile în domeniul securității internaționale, în vederea formării unei noi generații de femei lider. 

Coordonarea campaniei este în sarcina Ministerului Apărării Naționale care, prin Punctul focal național, va colabora cu reprezentanții sistemului național de apărare, ordine publică și siguranță națională, ai mediului academic, ai organizațiilor naționale și internaționale, mediului privat și ai societății civile, conform ,,Strategiei naționale privind implementarea Rezoluției 1325 (2000) a Consiliului de Securitate al Organizației Națiunilor Unite (RCSONU) - Femeile, Pacea și Securitatea pentru perioada 2024 – 2028”, aprobată prin HG 896/2024.

Pentru a atinge obiectivele campaniei, invităm societatea civilă, mediul academic și privat să ne devină parteneri în organizarea și desfășurarea de acțiuni, contribuind astfel la promovarea Agendei Femeile, Pace și Securitate în România.


Campania se adresează personalului din sistemul național de apărare, ordine publică și siguranță națională, dar poate fi extinsă la nivel național/internațional, în regim de cooperare interorganizațională, sub rezerva respectării legislației incidente protecției drepturilor de autor.

Name: Sjømilitære Samfund
Description:
SMS arbeider for et godt og troverdig Sjøforsvar. SMS engasjerer seg i forhold som berører Sjøforsvarets tarv og utvikling. SMS søker å engasjere seg i prosessen slik at fattede beslutninger kan støttes. Norsk Tidsskrift for Sjøvesen (NTfS) står sentralt i SMS’s arbeid. Vi ønsker å engasjere medlemmer gjennom tidsskriftet og de forskjellige arrangementer SMS står bak. SMS ønsker alt tilsatt personell i Sjøforsvaret som medlemmer. Likeledes ønskes som medlemmer alle som har interesse for kyst- og sjøfartsnasjonen Norge og Sjøforsvaret. SMS ønsker både medlemmer som er aktive, og medlemmer som vil bidra til organisasjonenes arbeid gjennom sitt medlemskap. SMS er en ideell organisasjon hvor de aktivt engasjerte virker på frivillig basis. Dette tilsier et avpasset engasjement og en prioritering av aktiviteter.

Vårt formål
SMS formål er å virke for Sjøforsvarets tarv og utvikling, å fremme medlemmenes interesse for militære, militærvitenskapelige og militærtekniske emner, samt å fremme kameratskap og korpsånd blant dem. Foreningen utøver dette ved å samarbeide med andre organer og ved å samle medlemmene til foredrag, diskusjoner og selskapelig samvær.

Dette forutsetter et utadvendt engasjement i det norske samfunnet om maritime forhold og aktiviteter, som synliggjør og skaper forståelse for Sjøforsvarets betydning og rolle i samfunnet generelt.

Vår visjon
SMS skal aktivt bidra til Sjøforsvarets synlige kvalitet og anerkjennelse som forsvarsgren.

SMS skal gjennom aktivt virke engasjere, først og fremst innad i egne rekker. SMS ønsker å bidra til at offiserene fortsatt skal identifisere seg med sitt yrke og holdes styrket i troen på at hans og hennes tjeneste som offiser er samfunnsgagnlig og fredsbevarende. I en tid med omstillinger er det viktig med et engasjert og dedikert offiserskorps. SMS skal arbeide for en fortsatt forståelse hos det norske folk om at: “en suveren stat har en rett og en plikt til å ha et forsvar”, og i særdeleshet betydningen av å ha et sjøfors

Name: AUKUS Clearance
Description:
AUKUS Clearance is the emerging benchmark for trusted collaboration across the industrial, technological, and defence ecosystems of Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

As the AUKUS nations accelerate joint production, shared standards, and integrated supply chains, industry requires a new form of credential — one that recognises not only security and integrity, but also technical competence, digital engineering capability, and alliance-ready mobility.

AUKUS Clearance fills that gap.

It is a forward-leaning framework designed to support companies, engineers, welders, apprentices, designers, researchers, and innovators who are contributing to the alliance’s most advanced programs — from nuclear-powered submarines to hypersonics, cyber, quantum, space, and autonomous systems.

What AUKUS Clearance Represents

Trust Across Borders

A shared standard of reliability, professionalism, and integrity recognised by partners across all three nations.

Industrial Readiness

Demonstrated capability to operate within secure, digitally-enabled supply chains — from additive manufacturing and high-precision machining to systems integration and advanced materials.

Workforce Mobility

A complementary layer to emerging concepts like AUKUS Visa and AUKUS Passport, enabling smoother movement of talent, expertise, and collaboration across the alliance.

Digital Engineering Alignment

Participation in a common digital ecosystem — including secure data environments, model-based engineering, and verified cyber-readiness.

A Whole-of-Nation Effort

AUKUS Clearance supports the broader mobilisation of industry, academia, and communities across the three member nations — the welders, coders, fabricators, technicians, and researchers who are building the backbone of AUKUS.

Trusted people, trusted companies, and trusted systems working seamlessly across borders.

AUKUS Clearance signals readiness to be part of the mission.

Name: ACORAM
Description:
Créée en 1925, l’ACORAM est la plus importante des  associations d’officiers de réserve de la Marine nationale.

C’est un réseau fort de plusieurs milliers d’adhérents de tous corps répartis en 25 sections régionales en métropole, en outre-mer et à l’international qui partagent les mêmes valeurs, celles de la mer et celles de l’officier.

Les membres de l’ACORAM sont des ambassadeurs de la Marine nationale et l’association contribue à la formation et à l’information des officiers de réserve de la Marine.

Les jeunes, regroupés au sein du groupe jeunes, sont particulièrement actifs au sein de notre organisation.

La contribution à la formation dispensée par l’ACORAM passe par l’organisation de stages embarqués, d’activités sportives, de séminaires et de colloques.

Elle édite la revue Marine ACORAM destinée à ses adhérents et à des personnalités du monde maritime et attribue annuellement son prix littéraire Marine Bravo Zulu.

Présentes sur l’ensemble du territoire, les sections organisent de multiples événements : conférences, visites, participations à des cérémonies patriotiques et réunions de section.

Par ses actions, l’ACORAM participe au développement de l’esprit de défense et à la prise de conscience des intérêts maritimes tout en conservant l’esprit d’équipage entre ses membres.

Marine oblige !

Name: AUKUS Visa
Description:
An AUKUS Forum initiative.

The AUKUS Visa initiative is a strategic program designed to support the seamless mobility of skilled professionals across Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States, ensuring the success of critical AUKUS partnership projects. This initiative will address workforce challenges by facilitating cross-border collaboration in defense, industry, and academia, driving innovation and delivering on the ambitious goals of the trilateral alliance.

Key Objectives:

 Enable the movement of highly skilled workers to support defense and industrial projects, such as the nuclear submarine program.

 Bridge workforce gaps by pooling expertise across AUKUS nations, fostering collaboration rather than competition.

 Strengthen ties between industries and governments, creating streamlined pathways for talent exchange.

Aligned with the AUKUS Passport, the AUKUS Visa will empower professionals to work seamlessly across borders, advancing innovation in fields like AI, quantum computing, undersea capabilities, and advanced manufacturing.

The AUKUS Visa Initiative is part of building a unified and agile workforce capable of meeting the complex demands of modern security and economic development.

For inquiries and partnerships, contact: Cathy Krimmer, AUKUS Forum Operations Director cathy@aukusforum.com

Regimental headquarters, including logistics and support elements, are garrisoned at Ohrdruf, Thuringia. Forward-deployed homeland defense companies are strategically positioned in North, Central, and South Hessen to ensure rapid regional responsiveness. Name: Alaska Defense
Description:
Alaska Defense is a vertically integrated defense solutions provider delivering advanced military systems engineered for expeditionary, contingency, and mission-critical operations worldwide.

With more than 50 years of proven experience, Alaska Defense designs, tests, and manufactures critical military infrastructure entirely in-house. This vertically integrated approach enables faster delivery, greater quality control, and reduced program risk—while eliminating delays associated with fragmented supply chains or third-party system integration.

Alaska Defense is globally recognized as the world’s fastest and largest supplier of military shelter systems and turnkey base camps, supporting defense, security, and humanitarian missions in austere, remote environments. Beyond individual products, the company delivers complete, interoperable solutions that integrate shelters, containerized facilities, power generation, environmental control units (ECUs), and mission-support systems into cohesive operational architectures.

Alaska Defense solutions are engineered to scale—from standalone systems to fully integrated deployments—supporting modern operational and command-and-control (C2) environments across land, air, sea, space, and cyber domains. Every system is designed for rapid deployment, sustained operations, and performance in extreme conditions.

From expeditionary missions to long-term operations, Alaska Defense delivers field-tested, combat-proven solutions built for speed, reliability, and operational certainty—wherever the mission demands.

Alaska Defense is a corporate division of AKS Industries, Inc.
