Name: The Global Gambit
Description:
The Global Gambit (GG) is the preeminent independent source for conversations on geopolitics, foreign policy, macroeconomics and current affairs, involving live, interactive audience participation. 

 Each month, GG brings you content covering the entirety of the globe on regional and thematic issues, especially interviews and panels, but also ranging from explainers to personal takes. GG brings together top-tier guests consisting of policymakers, journalists, academics and independent content creators to make sense of news and open usually complex, technical topics to new, non-specialised audiences.

 Join in with weekly live-audio conversations on Twitter Spaces or video content via YouTube interviews and Explainer Shorts also on TikTok, Instagram and more! Read my writing musings on Substack too!

 My goal is to bring clarity in a time of great uncertainty and distrust by making events more accessible to those with a passion or curiosity, in enlightening yet entertaining ways. And so, if that resonates, l invite you to support me where you can, be it by subscribing on YouTube or the podcast, and even chipping in to my Patreon or Buy Me Coffee, allowing me to improve and streamline content creation. 

This is the Global Gambit.

Name: Blue Star Families of New England
Description:
The families of our all-volunteer military make unprecedented sacrifices these days to serve our country. Blue Star Families was founded by military spouses in 2009 to empower these families to thrive as they serve. We're committed to strengthening military families by connecting them with their neighbors – individuals and organizations – to create vibrant communities of mutual support. We believe we're all stronger when we take care of one another.

Our groundbreaking research is raising the nation's awareness of the unique challenges of military family life. With the help of neighbors across the country, we are overcoming the isolation and alienation of frequent moves, deployments, and reduced support from the government. Our innovative programs are solving specific challenges for military families, including fighting economic insecurity with resources that foster spouse career development, creating family strength with rich family programming, and providing critical peer support for caregivers, whose numbers are only increasing.

With more than 250,000+ members in our network, including in chapters and communities all over the world, we have touched more than 2 million military families. Through our research and program partnerships, we ensure that wherever American military families go, they can always feel connected, supported and empowered to thrive – in every community, across the nation, and around the globe.

Name: Palisades Group
Description: We are a US Military, Law Enforcement, and Tactical Specialties supplier headquartered in Cincinnati, OH. We provide mission critical gear and equipment to those in uniform. We outfit and kit the US warfighter, LEO, and private security contractor so they can excel in their operations. We provide cutting-edge and next-generational equipment which allows our customers to defeat the emerging threats of tomorrow-today. We standby, day and night to support your operations. We are just a call away from providing mission success and an unmatched advantage. Secure the outcome of all operations through superior tactical solutions.

Name: Naval Station Mayport
Description:
(Follows/likes/shares/links ≠ endorsement) 
Naval Station Mayport is located at the mouth of the St. Johns River, and occupies over 3,400 acres.  Since its commissioning in December 1942, Naval Station Mayport has grown to become the third largest Fleet Concentration Area (FCA) in the United States. Its operational composition is unique, with a busy harbor capable of accommodating up to 34 ships and an 8,000-foot runway capable of handling any aircraft in the Department of War inventory.  With more than 3,400 acres, the base is home to Commander, U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command and U.S. Fourth Fleet; Commander, Naval Surface Squadron Fourteen; Destroyer Squadron Four Zero; Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) Squadron Two; Helicopter Maritime Strike Wing, U.S. Atlantic Fleet; and more than 10,000 Sailors and civilian DoD employees.  Mayport is also home to Unmanned Patrol Squadron (VUP) 19, which operates the Navy’s first MQ-4C Triton Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS). Currently, NAVSTA Mayport is home to over 20 warships, 1 Coast Guard cutter, 3 helicopter squadrons and more than 80 tenant commands.

Name: Poplicus
Description: The national security community and every branch of the military rely on Poplicus Inc. d/b/a Govini to accelerate Acquisition decisions. Govini transforms Defense Acquisition from an outdated manual process to a software-driven strategic advantage for the United States. Govini's flagship product, Ark, supports teams with AI-enabled Applications and best-in-class data to more rapidly imagine, develop, and field the capabilities we need.

Name: EDF PROTEAS
Description:
Joint Operations that involve Special Operations Forces (SOF) are rapidly becoming a very important instrument for MoDs worldwide and across Europe. It is evident that symmetric and asymmetric threats inside and outside the EU territory require fast response and the ability to rapidly deploy transportable units implementing a Special Operations Forces - Command Post (SOFCP) to areas of interest, during both peace and wartime. The PROTEAS project aims at providing a prototyped deployable SOFCPC2.

The proposed project will cover all necessary study, design, system prototyping, testing and validation actions required to engineer a state-of-the-art prototyped SOFCPC2 to support SOF operations in SJO (Small Joint Operations) missions. To the best of our knowledge, this will be the first European effort to develop a SOFCPC2, making the PROTEAS envisaged impact of paramount importance both at national and EU level.

The ultimate goal of PROTEAS project is to create the THE FIRST OF ITS KIND Special Operations Forces Command Post for Command and Control (SOFCPC2) to support EU-led SOF Small Joint Operations (SJO) and in compliance with European Union (EU) standards and with the EU-Members and Norway specific operational needs.

Name: Warfighting System Solutions
Description: Warfighting System Solutions empowers military and civilian agencies through world-class training, leader development, and operational support. Our adaptive and innovative solutions help leaders build warfighting readiness and sustain deliberate transformation for large-scale combat operations.

Name: Fletcher Security Review
Description: Fletcher Security Review (FSR) is a print and online journal managed and edited by students at The Fletcher School at Tufts University. Drawing on the school’s interdisciplinary academic approach, FSR engages established and emerging scholars, practitioners, and analysts across a range of security issues. For our contributors, FSR works to provide a forum through which to advance ideas and unique perspectives. This is sought in service of our dynamic audience, whose interests span traditional and nontraditional security topics. Ultimately, as a student-run publication, each edition of FSR is guided by the passions and perspectives of its editorial staff.

Name: Canadian Military Engineers Association
Description:
Version française ci-dessous

The CMEA is a dynamic association open to all members of the Canadian Military Engineer family.  Since its formation in 2000, it has grown to over 2500 members with more than 21 Chapters and six affiliated Reserve units spread across the country. The Association continues to welcome new members from the rank of Sapper to Lieutenant General. The CME family includes all who currently serve with or support the Canadian Military Engineers, or have done so in the past. These pages present our objectives and accomplishments, our stories and your history, news and events and items of interest.

--------------------------------------

L'ACME est une association dynamique ouverte à tous les membres de la famille des ingénieurs militaires canadiens. Depuis sa création en 2000, elle compte aujourd'hui plus de 2 500 membres répartis dans plus de 21 sections et six unités de réserve affiliées à travers le pays. L'association continue d'accueillir de nouveaux membres, du grade de sapeur à celui de lieutenant-général. La famille du génie militaire canadien comprend tous ceux qui servent ou soutiennent actuellement le génie militaire canadien, ou qui l'ont fait dans le passé. Ces pages présentent nos objectifs et nos réalisations, nos récits et votre histoire, les actualités et les événements, ainsi que des sujets d'intérêt.

Name: GALION GROUP
Description:
Galion Group helps companies and nations create or strengthen their industrial capabilities in the defense and security sector. 
In a fast-changing geopolitical and technological environment, we provide fresh, external perspectives to unlock maximum value.
We believe a strong defense and security system is the true cornerstone of freedom.

Name: Swatter Company
Description: Swatter is a Portuguese high-technology SME in the defense and security sector, focused on developing advanced solutions to address unmanned aerial threats. Founded in 2021, the company combines strong engineering capabilities with an operational mindset, delivering reliable technology for demanding real-world environments.

Name: Daniele Garofalo Monitoring
Description:
Daniele Garofalo Monitoring is an independent intelligence and threat-monitoring platform focused on Salafi-jihadist organisations, jihadist and Islamist insurgencies, and their operational, narrative, and cognitive dimensions.

 Daniele Garofalo Monitoring - All rights reserved.

Name: Grand Forks Air Force Base
Description:
Local Airmen, Global Impact. The 2,200 Total Force Airmen assigned to the 319th Reconnaissance Wing provide rapid combat support through mission support functions, mobility preparedness and continuous security to Grand Forks AFB, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Grand Forks International Airport and Cavalier Space Force Station located in Cavalier, North Dakota.  

The 319th RW provides a decisional advantage to our warfighters and national leaders through support of our nation's RQ-4 Global Hawk Block 40 high altitude Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR) mission. Ensures strategic command and control through the operation of the nation's High Frequency Global Communication System.  Affords Combatant Commanders mission-ready Airmen anytime, anywhere. Provides airmen and families of the 319th RW team, to include geographically separated units and mission partners, with responsive, tailored and mission-focused support.

Name: ADMM Cybersecurity and Information Centre of Excellence
Fight Club Deutschland (FCDE)
Description:
Fight Club Germany is part of the global network “Fight Club International” and connects wargamers from various professional areas as well as interested individuals from German-speaking countries. Our focus is on wargaming, crisis simulation, serious games and game-based learning.

We are committed to connecting people and existing initiatives, promoting wargaming as a method and supporting the next generation of wargamers in Germany.

As part of Fight Club International, Fight Club Germany is primarily aimed at German-speaking members. In the spirit of networking, all Fight Club members are welcome. The network language is (predominantly) German.

Fight Club Deutschland aims at: 
1. Establish wargaming as a critical training method, analytical tool and for raising awareness in Germany.
2. Bring local initiatives and practitioners together and draw on the diversity of perspectives and experiences.
3. Strengthen national and international networking and cooperation, promote underrepresented groups and empower the next generation of wargamers.
4. In this way, contributing to overcoming current and future polycrises, increasing overall societal resilience and defense capabilities.

Our Discord server is the heart of our activities. This is where members communicate, resources are made available, lectures are held, joint projects are organized and implemented, tournaments are held and topics are promoted.

Fight Club Germany is committed to the free and democratic basic order and stands for a pluralistic society. As a signatory of the Derby House Principles for Diversity in Wargaming, we promote equality and take a firm stance against any form of discrimination. We encourage and support the participation of underrepresented groups.

Name: Marine Reserve Leadership Collective
Description:
The Reserve Leadership Collective promotes retention, education, advocacy, and unity of effort within the Reserve Officer community. Service and leadership in the Reserve Component is demanding and successful career development and longevity requires unique skills. Our goal is to ensure restricted and unrestricted Reserve Officers have a community that promotes professional development, provides mentorship opportunities, challenges paradigms, and encourages continued service in support of our nation’s security and prosperity.

Name: Ateneo de Seguridad Internacional de Comillas
Description: El ASIC es una organización de alumnos de la Universidad Pontificia Comillas que tiene por objetivos fomentar, ampliar y apoyar la formación de los alumnos de la Universidad en materia de seguridad y defensa; promover y fomentar la cultura de la seguridad y defensa entre los alumnos universitarios; establecer y fomentar relaciones académicas y profesionales entre el ASIC, la Universidad y aquellos actores y sujetos involucrados en el ámbito de la seguridad y defensa, tanto en España como a nivel internacional, así como difundir recursos y/o contenidos relevantes con el objetivo de perseguir un crecimiento profesional y personal íntegro que introduzca a los alumnos en el área de la seguridad y defensa. Estos objetivos se logran a través de conferencias, actividades de carácter académico, visitas y proyectos de investigación.

Name: ICOA: INTERNATIONAL CENTER FOR OPERATIONAL ASSISTANCE
Description:
We are a specialized security and defense training organization providing mission-focused operational instruction for private military contractors, protective teams, and corporate security units operating in high-risk and complex environments. Our training is built on real operational experience, modern doctrine, and standards used by elite international forces.

Core Competencies:
We deliver advanced programs in PMC deployment readiness, Executive Protection (CPP/CPO), PSD mission execution, CQB & VCQB engagements, Psychological Operations (PSYOPS), armored vehicles operations (commercial & military), threat and risk-area assessments, mission planning, and high-level Access Management & Security Protocols.

Specialized Capabilities:
Our portfolio includes High-Risk Environment Training (HRE), tactical and evasive driving, counter-surveillance and anti-surveillance operations, convoy and escort tactics, static/mobile security operations, HEAT certification, Tactical First Aid / TCCC, Maritime Security Operations (MSO), UAV reconnaissance and drone integration, crisis response & evacuation procedures, as well as operational intelligence fundamentals (HUMINT/SIGINT basics).

We support organizations, teams, and individuals who require a high degree of operational resilience, adaptability, and mission efficiency. Each training program is designed to match real-world challenges faced in modern conflict zones, corporate threat landscapes, and critical infrastructure environments.

Our mission is simple: to elevate your operational capability, enhance decision-making under pressure, and prepare your personnel for the realities of today’s security and defense operations.

Name: Arkansas National Guard Foundation
Description:
The Mission of the Arkansas National Guard Foundation is to provide charitable and educational support to members of the Arkansas National Guard, their dependents and survivors, veterans, and to other charitable organizations that support veteran communities in Arkansas. 

The Arkansas National Guard Foundation was created in 2014 as the successor to the National Guard Association of Arkansas Insurance Trust. The National Guard Association of Arkansas (NGAA) was created in 1927 to advocate for support of the National Guard in Arkansas. In the 1960s, NGAA began providing term life insurance to its members. In 1967 the Trust was created to enable veteran-focused charitable giving from the proceeds of the insurance program. Since its creation, the Foundation and its predecessors have provided over $1.2 million in scholarships to Guard members, their dependents, and their survivors. The creation of the Foundation enabled the consolidation of several existing charitable organizations and programs. 

Today, the ANGF’s mission continues to have a direct impact on the lives of Guard members, their families, dependents, and survivors in all military branches. There are six specific program areas with sustained project initiatives focused on needs of contemporary Arkansas Guard members that support the ANGF’s mission: 1) Survivor Services Support, 2) Education, Scholarships & Professional Development 3) Medical and Dental Readiness of our Force, 4) Military Family Emergency Relief, 5) Youth Engagement and Recruitment and 6) Legacy Preservation.

These six areas, while broad in their scope and impact, all have one purpose in mind—providing support for Guardsmen & women and their families’ well-being. Whether it is a college scholarship for a dependent or emergency relief assistance for those struggling economically, ANGF's mission and aim is to support the needs of Guardsmen, their families, and Survivors.

Name: Project Volcan
Description: Project Volcan was created in 2019 to serve the needs of construction businesses, environment improvement professionals, and Government development projects. Our management has more than 10 years of APAC direct experience in the Region, having served most of the most influential and important MNCs across the Asia Pacific. Since then, our client base has grown to include large multinational corporations, port authorities, rental companies, agricultural organizations, and military and airport service organizations.

Name: Tampa Recruiting Battalion (US Army)
Description:
The Tampa Recruiting Battalion, nicknamed the "Gators", consists of the battalion headquarters and six company headquarters. Our Recruiters are responsible for enlisting young men and women into both the active and reserve components of the U.S. Army.

Gator Battalion recruiters cover the majority of West and Central Florida, from St Petersburg in the West to Daytona in the East, from Inverness in the North to Naples in the South. The Battalion Headquarters is located in Tampa and the Company Headquarters are located in Daytona, Sarasota, Orlando, Tampa, Lakeland and Saint Petersburg.

The Tampa Recruiting Battalion is a part of the 2nd Recruiting Brigade.

Name: Homecoming 250 Navy Marine Corps
Description:
As America prepares for the Semiquincentennial of the Declaration of Independence in 2026, Homecoming 250 Navy Marine Corps believes we should also celebrate the 250th birthdays of the military services that gained and defend our independence. The Navy and Marine Corps were founded on October 13 and November 10, 1775, in Philadelphia, and launched their first missions on the Delaware River. Homecoming 250 is a 501(c)(3) charity created to bring the celebration of the Navy and Marines’ 250th birthdays to their birthplace.
As a result of Homecoming 250’s efforts, the Secretary of the Navy has announced that the Navy and Marine Corps will celebrate their 250th birthdays in Philadelphia and Camden in 2025. The Navy and the Marines held a preparatory Navy and Marine Week in October 2023, highlighted by Homecoming 250’s Navy & Marine Corps Gala at which the Secretary and other military leaders spoke. www.homecoming250.org/secretary-of-the- navys-announcement/. Numerous national and area leaders, organizations, corporations, countless veterans, and other Americans are supporting our efforts. www.homecoming250.org/our-supporting-organizations/.
Homecoming 250 is proud to host the Navy and Marine Corps 250th Celebration in Fall 2025. It will be the grand opening of the Semiquincentennial, and likely its largest event honoring our military and veterans.

Name: Balkans 35th
Description:
Balkans 35th Commemoration is a volunteer Veterans led initiative.  Our aim is to acknowledge Balkans Veterans from all elements of the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF),  the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP), other Canadian Police forces, Canadian Border Services Agency (CBSA) and Canadian International Development Agency (CIDA) who deployed to the Balkans from 1992 to 2004.
 
       	Our mission is to commemorate the 35th Anniversary of Canada’s contribution to peacekeeping and stability operations in the Balkans.
 
     	It has been 33 years since Canadian Peacekeepers commenced the first major deployments to the Balkans in 1992.  Over 40,000 Canadians from all elements of the Canadian Armed Forces and other agencies participated in multiple missions in the Balkans, each with different Rules of Engagement, Objectives and circumstances.  Canadians were regarded as one of the most effective forces within the UN and NATO deployments.   2027 will be the 35th anniversary of the start of these deployments

       	Our intent is to facilitate multiple small events across Canada in 2026 and major events in 2027.  We will determine the interest in a Return to the Balkans between June to September 2027.  This will be for Veterans and their families only . 

       	We want to acknowledge Balkan veterans and their families and celebrate their achievements and sacrifices. We intend to educate Canadians on the challenges Balkans veterans faced and their experiences after returning to Canada without the benefit of the Mental Health and Suicide Awareness support that has been provided to more recent veterans.  

       	There were over 60 deployments to the Balkans Our largest project will be interviewing 1,500 veterans and documenting their experiences for the Oral History Project.  We are planning documentaries on specific actions in the Balkans.  We also plan to establish a National Balkans Repository that can be accessed by historians and researchers.

Name: CIOR Language Academy
Description:
The primary mission of the Language Academy is to teach English and French as foreign languages (EFL/FFL), by means of reservists instructing reservists of PfP, neutral European, MD countries and NATO nations. The Academy offers intensive courses teaching EFL and FFL at 3 proficiency levels. Courses are designed to develop listening comprehension and pronunciation skills. Conversation practice uses topical discussions in a military context. 
As a secondary mission, the Language Academy teaches cultural and military history as a supplement to its language program. The culture/history of the host nation is normally provided by a host nation guest lecturer and portions of the curriculum address CIOR background, philosophy of NATO and PfP as well as the United Nations Peacekeeping.

The Academy convenes once annually for a period of two weeks. 

A limited number of Scholarships are available as provided by CIOR affiliated nations, as well as military units, non-governmental organisations, individuals and other sources.

Name: TroopsToTruckers.com
Description: Troops to Truckers has been assisting transitioning military veterans in securing education and employment for over 20 years.

Name: Air Transport Wing
Description:
Air Transport Wing har over 400 dedikerede og professionelle medarbejdere, der har en stor og mangeartet palet af opgaver inden for lufttransport og drift af en flyvestation. 
Opgaver hos Air Transport Wing
Det betyder, at vi står for al den militære transport- og inspektionsflyvning, der kræver speciel kunnen og materiel, og som ikke umiddelbart kan løses af andre end Flyvevåbnet.
 
Vores C-130J Hercules transportfly flyver rutinemæssigt i Forsvarets internationale missioner med de trusler og udfordringer, det medfører.
Vores CL-604 Challenger flyver regelmæssigt miljø- og overvågningsflyvning i Danmark. CL-604 Challenger fungerer også som VIP fly for regeringen og kongehuset.
 
Begge fly opererer hjemmevante i Grønland, hvor naturen og infrastrukturen giver spændende, men ikke altid optimale arbejdsvilkår.
 
Ved siden af dette har Air Transport Wing også en stor opgave i at drive Flyvestation Aalborg som militær garnison og lufthavn. Vi står for bevogtning, sikkerhed, brand- og redning og støtte til besøgende fly. Derudover er det også mange besøgende og vi gennemfører erhvervspraktik.
Mange af disse opgaver er skjult i den daglige drift eller er så naturlige, at vi ikke tænker over det. Lige så lidt som en flyvemaskine kan flyve uden fuel, kan en flyvestation ikke virke uden alle disse funktioner.
 
Specialiserede opgaver
Air Transport Wing har et forbilledligt samarbejde med den civile Aalborg Lufthavn, som vi ud over start- og landingsbane deler Brand- og redningstjeneste og flyvekontrol med.
 
Vores motto "Fortis, Firmus Undique – Stærk, Pålidelig, Overalt" fortæller at vi er klar til at gøre en forskel, når det brænder på. Det lever vi op til – i al beskedenhed.

Name: NATO Allied Land Command (LANDCOM)
Description: On order, LANDCOM serves as LCC in support of JFCs and as a CFLCC to provide theatre-wide domain expertise to SACEUR. As SACEUR’s principal land advisor, LANDCOM coordinates AOR-wide activities to effectively deter Russia and Terror Groups and ensure a trained, ready, and lethal land force for NATO.

Name: Fleet Air Arm Officers Association
Description:
Membership is open to officers of all specialisations. Amongst our members are pilots, observers, Royal Marines, air engineers, air traffic controllers, meteorological forecasters, specialists in air-medicine, exchange officers from the RAF, AAC and foreign navies as well as officers from other branches of the Navy who served in ships having Fleet Air Arm squadrons and detachments.

Membership of the FAAOA is subject to the approval of the Committee.

Social Functions
The FAAOA organises an annual Taranto Night Dinner in London. There are also regionally organised First of the Month Meetings (FOTMMs) in many parts of the UK and abroad. Other dinners and reunions are advertised through the events section but members must log in to the website to view the details.

More member benefits on the website.

Name: Joint Base San Antonio
Description: Joint Base San Antonio (JBSA) is a United States military facility located in San Antonio, Texas. The facility operates under the jurisdiction of the United States Air Force's 502d Air Base Wing, part of the Air Education and Training Command (AETC). The wing's three Mission Support Groups are responsible for the installation support mission across the three bases that comprise JBSA.

Name: 335th Signal Command (Theater)
Description: Welcome to the official 335th Signal Command (T), Linkedin page where you will find photos, videos and great news stories highlighting the Soldiers, Civilians, and Families of the elite Signal and Cyber command. “Ready Lightning—-Strike Twice!”

Name: Operational Test and Evaluation Force
Description: We have been headquartered in Norfolk, Virginia, since inception in 1945. The Navy's Operational Test and Evaluation Force provides an independent and objective evaluation of the operational effectiveness and suitability of naval aviation, surface, subsurface, expeditionary, C4I, cryptologic, and space systems in support of Department of Defense and Navy acquisition and fleet introduction decisions.

Name: EOD ETC
Description: Looking for a trusted provider of customized training, product sourcing, and security solutions for state and federal procurements? Our team of procurement experts has a proven track record of delivering exceptional service and results to clients worldwide. As a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business, we pride ourselves on streamlining the procurement process by cutting out unnecessary middle management layers, and delivering efficient and effective solutions. Our impressive client list includes prestigious organizations such as the U.S. State Department, Pacific Architects and Engineers (PAE) Inc., Phalanx Group Ltd., Amentum Inc., and IDS International. Let us help you achieve your procurement goals with confidence and ease.

Name: PMW 760
Description:
The Ship Integration Program Office coordinates the design, acquisition, test, integration, and installation of C4I capabilities
developed by the various program offices within PEO C4I on New Construction and in service U.S. Navy surface ships, Military Sealift Command (MSC) special mission and support ships, U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) Cutters, and AEGIS Ashore sites.

Name: MilSpouse Transition
Description:
MilSpouse Transition is a space that welcomes every military spouse at every stage of the journey. Leaving active duty military life is hard. Here, you’ll find resources, organizations, tips, tricks, and the latest and greatest advice — all to support military spouses as they navigate all the moving pieces of transition tasks and then launch into living their best lives.

We’re a work in process continually refining and defining the best of the best to make your transition journey better than you ever imagined.

Name: AC SOLUÇÕES ASSESSORIA EMPRESARIAL
Description:
Partnerships for LOGISTICS, EXPORTS AND IMPORTS - Outsourcing solutions, supply chain, customs advice, multimodal transport, engineering and cargo handling and transport operations (special or not), in addition to a transport feasibility study (when necessary), always respecting Brazilian Army standards, when applicable. 
ORGANIZATION OF EXHIBITIONS AND NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL EVENTS - Complete solutions for holding events of any size, as well as assembly and design of stands at exhibitions, as well as marketing and communication focused on the sector. 
BRAZILIAN DEFENSE MARKET STUDIES AND MATCHMAKING - Tailored studies for foreign companies seeking partnerships in the Brazilian defense and security market and B2B approach to companies, aiming at new business. 
ELECTRIC ARMOUR - Unique solution in the world, patented in Brazil and internationally, it has guaranteed the protection of 100% of the loads that use the service, among them, Fedex. It can be used on any surface, such as bank treasury, weapons magazine, prisons, warehouses, museums, etc. EQUIPMENT FOR SECURITY OF FACILITIES - Various equipment for security of facilities (access control, cameras, drones etc), residential or industrial, for sale or lease. 
FINANCIAL SOLUTIONS - Complete financial options, such as export credit, foreign exchange, working capital, various insurance and investments for companies and individuals, with the greatest credibility in the market.

Name: UAPC - Union pour l'Armement de Petit Calibre
Description: L’UAPC est un groupement dédié à fédérer les industriels français du petit calibre, renforcer la réindustrialisation de ce secteur stratégique, promouvoir le tir sportif et défendre les intérêts des producteurs nationaux d’armement léger.

Name: U.S. Army Communication & Outreach Office - Northeast
Description: ACOO-NE is the main entry point into U.S. Army public outreach and earned national media engagement in New York City and the Northeast. The U.S. Army Communication & Outreach Office, - Northeast Regional Office, ACOO-NE is a small team uniquely positioned to bring Army stories, personnel and subject matter experts to critical audiences in civic, media, industry, entertainment and think tank spaces.

Name: Natolin Defence Society
Description: Natolin Defence Society of the College of Europe

Name: Invisible Warriors
Description:
Invisible Warriors is a community-driven nonprofit dedicated to honoring, supporting, and empowering military women at every stage of their journey.

We know that serving in the military shapes who you are — but your story doesn’t end when you take off the uniform. Our mission is to make sure you feel seen, valued, and unstoppable long after active duty. We believe every military woman deserves to be recognized for her unique contributions, supported through life’s challenges and transitions, and connected with a community that truly understands.

At Invisible Warriors, we provide more than just words of encouragement — we create real opportunities for connection, growth, and empowerment. Whether you’re navigating the shift to civilian life, looking for resources to help you thrive personally and professionally, or seeking a sisterhood of women who get what you’ve been through, we are here for you. Our programs, advocacy efforts, and support networks are designed to uplift your voice, honor your experiences, and help you reclaim your strength.

Together, we build a powerful community where military women can share stories, find support, and access tools that empower them to rise — no matter what life brings next. Because your service matters, your journey matters, and above all, your future is bright.

Invisible Warriors is more than an organization — it’s a movement dedicated to walking with you, celebrating you, and helping you step confidently into what’s next.

Name: Javlin Concepts
Description:
Javlin Concepts is the direct product of knowledge gained though years of Military experience in the Special Operations Community and the desire to improve the operator’s gear by increasing comfortability, modularity, and user maneuverability. 

Javlin Concepts has gone above and beyond this goal by challenging the traditional way of thought with typical designs and starting over from the ground up. This has led to truly unique designs, tailored to maximizing operator lethality while emphasizing the importance of maneuverability and comfort.

All Javlin gear is designed and manufactured in Austin, Texas by former Special Operations Operators using top-tier, Berry compliant, quality Mil-spec materials.

Name: Marine Aviation Detachment
Description:
The Marine Aviation Detachment (MAD) is responsible to the Deputy Commandant for Aviation regarding U.S. Marine Corps manpower support of NAVAIR.

MAD's role is to support the Marine Corps' equity in the various naval aviation acquisition programs through the entire life cycle. Assignments for the service members at MAD range from program managers, logistics and fleet support, to test pilots, engineers and administrative support.

The Commanding Officer performs duties as the Occupational Field sponsor and advocate for the entire Marine aviation acquisition work force. He provides assistance to the Commandant of the Marine Corps through the Deputy Commandant for Aviation, in coordination with the Marine Corps Systems Command and the Deputy Commandant for Manpower in the career development of Marine Corps members assigned nationwide to the aviation acquisition work force.

Name: SAME Australia Field Chapter
Description:
The Society of American Military Engineers (SAME) is a global, nonprofit professional engineering education and networking organization that actively promotes the advancement of both individual technical knowledge and the collective engineering capabilities of governments, the uniformed services, academia, and private industry.

The SAME Australia Chapter brings together members of the U.S. and Australian Defence Forces, government agencies, universities, and the Architectural, Engineering, and Construction community to strengthen cooperation and share expertise. Our mission is to foster collaboration in defence infrastructure, humanitarian assistance, disaster response, and emerging Indo-Pacific security challenges.

Locally, the Australia Chapter supports regular events and professional networking opportunities that feature guest speakers from military, government, academia, and broader defence industry sectors. We also participate in regional defence and industry events across the continent. These gatherings provide networking, professional development, and accredited continuing education opportunities for engineers, architects, and other professionals.

The SAME Australian Chapter is supported by the SAME Honolulu Post and the national Society of American Military Engineers, including access to publications such as The Military Engineer. Together, these resources help promote the interests and goals of our society and its members.

Name: SOF AT&L Acquisition Executive
Description:
Established in 1991, the Special Operations Forces Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics (SOF AT&L) Center is responsible for all USSOCOM research, development, acquisition, procurement, and logistics.

SOF AT&L works closely with government, academia, and industry in meeting its mission to provide rapid and focused acquisition, technology, and logistics support to Warfighters, delivering the most effective capabilities to our Special Operations Forces.

The SOF AT&L mission is to provide rapid and focused acquisition, technology, and acquisition logistics support to SOF Warfighters. To accomplish this mission, SOF AT&L comprises nine Program Executive Offices (C4, Fixed Wing, Maritime, Rotary Wing, SOF  Digital Applications, SOF Support Activity, SOF Warrior, Services, and Special Reconnaissance) and four Directorates (Comptroller, Logistics, Procurement, and Science & Technology). Together, they manage and support the development, acquisition, and fielding of critical items to enable a no-fail mission for the SOF Warfighter. SOF AT&L executes its mission and accelerates the force by following four key acquisition principles:

1) Delivers capability to the user expeditiously;
2) Exploits proven techniques and methods;
3) Keeps Warfighters involved throughout the process; and
4) Takes risk and manages it.

In a given year, SOF AT&L delivers more than 100 aircraft; 700 tactical vehicles; 4,000 weapon systems; 20 million munitions; 3,000 radios; 2,000 C4I items; and 600 ISR kits to SOF units.

For an overview of the Program Executive Offices and the Directorate of Science & Technology, please visit our Programs page.

PowerBrokers is a defense lobbying firm dedicated to strengthening Europe’s resilience and preparedness for armed conflict. Our mission is to unite key players across the defense industry to achieve meaningful breakthroughs.

At the heart of next-generation defense, PowerBrokers connects innovators, manufacturers, and decision-makers to deliver the best systems and solutions — right to the end user.

With offices in Europe, Ukraine, and Brazil, we foster global collaboration and strategic partnerships that bridge continents and drive progress.
PowerBrokers opens new sales channels, secures high-value deals, and arranges strategic investor injections. Through our extensive worldwide defense network, we connect opportunities with the right partners — turning potential into performance.

We work exclusively with three distinct types of companies — each a powerhouse in its own right:

1. The Small Brilliants: Startups and scaleups driving innovation and disruption.
2. Industrial Giants: Established large-scale defense and industrial players with proven capabilities.
3. One-of-a-Kind Jewels: Companies offering unique, hard-to-replace solutions that give their partners a competitive edge.

Together, we build connections that strengthen industries, accelerate growth, and ensure Europe’s readiness for the challenges ahead.

Name: Papua New Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF)
Description:
The Papua New Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF) is the military organization responsible for the defence and security of Papua New Guinea. Established in 1973, the PNGDF operates under the Ministry of Defence and plays a central role in safeguarding national sovereignty, supporting civil authorities, and contributing to regional stability across the Pacific.

Comprising the Land Element, Maritime Element, and Air Element, the PNGDF’s mission extends beyond traditional defence. Its personnel are actively engaged in humanitarian assistance, disaster relief, infrastructure development, and international peacekeeping operations under the United Nations and regional security frameworks.

Headquartered in Port Moresby, the PNGDF maintains operational bases across the country, including Lombrum Naval Base (Manus), Igam Barracks (Lae), Taurama Barracks (Port Moresby), and Murray Barracks (Headquarters). Through its partnership with the Australian Defence Force (ADF) and other allied nations, the PNGDF continues to enhance interoperability, training, and capability development to meet modern security challenges.

Name: LMD CONSEIL
Description:
Chez LMD CONSEIL, nous vous offrons des services de conseils personnalisés pour garantir des solutions sur mesure qui répondent à  tous vos besoins de développement et de sécurité à à l'international ainsi que de notoriété et d'influence.
Avantages de choisir LMD CONSEIL
Notre approche sans intermédiaires garantit un service efficace et sur mesure. LMD CONSEIL dispose d'une vision française de l'international ainsi que d'une culture permettant de décrypter les évolutions politico-diplomatiques françaises à l'international. Se développer à l'international en sécurité ou renforcer son influence en France et d'influence politiques.
À LMD CONSEIL, nous nous assurons de répondre aux besoins internationaux de nos clients en proposant des services sur mesure : conseil stratégique, due diligence, diplomatie économique, gestion du risque politique et géopolitique, aide à l'internationalisation, mise en relation, communication, influence, campagnes électorales.

Notre approche n'est ni strictement diplomatique ni strictement politique mais plutôt politico-diplomatique. Pas de langue de bois mais une connaissance réelle de vos interlocuteurs qui invite les impairs. Pas de perte de temps dans des considérations générales et peu opératoires.

Notre éthique nous conduit à ne pas nous mettre aux services des causes extrémistes. 
Notre dirigeant Erwan DAVOUX est diplômé de l'Institut d'Etudes Politiques de Paris et de l'Institut National des Langues et Civilisations Orientales.
Il fut en fonction au ministères de l'Intérieur et de la Défense (DGSE)
Chargé de mission pour la Francophonie puis le Monde Arabe à la Présidence de la République.
Erwan DAVOUX a conseillé de nombreuses personnalités politiques françaises à l'à l'International (Jean-Pierre Raffarin, Alain Juppé, Jean-François Copé, Michèle Alliot-Marie).
Il dispose d'une solide réseau à l'International, particulièrement Monde Arabe et Afrique, mis à disposition toute stratégie d'internationalisation.

Name: OPFOR Journal
Description: OPFOR Journal documents, analyzes, and anticipates the threats posed to the US by China, Russia, Iran and North Korea, treating them not as separate threats, but as a collective “Opposing Force” or OPFOR. OPFOR Journal tracks power—how these nations build it, wield it, and deploy it to threaten the interests of America and allies. Coverage spans all instruments of national power from Diplomatic, Informational, Military, and Economic actions.

Name: The Public Intelligence Project - CIA Labs
Description:
The Public Intelligence Project is a legally recognized sub-agency and office under the Public Intelligence and Military Medical Research Divisions of the CIA. Established by a Classified Legislative Committee in Washington DC, and the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) in McLean, Virginia, in 2010 under the Obama administration.

This initiative operates under the oversight of the United States Department of War, specifically the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA). The objective of the Public Intelligence Project, is to enhance transparency in government operations. Additionally, the project is co-managed by the Department of Justice, with AUSA Catherine Connelly (Washington, D.C.) serving as the acting agency head, ensuring proper government oversight, in all Public Intelligence Project activities, according to DoD policy, and applicable law and regulations.

In September of 2020, The Public Intelligence Project officially became a lab, and sub agency of the CIA Labs upon the Labs establishment.

Description:

Conceived at the end of 2022 and refined through silent developments in 2023–2024, DRONIVKA is now public with a clear mission: to help governments, armed forces, international organizations, companies and strategic infrastructures seize the drone revolution.

From doctrine and rules of engagement, to tailor-made tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs), to the creation of national drone schools (offensive and defensive), we provide end-to-end solutions.

Name: The Geo Godfather Wars podcast
Description:

Receive insights and hard-hitting analysis from Barak Seener & Leah Tedrow on realities on the ground far removed from the curated picture offered by today's media.

In an age of disinformation accompanied by changing trade and business practices, companies as well as members of the public want to sift between real and ‘fake news’ and have a deeper understanding of future trends and how global affairs are interconnected. Our security and economic landscapes are impacted by the post-Covid era, a rising China, a resurgent Russia, and a nuclear aspiring Iran. The Geo-Godfather Wars explores how these contribute to shifting trade patterns, heightening the risk to industries and investment portfolios, producing barriers to entry to foreign markets, and creating market volatility.

AE seeks to modernize the bureaucratic culture within US national security, defense, and foreign affairs to concur with modern geopolitical trends, risks, developments, and analysis. Name: ASAF : Association de Soutien aux Armes de la France
Description:
L'Association de Soutien aux Armes de la France (ASAF)  est une association nationale (loi de 1901) créée en 1983. Elle a pour but de contribuer à ce que la Défense soit toujours une priorité pour l’Etat et que l’armée demeure au cœur de la Nation.

Elle est organisée autour :-  d’un conseil d’administration, d’un bureau et d’un comité de rédaction, qui orientent et coordonnent les activités et assurent la réalisation des publications de l’association ; - de délégués départementaux qui organisent et conduisent les actions sur le terrain (conférences, cafés stratégiques dans les universités et grandes écoles, visites d’installations militaires,…) en liaison avec des partenaires locaux. 

Reconnue d’intérêt général, l’ASAF agit essentiellement dans les domaines éducatif et culturel. Elle vise en priorité, à sensibiliser, notamment les jeunes, aux questions de Défense et au devoir de Mémoire. Reconnue officiellement par les autorités politiques, membre du groupe des 12 associations les plus représentatives du monde combattant, elle s’exprime annuellement devant la commission de la Défense et coopère directement avec de nombreux organismes du ministère des Armées.  Elle agit toujours en étroite concertation avec les associations qui contribuent à promouvoir, comme elle l’esprit de défense et contribuent au devoir de mémoire.

Forte aujourd’hui de 4 000 adhérents, dont près de 500 associations qui représentent plus de  100 000 membres, l’ASAF a mis en place et développé les vecteurs de communication ...

Pour rejoindre l’ASAF : www.asafrance.fr contact@asafrance.fr

Desde sua primeira reunião, em 2006, a associação tem objetivos bem definidos:

A) Preservar as tradições das seguintes unidades da Polícia do Exército no Estado de São Paulo:

•	2º BPE – Osasco
•	8º BPE – São Paulo
•	11º Pelotão de Polícia do Exército – Campinas
•	12º Pelotão de Polícia do Exército – Caçapava

B) Reunir todos os militares – da ativa, da reserva ou reservistas – que tenham servido em qualquer dessas unidades, valorizando o privilégio de integrar a Polícia do Exército.

C) Congregar:
•	Personalidades brasileiras e estrangeiras;
•	Instituições condecoradas pelo Exército Brasileiro;
•	Agraciados com o título de Colaborador Emérito ou Amigo dos Batalhões de Polícia do Exército;
•	Oficiais e graduados, da ativa, da reserva ou reformados, com vínculos com essas unidades.

D) Difundir valores doutrinários e culturais que contribuam para o desenvolvimento nacional, com foco na Força Terrestre, sem envolvimento político-partidário.

• Acesse mais em nosso estatuto.

•	Nossa Missão
Atender com qualidade, eficiência, transparência e compromisso  às necessidades dos militares da ativa, da reserva e dos reservistas da Polícia do Exército.

Promover encontros periódicos com os seguintes objetivos:

 	Aliviar o estresse do dia a dia;
 	Compartilhar bons momentos e conversas;
 	Dar muitas risadas;

 	Manter vivas as tradições da Polícia do Exército;

•	Nossa Visão
Ser um grupo com atuação consolidada no estado de São Paulo, tornando-se referência na realização de eventos e na oferta de soluções integradas em serviços de integração ao setor de Segurança e Facilities afins, com profundo conhecimento das necessidades de seus associados.

Name: Military Order of Foreign Wars - New York
Description:
The New York Commandery of the Military Order of Foreign Wars was founded by military officers in New York City on 27 December 1894.  Who We Are:
•	Our members are known as “Companions.”  Our state headquarters is in New York City and we also have our Niagara Frontier Chapter, located in upstate New York.  
•	Our Companions display a healthy degree of sartorial enthusiasm at our events, almost all of which are jacket and tie / female equivalent.  
•	We emphasize the importance of fellowship among our Companions, each with different temperaments, talents and convictions, and all united in their appreciation for service to our nation during a time of war.  
•	We meet regularly throughout the year.  We host cocktail receptions, have a yearly dinner and attend functions of other organizations such as Flag Day at St. Thomas Church, and the Flag Parade hosted by the Sons of the Revolution in June.  
 
Eligibility.  There are three, basic categories of eligibility:

•	A Veteran Companion.  Men and women who served as Commissioned or Warrant Officers during a time of war or specified military expeditions (Active Duty, activated Reserve/National Guard, Coast Guard).   Wars include, but are not limited to, Iraq, Afghanistan, and expeditions supporting the Global War on Terrorism.  Service during wartime does not require deployment to a declared combat zone.  Accordingly, those who provide documentation they received the National Defense Service Medal are typically eligible to apply.  Expeditionary and campaign medals, such as the Kosovo Campaign Medal and the Global War on Terrorism Expeditionary Medal also typically make an applicant eligible.  

•	Hereditary Companion.  Men and women who are lineal descendants (child, grandchild, great-grandchild, et al) of veterans as described above.

•	Allied Companion.  Officers of Allies of the United States during a time of war, or lineal descendants of such officers.

Our work focuses on influencing attacker decision-making and building environments that actively deter further engagement.

Many members have served in military and intelligence roles, including operational deployment in high-risk regions such as Ukraine. Their background in rapidly shifting, high-pressure environments enables us to model physical threats and create realistic, effective risk-reduction measures.

Our team also includes experts in hybrid-threat domains: psychological and cognitive operations, information warfare, disinformation control, and influence activities across digital platforms. These insights allow us to deliver proactive, integrated countermeasures.

Our multinational staff shares a common tactical foundation shaped by real-world operations, enabling adaptive and internationally aligned responses.

Core Services
• Physical Security: Threat modeling, on-site vulnerability assessments, penetration testing, intrusion-path denial, and integrated risk-reduction planning.
• Human-Factor Security: Protection against social engineering, insider threats, and interpersonal risks.
• Hybrid-Threat Defense: Countermeasures against disinformation, manipulation, and influence operations.
• Military Technology Consulting: Support for capability development, technical adoption, and tactical evaluation.

We are not a company for those seeking symbolic or superficial security measures. We deliver value to organizations willing to confront threats decisively and build environments that work against attackers.

Name: National Veterans Rights Association
Description:
Sixty thousand homeless. Millions denied the care to which they’re entitled. Disability and pension claims so backlogged that soldiers returning from current conflicts are ending up on the streets. Among veterans who served after 9/11, the unemployment rate is 18% higher than the non-veteran population. Every day, approximately 22 veterans and 27 active duty soldiers commit suicide. That’s more than 8,000 veterans and soldiers killing themselves annually. The National Veterans Rights Association (NVRA) is dedicated to helping our forgotten heroes by offering them the assistance and guidance they need to achieve a better life.

If that were the NVRA’s sole mission, it would be lost in the sea of similar organizations. In addition to providing veterans with uniquely effective systems to get the help and benefits they deserve, the NVRA is shining the light of truth on the reasons behind the sad state of veterans affairs.

To that end we are producing a one-of-a-kind documentary, which will take the viewer inside the hearts and minds of these veterans, who put their lives on the line for the country they love. The untold story will rivet the audience, disclosing the mistreatment of veterans and how they fight for their compensation and healthcare rights to create a life worth living without becoming a suicide statistic. We’ll delve deep into the roots, and boldly show the cause of veteran despair from misdiagnoses, misconceptions, homelessness, suicide and benefit abuse. Along our journey we will meet some organizations like ourselves that have taken matters into their own hands to overcome adversity and bring hope and justice into the lives of veterans by assisting them in their quest for a better life.

Name: Veterans Transition Center
Description:
The Veterans Transition Center (VTC) is a comprehensive digital platform designed to modernize the military-to-civilian transition process.

Founded by two Senior Information Systems Executive with each having over 20-year Navy experience, VTC was built to solve a critical problem: the fragmentation of veteran resources. We replace outdated checklists with a secure, centralized digital command center.

We started our site with one goal in mind: support and empower reservists with the information and resources they need. Ultimately, we hope to build a community, a voice for reservists, and a repository that holistically informs and helps reservists, from details on deployments to updates on policy, from resume assistance to finding reserve friendly employers, from family resources to personal benefits. We are reservists. We've deployed, we've negotiated the VA processes, we've read the manuals, we've paid to get to drill. We've worked with active duty and civilian employers that truly supported and thanked us for our work and service, those who only provided lip service, and those who were downright antagonistic. We've seen, heard, and suffered from the misinformation, and we've been lifted up both those who helped us find the truth and benefits we were due. We are here to empower all past, current, and future Reservists.

Name: European Air Transport Command
Description:
The European Air Transport Command is a single multinational command. Its headquarters is located at Eindhoven air base in the Netherlands.

The EATC is a unique organisation for military air mobility, including transport, air-to-air refuelling and aeromedical evacuation within Europe.

The overall objective is to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of the member nations' military air transport efforts.

The idea of EATC was born in 1999. The driving parties were France and Germany, who looked back at a strong bilateral cooperation in the field of air transport. As NATO and the EU identified shortfalls in the domain of strategic transportation, initiatives were developed over the years, aiming at the highest degree of cooperation in a multinational command structure with an operational and a functional authority.

The ground rules for EATC were set in September 2010 and EATC was inaugurated at Eindhoven air base by the four founding members, The Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany.

Promoting the study of military medicine
Bringing together those with an interest in military medicine
Disseminating knowledge of military medicine
Publishing and distributing a journal in military medicine
Promoting research in military medicine
Membership of the Association is open to doctors, dentists, nurses, pharmacists, paramedics, human factors specialists, psychologists, psychiatrists and anyone with a professional interest in any of the disciplines of military health. The Association is totally independent of the Australian Defence Force, however there are strong connections with the Australian Defence Force Health Services Branch.

The Association reflects and encourages the broad spectrum of health that contributes to the fascinating and vital discipline of military medicine and veterans’ health.

ASLSP is an eleven-month senior service college (SSC) resident program that educates “future senior leaders of the Armed Forces, allies and the inter-agency for high-level policy, command, and staff responsibilities.” The program provides a rigorous graduate-level education, exposing students to, and preparing them for, the volatile, uncertain, complex, and ambiguous dimensions of the joint, inter-agency, and multinational security environment. Students learn about strategy formulation, implementation, and campaigning in a dynamic global setting. The ASLSP curriculum provides a comprehensive, multifaceted education focused at the theater-strategic level across the spectrum of joint and service operations during peace, crisis, and war.

Name: Milipol Equipment Trading & Services (METQA)
Description:
Milipol – Your Trusted Partner in Defense and Security
Milipol is a trusted global defense and security solutions provider, serving both military and private-sector clients with cutting-edge equipment, consulting, and support services.
Milipol is committed to delivering high-performance solutions to meet today’s complex security challenges. We specialize in advanced tactical support, defense consulting, and innovative technologies for government agencies, law enforcement bodies, and private-sector clients around the world. 
Our team is composed of highly skilled technical specialists with extensive field experience. We offer a comprehensive range of services, including infrastructure consulting and service provision, engineering support, the supply of customized equipment and systems, personnel training, logistics, and the development of cutting-edge security solutions.
With a strong focus on precision, reliability, and discretion, Milipol operates with the highest standards of professionalism and integrity. We are dedicated to helping our clients protect national interests, secure critical infrastructure, and safeguard lives in the most challenging environments.
Partner with Milipol to elevate your capabilities through proven expertise and next-generation solutions.

Description: 11th Airborne Division executes expeditionary operations within the Indo-Pacific theater, conducts Multi-Domain Operations in the Arctic, defends critical infrastructure in homeland defense and on order is capable of decisively defeating any adversary in extreme cold weather, mountainous, high latitude, and high altitude environments through large scale combat operations.

Name: 81st Readiness Division
Description:
Welcome the official page of the U.S Army Reserve’s 81st Readiness Division (RD).  The 81st RD, headquartered at Fort Jackson, S.C., is a two-star command responsible for maximizing Army Reserve Soldier, Equipment, and Facility readiness throughout nine Southeastern states, Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Our area of responsibility includes more than 50,000 Army Reserve Soldiers and more than 250 Army Reserve Facilities and maintenance shops.  
Bearers of the “Wildcat” patch, the first official patch of the U.S. Army, the Wildcats of the 81st Readiness Division continue to play a vital role in the combat readiness of the U.S. Army.  Our motto, “Wildcats Never Quit.”

Description:

Cette initiative française, à vocation internationale, s’adresse aux start-up innovantes, PME, ETI et groupes recherchant un réseau d’experts de défense et de sécurité de haut niveau et connectés à l’international. ECD Conseil accompagne les acteurs déjà établis ainsi que les jeunes pousses innovantes en apportant ses compétences, réseaux et conseils dans le secteur de la défense et de la sécurité. ECD Conseil fait le pont entre les acteurs publics et privés, français et étrangers afin de construire un écosystème mondial de référence dédié aux acteurs de la sécurité et de la défense.

Com atuação pautada na agilidade, imparcialidade e rigor técnico, o laboratório está plenamente alinhado às exigências das regulamentações compulsórias estabelecidas pela Diretoria de Fiscalização de Produtos Controlados (DFPC) e pela Secretaria Nacional de Segurança Pública (SENASP).

Constitui-se como um parceiro estratégico dos Organismos de Certificação Designados (OCDs), contribuindo diretamente para o processo de certificação de PCEs e fortalecendo os padrões de qualidade, segurança e confiabilidade no setor.

 Imparcialidade, confiabilidade e agilidade em cada ensaio.

Name: Great Philosophical Problems ThinkTank
Description:
MISSION STATEMENT

GPP THINKTANK | Our mission is the unifying definition of the moral injury construct to address suicide in veteran, and wider civilian populations.  The provision of a universal lexicon that can articulate “moral grammar” for the epoch-defining emergence, and psychological articulation, of Moral Injury.  Collaboration with international centers of excellence, including the International Centre for Moral Injury and United States War College, Washington.

Moral Injury is a disruption to the psyche from the will to truth that has become conscious of itself as a problem in us.  Deeply embedded substructure psychologies, that for the West emerge over two thousand years as synchronous psychological concomitants of the Christ-figure. For to be sure, Nietzsche would have agreed with Jung when he says; "man can suffer only a certain amount of culture without injury.” 

Great Philosophical Problems ThinkTank will articulate a Moral Grammar derived from Friedrich Nietzsche.  A lexicon obtained from a cipher the author embedded in his works that utilize the power of the A-E-I-O-U Bible method and Vedanta-philosophy. A way suffers can form a relationship with the four main questions for the Christian psyche concerning strength in the inner being; what is the Breadth and Length and Height and Depth?

While soldiers provide the coal face psychology for Moral Injury today, just as PTSD before, such a assignation will not be restricted to such cohorts. Moral Injury represents, the great difficulty Nietzsche identifies as looming for the contemporary Western consciousness, where not only the rationality of millennia – but also its madness, breaks out in us – where dangerous is it to be an heir.

Description:
Join 1500+ professionals at DSET 2025, the fully hybrid event for Military, Government, Blue Light, Industry, and Academia in Simulation & Training.

What sets DSET apart?

• Military-set agenda led by experts.

• Integrated Defence & Blue Light collaboration, focusing on MACA and JESIP principles.

• Open forum for honest discussions on crisis management, interoperability, and resilience.

• Free access for serving Military, Academia, Government, and Blue Light services.

This year’s speaker programme includes leading experts from academia, emergency services, and national resilience organisations. Whether attending in Bristol or online, you’ll be fully engaged in live discussions, networking, and collaborative learning.

Name: Parachute Regiment
Description: The Parachute Regiment leads from the front as airborne Infantry. Of the four battalions, one is permanently at High Readiness for world wide intervention operations. 1 PARA is in role as the Special Forces Support Group.

Name: 1st Homeland Defense Regiment | Heimatschutzregiment 1
Description: Heimatschutzregiment 1 operates under the command structure of the Heimatschutzdivision (Homeland Defense Division) of the German Army and is tasked with regional defense operations within the states of Bavaria and Baden-Württemberg. Regimental headquarters, including logistics and support elements, are garrisoned In Bavaria. Forward-deployed homeland defense companies are strategically positioned both in Bavaria and Baden-Württemberg to ensure rapid regional responsiveness. Reservist training is executed year-round by mobile training teams, maintaining operational readiness across all units. All personnel undergo a comprehensive training regimen encompassing: basic and advanced combat skills, weapons handling and marksmanship, military physical fitness, political education, first aid and field medical support, guard/security operations, and NBC (nuclear, biological, chemical) defense protocols. The regiment stands ready to support both territorial defense and civil disaster response operations in line with national defense mandates.

Name: European Union Military Committee
Description:
The EUMC is the highest military body set up within the Council, and is composed of the Chiefs of Defence (CHODs) of the member states, who are regularly represented by their permanent military representatives.

It is the forum for military consultation and cooperation between the EU Member States in the field of conflict prevention and crisis management.

It directs all military activities within the EU framework, in particular the planning and execution of military missions and operations under the Common Security and Defence Policy (CSDP) and the development of military capabilities.

Based on consensus, it gives military advice and makes recommendations to the Political and Security Committee (PSC), while providing military direction to the European Union Military Staff (EUMS),.

Name: NATO Maritime GEOMETOC Centre of Excellence
Description: The Maritime Geospatial, Meteorological, and Oceanographic Centre of Excellence, located in Lisbon, Portugal, was accredited as NATO’s 28th Centre of Excellence in 2021. Sponsored by Portugal, Romania, Spain, and Türkiye, with Portugal as the framework nation, the Centre supports NATO’s transformation efforts in maritime geospatial, meteorological, and oceanographic sciences. Its goal is to enhance environmental awareness and operational effectiveness for NATO, its Allies, and Partners. The Centre aspires to become an internationally recognized hub of expertise and expand NATO’s capabilities in these fields.

Name: F. Edward Hebert School of Medicine at Uniformed Services University
United States Army Warrant Officers Association (USAWOA)
Description:
Founded in 1972, the USAWOA was incorporated as a not-for-profit association in Virginia on 7 November 1974, and in 1985 became a charter member of The Military Coalition (TMC). On June 23, 2021 the IRS notified the association that it was recognized under Section 501(c)(19), as a non-profit Veterans Service Organization (VSO), effective February 24, 2021.

The three words in the association’s logo predominantly define its mission of advocacy on behalf of the Warrant Officers in all three components of the Army: Professionalism, Representation, and Recognition. The founders of the USAWOA saw the Warrant Officer Corps of the early 1970s as a disjointed group of technical experts with little or no standardization in the area of professional development and sought to change this.

From the beginning the USAWOA has worked within the Army system to encourage the development of an evolving system of standardized professional development for Warrant Officers to complement their superior technical knowledge and skills with increasing levels of leadership development. Simultaneously, it developed and encouraged sources of recognition of Warrant Officer excellence both inside the association and throughout the Army.

Founded in 1972, the USAWOA was incorporated as a not-for-profit association in Virginia on 7 November 1974, and in 1985 became a charter member of The Military Coalition (TMC). Merging the legacies of the Journal of Indo-Pacific Affairs, Journal of the Americas, and Journal of European, Middle Eastern, & African Affairs, The journal provides a truly global perspective on defense strategy, great-power competition, and emerging threats. With a focus on integrated deterrence and cross-regional analysis, Strategic Horizons delivers cutting-edge research and strategic insights to military leaders, policymakers, and security professionals shaping the future of international security.

Description:

The Forge is SLD 45's foundation for innovation, designed to leverage technology and off-the-shelf- solutions to fix the military's problems and bridge the gap between Guardians, Airmen, and industry experts.

Name: Fonds de dotation de l'IHEDN
Description:
Le Fonds de dotation de l’IHEDN a été créé en 2010 afin de gérer les dons qu’il reçoit de mécènes en vue d’assister l’Institut dans l’accomplissement de ses missions de développement de l’esprit de défense et de diffusion des connaissances en matière de défense, de sécurité nationale et de souveraineté (numérique, économique, etc.).

Ceci concerne aussi bien le développement d'initiatives favorisant la cohésion nationale, la citoyenneté et la diffusion de l'esprit de défense, que la conduite de travaux de recherche.

Le fonds administre notamment deux chaires :
- Chaire Économie de défense – IHEDN
- Chaire Cyber et souveraineté numérique – IHEDN

Ces chaires ont pour objectifs : 
- De produire des travaux de recherche universitaire à destination de la communauté scientifique, des partenaires institutionnels et du grand public ;
- D’intervenir dans le cadre d’enseignements spécialisés, d’organiser des conférences et d’animer des groupes de travail ; 
- Et ainsi d’être une interface entre le monde académique, les décideurs publics et les acteurs privés. 

Elles contribuent également à la réflexion stratégique de l’IHEDN par leur apport aux formations organisées par IHEDN, par exemple la Session nationale de l’Institut, en particulier  trois de ses majeures, Armement et économie de défense (AED), Défense et sécurité économique (DSE) et Souveraineté numérique et cyberdéfense (SNC) : contribution à la réflexion sur les thèmes de comités, contribution à l’élaboration de fonds documentaires, soutien aux travaux de comités, interventions comme conférenciers dans les formations, etc.

Le fonds organise aussi des formations pour les étudiants post bac désireux d’étendre leurs connaissances en matière de défense et de sécurité nationale.

Le Fonds de dotation de l'IHEDN est membre fondateur de l'Académie de défense de l'École militaire (ACADEM), créée en 2023.

Description:
The British Nuclear Dialogue is a premier international forum dedicated to fostering collaboration between industry, government, and academia across the United Kingdom, Australia, and the United States. As part of the broader AUKUS partnership, this initiative brings together leaders in nuclear energy, defense, and advanced technologies to drive innovation, strengthen supply chains, and develop the skilled workforce needed for the future of nuclear power.

Building on the success of the Sydney Nuclear Summit and the Virginia Nuclear Industry Working Group, the British Nuclear Dialogue provides a strategic platform for policy discussions, industry partnerships, and research collaborations. Key focus areas include nuclear propulsion, small modular reactors (SMRs), fusion energy, and regulatory alignment.

Join us as we shape the future of the nuclear industry and unlock opportunities for clean energy, national security, and technological advancement.

Wild Hornets is a Ukrainian MilTech company developing combat drones for the needs of the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

Founded in the spring of 2023, the organization closely cooperates with elite units of Ukraine’s defense forces, providing innovative solutions in the field of unmanned systems.

The main focus is the development and mass production of cutting-edge FPV drones, aerial interceptors, wing-type reconnaissance drones, kamikaze drones, ground control stations, systems for remote mining, bombing, and aerial logistics missions.

The flagship products of Wild Hornets include kamikaze drones, the large FPV drone Queen Hornet, digital ground stations, batteries, anti-aircraft drones for intercepting enemy reconnaissance UAVs, and STING — a drone designed to destroy kamikaze drones such as the Shahed.

Name: Delaware Air National Guard
Description:
The 166th AW is located on New Castle Airport, near New Castle and Wilmington, Delaware. 

More than 1,000 men and women of the 166th AW support, maintain and fly the C-130H2 model Hercules transport. This venerable aircraft is used in a variety of roles to include airlift and airdrop of people, equipment and supplies, and aeromedical evacuation of people. Our particular aircraft have seen duty in Saudi Arabia during Operations Desert Shield and Desert Storm inside Iraq and in nearby nations in Southwest Asia, in Operation Just Cause in Panama, in Operation Joint Endeavor in Bosnia, in Operations Iraqi Freedom and New Dawn inside Iraq and in nearby nations in Southwest Asia, in Operation Enduring Freedom inside Afghanistan and in nearby nations in Southwest Asia, in a host of other countries around the world, as well as in several U.S. states performing humanitarian relief and natural disaster response missions. It is a great aircraft with a great mission. 

The Delaware ANG is much more than just its aircraft. It is people performing every vital role that the Air Force, the nation and our state may need during a time of crisis. We invite you to learn more about the Delaware ANG, its history, its people and its opportunities.

Name: SAME Honolulu Post
Description:
About the Post

The Society of American Military Engineers is the only nonprofit professional engineering education organization that actively promotes the advancement of both individual technical knowledge and the collective engineering capabilities of governments, the uniformed services and private industry.

Our goal is to unite public and private sector entities and individuals in the A/E/C fields so that we can prepare for and overcome natural and man made disasters, acts of terrorism and improve security at home and abroad.

The Honolulu Post accomplishes this goal locally by supporting monthly luncheon meetings and other professional/social activities attended by members of local government, military, private industry, students and other interested parties. These meetings are announced via our e-mail distribution list and quarterly newsletter. We are supported by the national Society of American Military Engineers and publications such as The Military Engineer, which also promotes the interests and goals of our society and its members. Please peruse our website to discover the benefits of SAME Honolulu Post membership.

Name: Instituto de Direito e Defesa
Description:
O Instituto de Direito e Defesa (IDD) é uma entidade independente de natureza analítica e técnica, voltada à produção autoral de conhecimento estratégico nas interfaces entre o Direito, as Relações Internacionais e a Defesa Nacional. Fundado com o propósito de fomentar uma cultura jurídica e geopolítica voltada à soberania, à ordem constitucional e à segurança do Estado, o IDD atua como centro independente de pesquisa aplicada, reflexão crítica e apoio técnico-acadêmico.

Seus eixos de estudo compreendem o Direito Militar, o Direito Internacional Público, o Direito de Guerra, o Direito Humanitário e os mecanismos jurídicos e institucionais que resguardam a Defesa Nacional, a Garantia da Lei e da Ordem (GLO), a segurança jurídica das operações militares e a proteção da soberania.

Com atuação voltada à produção de artigos e conteúdos voltados à formação técnico-estratégica, o IDD compromete-se com os valores da legalidade, do civismo e da proteção ao Estado Democrático de Direito. A proposta do Instituto é ser um elo entre o pensamento jurídico e as exigências contemporâneas da política de defesa, promovendo uma abordagem interdisciplinar que reúna doutrina, jurisprudência, geopolítica e estratégia nacional.

O Instituto de Direito e Defesa tem como missão apoiar e qualificar o debate público e institucional sobre temas sensíveis ao interesse nacional, contribuindo para o fortalecimento de uma consciência crítica e responsável sobre os fundamentos legais da soberania, da paz e da ordem social.

Are you ready to take your life to the next level and make a real difference? As the largest and most respected branch of the United States Armed Forces, the U.S. Army offers unparalleled opportunities for personal growth, professional development, and a chance to be part of something greater than yourself.

Who Are We Looking For?

The U.S. Army welcomes individuals from all walks of life who embody our core values of loyalty, duty, respect, selfless service, honor, integrity, and personal courage. We seek individuals who are driven, adaptable, and dedicated to making a positive impact in the world.

Whether you're a recent graduate looking to kickstart your career, a seasoned professional seeking a new challenge, or someone searching for a higher purpose, the U.S. Army has a place for you. We offer a wide range of career paths, including combat roles, technical positions, medical and healthcare specialties, intelligence, logistics, and more.

Name: Center for the Middle East and International Law
Description:
Few geopolitical conflicts attract as much attention as those involving Israel and its Arab neighbors – and none are as ubiquitously framed in the terminology of international law. Thus nowhere is objective academic analysis and debate about the application of international law more essential.

Yet academic legal discussions of the conflict are often explicitly normative, seeking to develop “new” legal norms in the unhospitable soil of the Levant.  Legal discussions of the Israeli/Arab conflict often take place in a bubble, where purported norms are not tested by applicability elsewhere.

The Center will seek to foster debate and critical engagement on international law in the Middle East, with a particular emphasis on developing voices and perspectives that have largely been excluded from the academic discussion, and on challenging underexamined consensus.
