Description:
The Echelon Network Group operates with full discretion in selecting its consultations—be they private, public, or personal. We conduct short-term market analysis and statistics in the cyber and AI domains while maintaining strict independence from corporate or governmental entities.

Our group consists of experts from various fields, including transmission interception, telemetry jamming, and surveillance of high-interest movements. Due to the nature of our work and the backgrounds of our members—many of whom have departed from governmental intelligence—we uphold absolute anonymity. While I am the sole publicly known member, we affirm that no current participant collaborates with any government or corporate entity. We categorically reject conflicts of interest.

Membership is contingent upon rigorous evaluations. We do not admit individuals with criminal or civil records involving violence, substance abuse, or prescription drug overuse. Credibility assessments range from medical analyses to unannounced verification tests. This is not solely about legality but about shared vision and purpose. The Echelon Network Group exists to serve, never to undermine democratic or utilitarian principles, and will not provide services that act against these fundamental tenets, regardless of financial incentives.

While we do not disclose client identities, we accept requests from government entities, corporations, firms, and individual citizens. Pricing tiers are adjusted based on the scope and necessity of our services. Our commitment lies in national defense and the preservation of constitutional democracy. However, we only extend services to entities aligned with the principles of the UN Charter, the EU, UNESCO, and the Geneva Conventions.

We do not submit to any form of national audit, responding only to specialized intelligence and counterintelligence commissions under strict anonymity, except in my case as the founder. Our group is collectively egalitarian—without hierarchy

Name: Reserves 2050
Description:
Reserves 2050 is a grassroots organization that is privately funded, organized, and managed by Canadian citizens who desire to ensure that the service of the Citizen Soldiers, Sailors, and Aircrews are both enabled and managed responsibly.  We are the primary group advocating on behalf of Canada’s Reserves to the Government of Canada without prejudice or political affiliation.

Reserves 2050 seeks to work in partnership with the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) chain of command and senior leadership to ensure that reserve force members are provided opportunities to serve and to advocate for their care when required.  Reserves 2050 believes that a healthy and integrated Reserve Force within the CAF is vital to Canada’s defence needs.

A unique element of the Reserve Force is that it is geographically dispersed across Canada in contrast to the regular force which tends to be concentrated in a few key areas within the country.  As a result, elected officials at all levels across Canada often have reserve units as part of their constituency that may not be well understood.  Reserves 2050 specializes in education about the primary reserves to increase the value proposition of these units and Canadians who desire to serve the country.

Finally, thanks to its independent nature, Reserves 2050 can expertly act as an influence and advocate where public service and uniformed Canadians are not able to be involved.  This part of the advocacy mission ensures that the “citizen” aspect of the reserves is guarded in accordance with Canadian law.

Department of Defense (DoD) |  Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA)  |  Military Contractors  |  Active Duty and Dependents

Are you considering a transfer to Japan? 
+ Talk to your HR about access to medical care in Japan
+ Talk to your insurance company to ask about access to translators (in person, emergency, and for paperwork including claims), to determine what speciality clinics will accept US citizens near your potential base, and if there is a direct billing agreement with your insurance for the clinics that are in-network (**NOTE: SOFA status does not guarantee medical care for US citizens in Japan.)

SALWMAS provides integrated risk management solutions for institutions, corporations, and international organizations operating in Central Africa

Risk Awareness / Mine Risk Education (MRE) 

Explosive Clearance (mines, UXO, ERW) 

Victim Assistance 

Advocacy & Coordination 

Secure SALW Management (stockpile management, control & destruction) 

Our operational services include personnel and asset protection, security of sites and critical infrastructure, and operational surveillance, ensuring robust protection in complex and high-risk environments 

We also deliver specialized training programs for high-risk environments, including:

First aid & emergency response 

HEAT – Hostile Environment Awareness Training

Defensive & off-road driving 

Personal security & crisis management 

Beyond field operations, SALWMAS provides strategic advisory, security intelligence, and high-risk area monitoring, enabling decision-makers to anticipate threats, reduce exposure, and ensure business continuity in volatile contexts 

With deep regional expertise and strict adherence to international standards (IMAS, UN frameworks), SALWMAS supports its partners in securing operations and managing risks sustainably across Central Africa

Name: Helicopter Training Squadron 28 (HT-28)
Description:
HT-28's primary mission is to transition student aviators through basic and advanced rotary wing pilot training. Basic training introduces and develops student skills in helicopter flight maneuvers as well as training in visual navigation and tactics. Advanced training completes an intensive curriculum of basic and radio instruments, advanced tactics, and shipboard landings. The completion of this exacting training syllabus culminates in a highly trained and proficient all-weather aviator.

HT-28, along with its sister squadrons, HT-8 and HT-18, provides advanced helicopter flight instruction to all Navy, US Marine Corps, and US Coast Guard helicopter flight students as well as international students from several allied nations. Students who successfully complete the program earn the right to wear the coveted "Wings of Gold."

Name: Korbel Wargaming Club
Description:
The Korbel Wargaming Club (KWC) is a vibrant community of graduate students at the Josef Korbel School of International Studies, dedicated to exploring the complexities of strategic decision-making and conflict resolution through immersive wargaming experiences. Our club fosters an engaging environment where participants can delve into realistic military and geopolitical scenarios, enhancing their critical thinking, teamwork, and problem-solving skills.

KWC provides a platform for students from diverse backgrounds and academic disciplines to collaborate, simulate real-world challenges, and develop a deeper understanding of international relations. Through a variety of exercises held over multiple days or in single-day events, members navigate intricate tactical landscapes, assume the roles of decision-makers, and engage with the dynamic constraints faced by military and political leaders.

In addition to our core mission, KWC emphasizes mentorship and guidance, working closely with faculty and industry experts to enrich the learning experience. Whether you're a seasoned strategist or new to wargaming, KWC offers a welcoming space for growth, connection, and intellectual exploration. Join us as we engage in the art of strategy and diplomacy, preparing ourselves to tackle the global issues of tomorrow.

Name: 35th Infantry Regiment Association
Description:
The 35th Infantry Regiment Association is a non-profit organization dedicated to honoring the legacy, preserving the history, and fostering camaraderie among veterans and active members of the 35th Infantry Regiment, United States Army. Our association brings together individuals who have served or are currently serving with the 35th Infantry Regiment, promoting a strong sense of community and support among our members.

Our mission is threefold: to provide support and assistance to the members and families of the 35th Infantry Regiment, to educate the public about the Regiment's history and contributions, and to honor and remember the sacrifices made by the Regiment's veterans. We achieve these objectives through various initiatives, including annual reunions, memorial ceremonies, scholarship programs, and the publication of the Cacti Times.

Our membership is open to all individuals who now serve with or have previously served in the 35th Infantry Regiment, provided they were honorably discharged. Members can choose to receive either the electronic or printed version of the Cacti Times. We also welcome family members and friends of the Association to attend our reunions and receive the electronic Cacti Times.

One of our key initiatives is the Cacti Scholarship program. Thanks to the generosity of our Association members, we offer scholarships to eligible applicants every year. We encourage interested individuals to read the Association Scholarship Rules carefully and follow the application procedures.

We invite you to join our community and be part of the 35th Infantry Regiment Association. Together, we can honor the legacy of the Regiment, preserve its history, and support our fellow Cacti veterans and their families. Join us today and become part of the proud Cacti family!

Unit 666 is a highly specialized and unconventional military formation known for its elite tactics, rapid adaptability, and dominance in asymmetric warfare. Operating globally, the unit has played a pivotal role in counterinsurgency, special operations, and front-line combat, often in high-risk, high-intensity environments.

 Core Capabilities
 Unmatched Tactical Execution – Unit 666 operates with precision, efficiency, and ruthlessness, integrating elite infantry tactics, mechanized warfare, and electronic warfare into a seamless force.
 Innovative Combat Doctrine – Pioneered combined arms tactics, drone warfare deployment, and modern mechanized assault strategies, influencing global military doctrine.
 Special Operations Mastery – Conducts deep strikes, counterinsurgency, hostage rescue, and unconventional warfare across diverse battlefields.

 Combat Record
 Battle-Tested in Global Conflicts – From the Middle East, Africa, and Eastern Europe, Unit 666 has executed high-risk operations, influencing key battles and shifting frontlines.

 0% Casualty Operations – Known for its flawless execution, maintaining a record of success with minimal losses, even in hostile environments.

 PMC & SF Integration – Works alongside PMCs, airborne units, mechanized forces, and intelligence networks, ensuring maximum battlefield impact.

 Strategic Importance
 Force Multiplier – Unit 666 is not just a combat force; it is an elite operational think tank, influencing modern warfighting strategies.
 Decisive in Modern Conflicts – Its ability to deploy rapidly, adapt tactics on the fly, and integrate advanced technology makes it a lethal battlefield presence.

Name: Nunatak Teknik Tekstil İç ve Dış Ticaret A.Ş.
Description:
Firmamız, ticari hayatına 1994 yılında başta uyku tulumu ile başlayıp sonrasında ise ürün yelpazesini genişleterek birçok ürün üretimine başlamıştır. Üretimini yapamadığı ürünlerin de ithalatına başlamıştır. 
Şirketimiz ilk üretime başladığı günden itibaren kaliteli ürünleri üretmeyi kendisine ile edinmiştir. Sektörde yenilikçi düşünen firmamız, teknik tekstil alanında öncü firmalardan olup Türkiye'de ilk uyku tulumunu üreten firmadır.
Şirketimiz İstanbul'da bir satış mağazamız ve 64 bayimiz bulunmakla beraber, birçok kurum ve kuruluşa hizmet vermekteyiz. Bu kurum ve kuruluşlar arasında ( İç İşleri Bakanlığı ve bağlı birimleri, Milli Savunma Bakanlığı ve bağlı birimleri, Dağcılık Federasyonu, Gençlik ve Spor Bakanlığı ) bulunmaktadır. İhtiyaç duyulabilecek her türlü teknik malzemenin ve hayati idame malzemelerinin tedariğini sağlamaktadır.
Şirketimiz bünyesindeki seçkin ve deneyimli personel ile teknik tekstil alanındaki Ar-Ge çalışmalarına büyük önem vermekte olup yerli ve milli üretimin en büyük destekçilerindendir. 
İlk hedefi yerli ve milli üretim olan şirketimiz üretimini yapamadığı ürünlerinde tedariğini sağlamak maksadıyla çeşitli markaların distribütörlüğünü yapmaktadır. Distribütörlüğünü yaptığımız markalar tüm dünya kalitesi ve geniş ürün yelpazesi ile adından söz ettiren uluslararası firmalar olup, başta Cascade Designs grubu içinde bulunan MSR, Therm-a-Rest, Platypus, Sealline, Nikwax, Pieps, Marlow ve Black Diamond'dır.

Name: International Defence Esports Games (IDEG)
Description:
The International Defence Esports Games (IDEG) will help members of the Armed Forces develop cyber, digital and wider military skills. Initially open to service personnel, including reservists, IDEG will expand to eventually include cadets, veterans, civil servants and anyone working in the defence industry.   

Minister for Veterans and People, Al Carns DSO OBE MC MP, said:  
Esports will help attract, develop, and retain top cyber and digital talent, while fostering this government’s Plan for Change. Our people must now be as adept with code, cybersecurity and a games controller as they are with traditional combat skills.   

From drone operations to data analysis, modern defence and deterrence needs agile minds that can navigate both physical and digital battlegrounds.   

The International Defence Esports Games is an exciting initiative that will help foster exactly these skills in a fun, collaborative way, and overcome many traditional boundaries between our international partners.

The inaugural IDEG finals – where competitions would be held - will be organised in partnership with British Esports and a leading esports production company, and be held in the UK in late 2026.

Name: Military Transition Assistance Program
Description:

MTAP combines AI-driven career matching, cybersecurity training, and real-world mentorship to equip every participant with the skills, confidence, and network to thrive in today’s evolving workforce. Whether you’re pursuing government service, private-sector leadership, or entrepreneurship, MTAP delivers the tools, insights, and community support to make your next mission your most impactful yet.

Through strategic partnerships with federal agencies, defense contractors, universities, and Fortune 500 employers, MTAP helps bridge the gap between military excellence and civilian opportunity—while reinforcing our nation’s commitment to those who served.

Name: ArkenDyne
Description:
Arkendyne is a defense technology company modernizing unmanned operations for the realities of modern conflict.

We deliver mission software, secure data systems, and AI-driven tools that enhance unmanned and Counter-UAS operations—giving warfighters the ability to understand, decide, and act with speed inside contested environments.

Modern warfare demands large numbers of affordable drones that can be fielded fast, upgraded rapidly, and operated confidently in degraded electromagnetic conditions. Most unmanned systems today—commercial or military—lack the intelligence, resilience, and secure data handling required to survive and scale in this fight.

Arkendyne addresses this gap by upgrading the systems that already exist. We enhance existing UAVs by adding intelligence, resilience, and secure decision support—turning today’s aircraft into smarter, more adaptive mission assets without requiring new hardware or complex integrations.

Our platform includes:
• ArcSecure, a zero-trust operational data architecture that protects command and control and ensures mission continuity
• A Mission & Data Platform that unifies unmanned systems, mission data, and operator tools into a single decision environment
• AI-driven operational tools that help operators manage large formations of unmanned systems and act at speed—without losing human control

Arkendyne supports enhanced UAV operations at scale, Counter-UAS integration, training and readiness (JRTC, NTC), contested-environment operations, high-tempo conflict adaptation, and operational innovation.

Built for mission speed, operator-centered by design, and engineered for contested environments, Arkendyne delivers what the modern fight actually requires: a way to take what exists, make it intelligent, and field it at the speed of relevance.

Name: FasJus

FasJus is strategic philology.

We read and argue primary legal and regulatory texts from China, Latin America, MENA, Eurasia (including Russia), and sub-national Europe, into precise English with full institutional context.

Those statutes, decrees, standards, lists, designations, tenders, and technical annexes are, ultimately, what govern any strategic industry — including those in our portfolio.

Two sub-practices are housed in FasJus...:

... 1. Fas ([fæs])  is a recurring, subscription-based brief that identifies new law, places it in the issuing hierarchy, and states what changes, who is covered, and when obligations bite.

... 2. Jus ([d͡ʒʌs]) provides custom, SLA-bound, and on-call readings on a fixed schedule with decision matrices tied to the client’s facts. Capacity is reserved for “fire-edge” documents that flip a control or open or close a lane.

Both operate by referral only.

Name: Nato Mountain Warfare centre Of Excellence
Description:
The NATO Mountain Warfare Centre of Excellence (NATO MW COE) is a multi-nationally sponsored and accredited international military institution which offers recognized expertise and experience for the benefit of the Alliance and its member nations.

The NATO MW COE is not part of the NATO Command Structure (NCS), but forms part of the wider framework supporting the NATO Command Arrangements (NCA).

The mission of the NATO MW COE is to assist NATO member states, partners, other countries and international organizations in order to enhance mountain warfare capabilities through the following core areas:

The development of mountain warfare-specific doctrine and tactics,
- Concept development and experimentation,
- The mountain warfare lessons learned process,
- Education and training,
- Support of capability development.

The vision of the NATO MW COE is for it to be the hub for mountain warfare expertise in the NATO community.

Name: DAF Family Life Action Group
Description:

FLAG improves quality of life by listening to concerns from the field, leveraging partnerships when appropriate to action change, and increasing communication between DAF senior leaders and those most impacted by DAF policies.
The organization aims to realize a a force that ensures service-members and their families are comprehensively supported by DAF organizations, policies, and their local communities, ensuring mission readiness, resiliency, and retention of the force.
FLAG coordinates and equitably partners with organizations at all levels to execute their mission and does not endorse any non-federal entity.
Keep an eye on this page for any and all updates, including data calls, volunteers calls, and a mechanism to submit your concerns and challenges to DAF senior leaders!
Connect with us at www.dafflag.com or on other platforms here https://linktr.ee/dafflag

Een waarderende ruggensteun met als doel dat zij beter (of nog beter) om kunnen gaan met die in hun leven zo ingrijpende gebeurtenissen. Wij betrekken daar het thuisfront of nabestaanden bij, dat geeft een extra meerwaarde voor iedereen. 

Wij richten ons in het bijzonder op de volgende dienstverlenende (risicovolle) beroepen: brandweer, defensie, politie en ambulance personeel. Maar er is ook ruimte voor douane, boa's e.d.

In het algemeen, zij die zijn ingezet voor de samenleving in risicovolle beroepen en daardoor mentale of lichamelijke verwondingen hebben opgelopen. Een inzet al dan niet als beroepskracht of vrijwilliger evenals voormalige dienstplichtigen. En evenzo de partners en (jonge) kinderen van gesneuvelden/verongelukten uit de voornoemde groepen.
Volg ons voor meer informatie over onze activiteiten. Bekijk de website op http://nu-of-nooit.com

The Norwegian Defence staff, is the strategic level of the Norwegian Armed Forces and is in crisis management and governance context the military strategic level. They are tasked with providing the Chief Head of Defence with the necessary help in the operations and development of the Norwegian Armed Forces.

The personnel in the general staff do not represent any specific defense branch. The functions of the staff can be divided into three functional areas:

- Decision support which involves creating the best possible decision-making basis for current actions.
- Implementation of measures that involve realizing decisions taken by politicians and defense leadership."
- "Control support", meaning following up on measures implemented to ensure that the purpose and objectives are achieved.

Name: Charlottesville Defense Affairs Committee
Description:
The mission of the Chamber Defense Affairs Committee (DAC) is to foster economic vitality by further strengthening the alliance of relationships among the Central Virginia community, the regional defense & intelligence community, and our veterans.

Specifically, the DAC provides connections to a unique network of business, academic, and government participants that position the region for growth in the defense/intelligence enterprise. In fact, the DAC is the federal government’s preferred and primary interaction conduit with the community. 

For local defense sector contractors, veterans, local government, and service providers with interest in the defense and intelligence community and who desire visibility and access to local federal government agencies, the DAC is a unique conduit for the sharing of information and facilitating networking meetings so that the needs of the federal government, their employees, and veterans can be met by the local community. 

Five main DAC focus areas:

1. Community engagement: Advocacy and support for the defense industry in our region.
2. Government engagement: Advocate and support the needs of the local defense community with local, state and federal elected officials.
3. Education: Identify and facilitate partnerships between the local defense community and local K-12 and higher education institutions for research and education.
4. Research: In process
5. Veterans: Advocacy and support for veterans and their families.

Name: Raven Innovation Ltd
Description:
We are a small consultancy company passionate about helping business to connect successfully with government opportunities. 

By doing this for SMEs we support the aspiration to get brilliant innovative products to the UK military at the speed of relevance, ensuring those that serve on our behalf have the best possible support. 

By working with big business we can provide training, methods and support to generate innovation depts that can respond to change and experiment with novel technology. 

Ensuring that any company has a well-founded, evidenced and compelling Purpose, codified in a clear Strategy (not coffee table glossy brochure stuff) generates cohesive effort and drive for delivery. We Red Team, coach and test strategies for success.

Estimates suggest that we will see the same progress in technology in the next 10 years, that we saw in the previous 100 years! Is your organisation ready for this, are you preparing for a world your product will be exist in or for today's world, they are the same. We advise, coach and codify technology road maps relevant for Defence that are designed to balance portfolio risk against opportunity and provide decision advice for investment.

Most of the answers to these challenges in the context of your organisation sit within the minds of your teams. Generating the culture and values, supported by process, that enables you to get the best from your team is vital maximising opportunity with a balanced risk profile. We work with you to give you the tools and knowledge to supercharge your collective brains, in the process raising employees' sense of value and job enjoyment.

Even with the approaches above Government (UK or otherwise) can seem impenetrable and confusing - we have years of experience across Defence in innovation, R&D, S&T and in novel commercial approaches. We can connect, explain and help maximise your opportunities, where to focus, what to stop.

Helping you help the warfighter is our Purpose.

Name: 618th Air Operations Center (Tanker Airlift Control Center)

Description:
Turning Civilian Ingenuity into Military Excellence: Bridging Innovation for National Defense

TechGrid serves as an expansive collaborative space, enabling the Navy to engage in direct partnerships with industry, academia, government and fellow military branches, breaking free from the limitations of traditional military bases.

It’s primary objective is to foster connections between small businesses, industry, academia, and government, fostering a more cohesive innovation ecosystem that addresses the strategic priorities of our Command.

Through our regional alliances, we aim to facilitate innovation, experimentation, and rapid procurement, driving forward novel concepts and emerging technologies to empower our Warfighters with new capabilities.

Name: Lethal Minds Journal
Description:
The Lethal Minds Journal is a veteran created art, culture, and foreign and military affairs media publication.

The military community on social media did what all great Soldiers, Sailors, Airmen, and Marines do when given a platform: They spread knowledge. They work to better the community. Hundreds of servicemembers and veterans are out there creating content to educate, inform, and to create dialogue. Veterans and servicemembers are creating art and works of literature, moving into fields that have been waiting for veteran voices for a long time.

To me, the Lethal Minds Journal is a living gallery of the veteran and active military communities. The goal of the Journal is to gather together all the content spread across dozens of platforms and present it in a clear, concise, and easily accessible manner.

We’ve had huge support from the mil community, great submissions from hundreds of contributors, and volunteer support from veterans who dedicated personal time to building a digital framework and editing submissions. We are excited for you to read our first edition, and we hope the Journal grows to be something that everyone; creators, contributors, and readers, can be proud of.

Be informed, be prepared, be lethal.

They create a shared understanding for operating forces of the priorities and processes at the executive levels, and provide important, relevant perspectives from the front lines to inform enterprise-level decisions, manage risk, and.  The alumni network flattens communication across large organizations and proliferates new technologies, best practices, and lessons learned to maintain the U.S. Army's leading position in the DoD and among Partners and Allies. Name: Delaware Air National Guard Recruiting and Retention
Description:
Company Description
 
The 166th Airlift Wing, part of the Delaware Air National Guard, is a versatile unit capable of mobilizing and deploying C-130H Hercules aircraft and personnel globally to address both peacetime and wartime situations. The Wing provides essential airdrop services for troops, cargo, and passengers, and includes aeromedical evacuation and civil engineering functions. Under the command of the Governor of Delaware, the 166th Airlift Wing also contributes to state humanitarian missions to protect life and property and maintain public safety and order.

Role Description

Are you ready to serve your community and country while gaining valuable skills and benefits? The Delaware Air National Guard is actively recruiting for a variety of part-time positions in fields such as aviation, cybersecurity, medical, engineering, logistics, and more. As a member of the Delaware Air National Guard, you’ll receive hands-on training, leadership development, and opportunities to advance your career—all while serving part-time.

Qualifications & Requirements

	•	Age: 17-42 years old (some positions may have specific age requirements)
	•	Education: High school diploma or GED (some roles may require additional education or certifications)
	•	Citizenship: U.S. citizen or legal permanent resident
	•	Physical Fitness: Must meet military fitness standards
	•	Background: Ability to pass a background check and medical evaluation
	•	Commitment: Serve part-time (typically one weekend a month and two weeks a year)
	•	No Prior Experience Required – We provide all necessary training

Benefits of Joining
	•	Education Assistance: Tuition assistance and the GI Bill for college
	•	Career Training: Gain experience in high-demand fields
	•	Financial Incentives: Competitive pay, bonuses, and retirement benefits
	•	Healthcare & Insurance: Low-cost healthcare and life insurance options
	•	Networking & Leadership: Build professional skills and connections for your future

Name: Urban Warfare Institute
Description:
The Urban Warfare Institute is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to advancing the study of urban environments, urban operations, and urban warfare. Our research spans the entire conflict spectrum and provides valuable insights to a diverse range of stakeholders.

Throughout history, wars have been waged in urban areas and this trend seems likely to continue given the world is becoming increasingly more urban. Recent wars or battles in Iraq, Syria, Ukraine, and Gaza have illustrated this. Yet militaries around the world are repeatedly unprepared for urban operations despite increasingly being pulled into them for a wide span of operations from humanitarian relief to combat.

As urban areas become an increasing site of political instability, natural disasters, and conflict, a robust public understanding of the complexity of urban operations and urban warfare is essential to preparing for the future. Our activities focus on conducting research, educational activities, and advising.

Research. The Urban Warfare Institute conducts research to produce articles, reports, books, podcasts, multi-media, and other products related to urban operations. This includes research to understand urban areas as well as research to understand the wide range of military and civil operations. Our research ranges from understanding urban areas, what generates conflict in urban environments, how to conduct urban operations most effectively to include how reduce civilian harm, how to improve de-escalation tactics for large crowds and riots, and to reduce human suffering. 

Educate. The Urban Warfare Institute conducts educational activities to increase the global understanding of all topics relating to urban operations to all stakeholders including political leaders, militaries, students of all ages, and general populations. The institute’s educational activities include teaching courses and individual classes, providing lectures, panels, podcasts, expert testimony, and more.

Name: United States Army Intelligence School
Description:
The United States Army Intelligence School, also known as USAIS, is an educational institution within the U.S. Army that provides training and education in intelligence disciplines. It is responsible for training and developing military personnel in various intelligence fields to support the intelligence needs of the U.S. Army and the broader U.S. Department of Defense.

The USAIS offers a range of programs focused on intelligence gathering, analysis, and dissemination. These programs cover subjects such as signals intelligence, human intelligence, geospatial intelligence, counterintelligence, and cyber intelligence. The curriculum is among the most demanding in the department of defense and designed to equip military personnel with the knowledge and skills necessary to collect, process, analyze, and distribute intelligence information effectively.

The school is located at Fort Huachuca in Arizona, USA. It provides a comprehensive learning environment, including classrooms, training facilities, and simulation resources, to facilitate the education and training of Army intelligence professionals.

The USAIS plays a vital role in preparing Army personnel for intelligence operations and supporting the overall mission readiness of the U.S. Army. It helps to ensure that intelligence personnel are equipped with the necessary expertise to contribute to the success of military operations, threat assessments, and decision-making processes.

Name: Groupement de Recrutement de la Légion étrangère (GRLE)
Description:
La Légion étrangère est une unité militaire d'élite de l'armée de terre française, renommée pour son esprit de corps, sa discipline et son engagement au service de la France. Créée en 1831, elle accueille des volontaires de toutes nationalités, dont des français, unis par des valeurs communes de fraternité, d'honneur et de fidélité.

Le groupement de recrutement de la Légion étrangère (GRLE) est l'organisme officiel chargé du recrutement, de la sélection et de l'accompagnement des candidats souhaitant intégrer la Légion. Basé à Fontenay-sous-Bois, le GRLE gère l'ensemble du processus de recrutement, depuis la première prise de contact jusqu'à l'intégration des nouveaux légionnaires.

La plateforme www.legion-recrute.com constitue le portail officiel d'information et de candidature, offrant aux postulants :

    Des informations détaillées sur les conditions d'engagement
    Des outils de préparation aux tests de sélection
    Des témoignages et retours d'expérience de légionnaires

Le GRLE s'engage à maintenir des standards d'excellence en matière de recrutement militaire, avec un processus transparent, exigeant et respectueux des valeurs de la Légion étrangère.

#LégionÉtrangère #ServiceMilitaire #Engagement #France

Name: Korea National Defense University
Description:
Korea National Defense University was established in 1955 as the National War College under the Ministry of National Defense. The objectives were three-fold: First, the integrated operation of national defense covering all the fields of politics, economy, military, and concept, etc. Second, to attain deep understanding and cooperation between the military and the civilian sector. At the same time, it addresses rising global challenges, including energy and economic security, human security, and the intricate intersections of technology, climate change, and international stability. This comprehensive approach reflects the institute’s dedication to fostering informed dialogue and actionable solutions to pressing international issues.

Through its extensive networks with scholars, policymakers, and international organizations, IIRI organizes high-impact conferences, workshops, and collaborative research initiatives that shape global policy discussions. Anchored in a legacy of excellence, the institute strives to enhance its role as a world-class research institution, offering thought leadership and practical strategies to navigate the complexities of today’s interconnected world.

Name: Prylit || Missile and Drone Detection App Stack
Description:
Cross-platform drone and cruise missile detection app stack.

Prylit Citizen - Air-raid alert and data gathering app for civilians - https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=plus.mk.prylit - (Done, Released).
Once installed and launched, the app starts sending anonymous data that includes noise level and approximate location (300m). The data is sent to secure Google servers. After that, the data is analyzed by special algorithms and the possible trajectory of the missile is calculated. Next, users who are inside this trajectory and using this app at the moment will receive an alert about the impending danger. The more people install the app, the more data is received and analyzed, and the more people will be warned of a possible missile approach.

Prylit Swarm - Custom hardware implementation of proprietary detection algorithm with 5G and sound mirror  - (Design and Architecture Stage).

Prylit Commander - Web application with cruise missile tracking visualization for DoD, MoD- https://prylit.app/ - (Done, Released).
Dots are signals received either from Prylit Citizen crowd-sourced application or from Prylit Swarm noise registration spots. Red area with directional arrow is the alert activation area, where sound and visual alarm are shown to user keeps the app active.

Next steps:

1. Creating Prylit Swarm based on Prylit, moving data gathering from crowd-source to custom hardware with AI and ML processing, converting quantity to quality of data overall.
2. UAV detection mobile app. Orlan type UAV recognition with RTL.SDR USB dongle to catch close frequencies spikes ~920 kHz.

Consider investing in project directly or via main crowd-funding platforms. This is not investing or supporting into yet another "useful" *etwas*24.de, but a real opportunity to make an impact for humanity's safety for tens of years to come. 

https://www.patreon.com/prylit
https://www.gofundme.com/f/prylit

Name: SITM - International Mountain Troops SUMMIT
Description:
The International Mountain Troops Summit (SITM) is the meeting place for armies from all over the world who operate in extreme conditions, in mountainous environments and in freezing temperatures. It will be held in Grenoble on 10 and 11 February 2027.

Two days of exhibition, talks and workshops, debates and high-level meetings dedicated to showcasing the latest technical and technological developments, best practice in training and opportunities for international cooperation, to meet future challenges and guarantee security in these sensitive regions of the world. More than 1,000 participants are expected to attend, including manufacturers, start-ups, government representatives, military experts and academics.

As geopolitical dynamics and the climate change, technologies are evolving to support the success of military operations around the challenges of complex logistics, restricted mobility, difficult communications and soldier protection. Armies have to think ahead when it comes to future combat in mountainous terrain and extreme-cold zones.

Name: Kuntum Ilham
Description:
Kuntum Ilham is an independent, Malaysia-based platform dedicated to feminist peacebuilding, historical memory, disarmament, and collective healing. Rooted in Southeast Asia and connected to broader global movements, we engage in storytelling, advocacy, and reflection to reimagine just, peaceful, and liberated futures. Our work centers on creating intentional spaces for dialogue, memory work, and resistance, where personal and political histories are honored and where community-based healing can take root.

We believe that feminist approaches to peace and security must go beyond policy frameworks to include lived experiences, historical truths, and collective narratives. Through written reflections, policy input, and creative collaborations, we document and amplify both the visible and invisible aspects of feminist resistance — from submissions to peace processes and community zines, to stories of disarmament grief and intergenerational healing.

Kuntum Ilham offers speaking and facilitation services for talks, panels, and community gatherings, as well as consultation on national, regional, and global policies related to Women, Peace & Security (UNSCR1325), Youth, Peace & Security (UNSCR2250), transitional justice, and feminist organizing in Southeast Asia. We also engage in writing and creative partnerships with individuals and platforms that share our values, co-creating essays, zines, and visual narratives rooted in justice, care, and remembrance.

As part of a growing ecosystem of peacebuilders, artists, and advocates, we invite collaboration and co-resistance. Kuntum Ilham is not just a platform, but a living archive — a quiet, reflective space where memory becomes a political act and storytelling is part of the healing process.

Contact us at contact@kuntumilham.org to collaborate or learn more about how we can work together toward feminist peace and healing.

Name: JOINT LOGISTICS SUPPORT GROUP BRUNSSUM

The coordination of multinational logistic requirements across Land, Sea and Air domains is complex.  JLSGBS HQ is an essential element for the projection of forces into a theatre, essential for a swift delivery of forces into an area of tension and their sustainment on the ground.
JLSGBS HQ provides a capability to coordinate logistic operations, enabling effective, efficient and optimal support to the troops in theatre.
When deployed, the HQ is one of the first elements to enter a crisis and one of the last elements to leave.

Guided by the vision of "Restoring Our Service Wounded," we stand as a beacon of empathy, understanding, and transformative support for those who have borne the scars of MST. We are driven by a steadfast commitment to ushering in transformative change for women veterans. Our mission is rooted in empathy, understanding, and the resolute belief that every survivor deserves a path to healing, empowerment, and a brighter future.
Our Vision: We envision a world where the echoes of two hearts beating as one drown out the silence of suffering. We want to build a future where the hearts and minds of our service-wounded women veterans are not just healed but restored to a state of strength, empowerment, and renewed purpose. Operation Two Hearts and Minds is more than an initiative; it's a collective pledge to create an inclusive culture that supports, uplifts, and embraces the unique journey of women who have experienced MST.

Name: The Particle Accelerator
Description:
Particle accelerators smash atoms together in a large, high speed environment with the intention of creating an entirely new element.  This scientific practice inspired me many years ago when I was an artist in the entertainment field.  Cut to now, when I am a security professional, I am still compelled by this kernel of thought: what if we had a physical space that brought a wide swath of visionaries together from the security and the arts communities in this unique, secure space to create something new that could change the world? What level of progress could we achieve by trying a new way to approach old and destructive social problems?  
This is what we do:
Create solutions for complicated policy issues, civil-social problems, and national/international security concerns.
Create more engaging media to educate the public on political and security agendas, issues, developments, etc.
Help develop compassion and influence a more balanced view-point in our governmental leadership.
Develop achievable schedules, bound in a sacrosanct commitment, for the implementation of these solutions. 
Have a space where the two groups are motivated and inspired by each other while independently working.

Name: Offiziersgesellschaft Thun
Description:
Die Offiziersgesellschaft Thun wurde 1863 gegründet und blickt auf eine lange Tradition militärischer Kameradschaft und Ausbildung zurück.
Seit über 150 Jahren ist sie ein zentraler Treffpunkt für Offiziere im Berner Oberland und darüber hinaus. Die OG Thun pflegt nicht nur die Schiesskunst und den sportlichen Wettkampf, sondern auch die gelebte Gemeinschaft unter Offizieren, national wie international.

Ein Höhepunkt im Vereinsjahr ist das internationale OG Thun Wettschiessen im Juni, das im Schnitt rund 100 Teilnehmer aus sieben Nationen vereint. Darüber hinaus organisiert die Gesellschaft regelmässig Schiessanlässe, technische Kurse, gesellschaftliche Treffen und im September als weiteren Höhepunkt den jährlichen OG-Ball, der Offiziere, Mitglieder und Gäste zusammenführt.

Die OG Thun pflegt den Austausch und die Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Blaulichtorganisationen, Behörden und der Bevölkerung, vermittelt Wissen über militärische Ausbildung und verbindet Tradition, sportlichen Wettkampf und gesellschaftliches Engagement.

Durch diese Aktivitäten werden nicht nur militärische Fähigkeiten gestärkt, sondern auch Werte wie Kameradschaft, Disziplin und Verantwortung gelebt. Die OG Thun bleibt so ein lebendiger Teil der Schweizer Offizierskultur und ein Bindeglied zwischen Militär, Zivilgesellschaft und internationalen Partnern.

Wir freuen uns Sie an einem der Anlässe der Offiziersgesellschaft Thun begrüssen zu dürfen!

Ayant pour vocation la formation des futurs cadres, managers et experts de la sphère publique, l’ISMaPP, au travers de la Chaire, veut relier les Savoirs et les enseignements dispensés aux étudiants à des activités de recherche et de publication. 

Les élèves managers publics vont ainsi pouvoir participer à des travaux de réflexion et à des séminaires scientifiques organisés avec l’Institut de la Décentralisation, think-tank spécialisé dans l’organisation des pouvoirs publics et à leur application territoriale.

Cette Chaire se doit de faire émerger et refléter les innovations administratives et managériales du Nouveau Management Public en valorisant les études et travaux de recherches liés aux processus, pratiques et techniques de la gouvernance publique.

Avec l’édification de cette Chaire, l’ISMaPP donne aux études en Management public une portée scientifique de premier plan en fédérant d’autres disciplines académiques telles que la science administrative, la science politique et la gestion publique tant au niveau français, européen qu’international.

Name: UNOR Aquitaine - Conseil de région
Description:
Le Conseil de région Aquitaine de l’UNOR assure, sur le territoire aquitain (Gironde, Landes, Lot et Garonne, Dordogne et Pyrénées atlantiques), la représentation de l’Union nationale des officiers de réserve et des organisations de réservistes (UNOR).
L’UNOR, association reconnue d’utilité publique, fondée par Raymond Poincaré en 1922, fédère les associations territoriales interarmées (AOR) et coordonne les actions communes des associations nationales représentatives d’armées (Terre, Air, Marine) et de services (https://unor-reserves.fr/).
Les associations qui sont représentées au sein du Conseil de région Aquitaine.
Les sept associations territoriale interarmées :
- L’Association des Officiers et Cadres de Réserve de Bordeaux Gironde (AOCRBG), Gironde,
- L’Association des Officiers de Réserve de Libourne (AOR Libourne), Gironde,
- L’Association des Cadres de Réserve des landes, Landes
- L’Association des Officiers de Réserve du Lot et Garonne, Lot et Garonne
- L’Association des Cadres et Officiers de Reserve des Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Bayonne (ACOR 64),
- L’Amicale des Officiers de Réserve des Pyrénées-Atlantiques (AOR PA), Pau.
Les entités territoriales des associations nationales d’armée :
- Sections Aquitaine et Gascogne de l’Association des Officiers de Réserve de la Marine Nationale (ACORAM)
- Secteurs de l’Association Nationale des Officiers de Réserve de l’Armée de l’Air et de l’Espace (ANORAAE).
Invite au Conseil de région : Amicale du 144e Régiment d’Infanterie.

Name: 416th Civil Affairs Battalion (Airborne)
Description:
Constituted 24 February 1949 in the Organized Reserve Corps as the 416th Military Government Company. Activated 10 March 1949 at Norristown, Pennsylvania. Organized Reserve Corps redesignated 9 July 1952 as the Army Reserve. 

Reorganized and redesignated 20 August 1956 as the 416th Civil Affairs and Military Government Company. Redesignated 1 December 1959 as the 416th Civil Affairs Company. Reorganized and redesignated 16 September 1992 as Headquarters and Headquarters Detachment, 416th Civil Affairs Battalion.

Ordered into active military service 1 December 2003 at Norristown, Pennsylvania; released from active military service 29 November 2004 and reverted to reserve status. Location changed 16 September 2006 to San Diego, California. Company A ordered into active military service 16 August 2008 at San Diego, California; released from active military service 19 September 2009 and reverted to reserve status. Company B ordered into active military service 23 June 2010 at San Diego, California; released from active military service 27 July 2011 and reverted to reserve status.

Name: Business Club Luchtmobiel
Description:
Luchtmobiel bloed door je aderen? Dan hoor je bij ons.

De Business Club Luchtmobiel (BCL) is opgericht voor (oud-)leden van de 11 Luchtmobiele Brigade die hun ervaring, mentaliteit en kameraadschap meenemen in het bedrijfsleven.
Of je nu een eigen bedrijf runt of eindverantwoordelijk bent binnen een organisatie: als je bij de brigade hebt gediend, hoor je erbij.

Wij zijn geen netwerkclub waar je visitekaartjes komt uitdelen. Wij zijn een broederschap. Gebouwd op gedeelde waarden, gevormd in het stof van het oefenterrein en gesmeed onder druk.

Binnen de BCL brengen we ondernemers en leiders samen die de taal van de brigade spreken. We delen kennis, openen deuren voor elkaar, helpen veteranen hun plek te vinden en slaan bruggen tussen defensie en het bedrijfsleven. We organiseren bijeenkomsten, projecten en initiatieven waar je wat aan hebt, strategisch, zakelijk én menselijk.

Want wie ooit luchtmobiel was, blijft dat voor altijd. De kracht, het moreel, de daadkracht en kameraadschap, dat verdwijnt niet met het inleveren van je uitrusting. Sterker nog: het vormt de basis voor leiderschap buiten de poort.

Sluit je aan. Laat zien wie je bent. En bouw mee aan een club met impact.

Name: Army Reserve Aviation Command (ARAC)
Description:
Mission
The Army Reserve Aviation Command (ARAC) based in Fort Knox, Kentucky, provides command and control for all Army Reserve aviation.

The ARAC provides air traffic services, airfield management, aeromedical evacuation, combat aviation brigade reinforcement, theater aviation support and coordination of aviation staging and onward movement in order to support Army or joint operations.

With more than 4,400 Soldiers, approximately 600 civilians and 230 aircraft in 12 states, the Army Reserve Aviation Command is one-of-a-kind, providing command and control for all Army Reserve aviation, air assault, air movement, aeromedical evacuation, and inter- and intra-theater transportation.

Our rotary-wing aircraft includes UH-60s, HH-60s and CH-47s, while C-12s and UC-35s make up our fixed-wing.

Additionally, the command supports all FEMA regions within the United States to support emergency response.

Vision
A trained and ready Aviation Command composed of professional and resilient Soldiers and civilians with skills and knowledge to execute both domestic and theater aviation support and coordination of aviation staging and onward movement in order to support Army or joint operations.

Name: Brackish Experts
Description:
At Brackish Consulting we pride ourselves in facilitating teams with operational requirements to work together, but need assistance in understanding each other’s goals, priorities, culture, or even language.  Throughout our careers, we have found that our diverse experiences working across government agencies, industry, and academia have created a valuable perspective that is often critical to success when two or more of those sectors are partnered together.  Whether it is executing a private-sector contract for a government client or assisting an industry client in understanding government processes, organizations, or operational needs, we have the right insights to help the team succeed. 

Brackish water is where fresh and seawater blend to create a unique environment often habitable by life from both ecosystems.  Similarly, Brackish Experts leverage their diverse professional experience to provide perspectives critical to success in cross-sector ventures. Inside government, the structure, policy, processes, lexicon, acronyms, and culture are vastly different and often unrecognizable to members of another service or agency. Things are no different in industry or academia. Our team provides the perspective of experts who have worked throughout all three sectors: serving at the highest levels of government from the halls of the Pentagon and the State Department to Capitol Hill and the White House, in small and large for-profit and not-for-profit businesses, and in both military and civilian schools and universities. Whatever the challenge, we will build the best team with the right blend of experience to make your endeavor successful.

Name: Team 4 Ukraine z.s.
Description:
The Team4Ukraine (T4U) is volunteer-based, non-governmental organization, comprised of highly skilled experts as well as dedicated citizens, formed in 2014.

Originally, the organisation's aim was to deepen relations between Czech republic and Ukraine, to raise awareness about challenges Ukraine faced at the time. Therefore T4U has always striven for bilateral exchange of knowledge and expertise and for capacity building of several civil society organizations and public administration services, especially in the area of security, cybersecurity and criminology. 

Russian full scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 has proved to be transformative even for our organisation, since the T4U’s activities have grown in scale, size and expertise, currently dividing our efforts in 5 pillars: 

1. Monitoring and analysis: Our team members actively participate in monitoring and analysis of war crimes against the civilians committed by the RF, including damage to the cultural heritage and environment. 

2. Exchange of knowledge and experience between the Czech Republic and the UA in the field of defence and security: including, but not limited to training of medical first aid in tactical situations. 

3. Specific and direct humanitarian assistance to civilians: We supply core relief items to orphanages and provide medical aid shipments reflecting the urgent needs of several health-care facilities. We also support civilians in the frontline areas with humanitarian aid shipments. 

4. Specific and direct assistance to the defenders of Ukraine: We support selected units of the Armed Forces of Ukraine with on-demand supplies and equipment, strengthening their efficiency in fulfilling their missions and reassuring their progress towards compatibility with the operational standards of EU and NATO forces.

5. Cyber security and information warfare: We monitor, analyse and report on methods and strategies of hybrid warfare, used by Russia agains the collective West.

Name: Special Operations Campaign Artistry Program
Description:
What is Red Teaming?  Answer:  A function that provides commanders a capability to fully explore alternatives in plans, operations, concepts, organizations and capabilities in the context of the operational environment (OE) and from the perspectives of partners, adversaries and others.
Examples:
Challenging planning assumptions
Assisting in defining the problem and end state
Identifying friendly and enemy vulnerabilities
Identifying assessment measures.


Why Red Teaming?  People and organizations court failure in predictable ways.  Especially among groups with strong team spirit, high cohesiveness, limited contact with outsiders and outside ideas, high stress, and directive leadership.  Example – Groupthink and the Bay of Pigs incident.


What Red Teaming Does:  RT supports Design, Planning, and Decision Making.
Design – Understanding, visualizing, and determining “what is”
Planning and Decision Making – Describing, directing, and determining “what’s next”
By asking…”What If?”

Our enduring commitment since our inception is to showcase and spread awareness about the remarkable capabilities of Polish industry in serving our uniformed services, including the military and other security sectors.

Our activities include:

1. Hosting trade fairs: We bring together our members to exhibit their productive achievements, services, and technologies at specialised trade fairs across Poland. These events focus on uniform and military applications, offering a platform for our members to shine.

2. Fostering continual learning: We regularly organise training sessions in collaboration with designated institutions. These sessions cover a range of topics, from public order procurement and product codifications to industrial safety certification processes, ensuring our members stay at the forefront of industry standards and practices.

3. Celebrating innovation with esteemed recognition: In past editions, our "Leader of National Security" awards gala was honoured with the patronage of Poland's President and the Head of the National Security Bureau. This prestigious acknowledgment has been a testament to the significance and impact of our awards, showcasing the best in innovative and modern products, services, and technologies with potential applications in collective safety.

4. Building international bridges: we organise foreign missions for our members and associated companies. These missions are pivotal in fostering international relationships and exploring new opportunities, expanding our members' horizons beyond Polish borders.

Together, we strive to elevate Poland's profile in the realm of security and defence, championing innovation and excellence.

Name: Heidi Duty LLC
Description:
Free Caregiver Course for Military Families:

https://heididutytime.com/

#heididutytime #EvergreenState

Connect with me to learn more about how you can get paid as a family Caregiver, especially if you're a Military family.

I am a Washington State Nurse Assistant/Home Care Aide/Notary Public/SEIU 775 member with 501(c)(3) status on TIN 82-4129924.

One objective is to educate Caregivers on how to unload and safely store firearms to prevent Veteran death by suicide.  70% of Veteran deaths by suicide are committed with their own firearms.  While I support our Bill of Rights, we all see the necessity of recoupling responsibility with rights. It's far better to get Veterans out to a legal outdoor gun range than to ignore suicidal ideation reinforced by social isolation and media over consumption.

20 Veteran suicides per day is 20 too many. Men Veterans in rural areas are particularly vulnerable to death by suicide with firearm, and sadly women Veterans die by suicide from other means (Veterans Administration, 2023).

Black Veteran death by suicide exceeds death by combat.  Black Veterans are often assumed not to be patriotic, because of their own beliefs, preferences, and culture.  This must stop. Denying their Hero status because they don't fit an ideological mold may lead to social isolation, which is one cause of death by suicide (Veterans Administration, 2023).

LGBTQIA+ Veteran death by suicide is not well quantified, but one death by suicide is too many. https://www.patientcare.va.gov/LGBT/ is a recent resource for LGBTQIA+ Veterans (Veterans Administration, 2023). 

We must love ourselves and each other to life, and end this culture of death.

Donate: https://heididuty.com/about-us/

Thank you for visiting me.  Please contact me and I will help you in any way I can.

Name: Naval Small Craft Instruction and Technical Training School (NAVSCIATTS)
Description:
NAVSCIATTS trains and educates strategic allied and partner Security Force Professionals across the tactical, operational, and strategic spectrums through in-residence and Mobile Training Team Courses of Instruction. To date, almost 15,000 students from 132 Partner Nations have trained with this Naval Special Warfare command.

NAVSCIATTS Courses of Instruction include operations, repairs, sustainment of craft (both riverine and littoral), communications, weapons, small unit tactics, UAS, intel fusion operations, as well as Partner Nation instructor development. While the name implies, "naval" and "small craft," many of the current courses apply to any security force.

NAVSCIATTS has a proven methodology for Building Partner Capacity. We "Train the Trainers" using a combination of in-resident and mobile training team engagements. The mobile training team engagements are used as follow-on to in-resident training and utilize the NAVSCIATTS-trained Partner Nation trainers as the Instructor Cadre. This provides the ability for our Partner Nation trainers to establish credibility as Subject Matter Experts and allows NAVSCIATTS to critique and provide continued mentorship. 

One of, if not the primary benefit of training with NAVSCIATTS, is relationship-building. Many of our alumni remain in contact with the staff regarding professional matters, such as evolving technologies or ever-present training opportunities. Offering the ability to form professional and personal bonds that reach across oceans and continents, NAVSCIATTS has allowed for the establishment of networks that have aided in counterterrorism, counternarcotic, and counter human trafficking operations in a global environment.

Name: PMO 555 Shipyard Infrastructure Optimization Program (SIOP)
Description:
The Shipyard Infrastructure Optimization Program (SIOP) mission is to execute the Navy’s investment to reconfigure, modernize and optimize our four aging naval shipyards into new modern facilities that will serve our Nation into the future.
In September 2021, the Department of the Navy updated SIOP’s reporting relationships to ensure disciplined and synchronized execution of the multiple major construction efforts managed within SIOP and designated Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command as the single, responsible executive lead for execution of SIOP and established the Program Executive Office Industrial Infrastructure (PEO II) to oversee the Program Management Office-555 (PMO-555) SIOP.
The Navy’s four public shipyards – Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY), Portsmouth Naval Shipyard (PNSY), Puget Sound Naval Shipyard and Intermediate Maintenance Facility (PSNS & IMF), and Pearl Harbor Naval Shipyard and Intermediate Maintenance Facility (PHNS & IMF) – are essential elements of our national defense.
The Navy's four public shipyards need substantial recapitalization and reconfiguration in order to improve the timely return of ships and submarines to the fleet following maintenance and modernization.
Completing ship and submarine maintenance availabilities on time and getting them back to the fleet is vital to ensuring the United States maintains its maritime dominance. 
The Navy is investing heavily in shipyard infrastructure. SIOP will construct and recapitalize dry docks, recapitalize and reconfigure infrastructure, and modernize industrial plant equipment.

Name: Naval Aviation Enterprise
Description:
The NAE is a military organization but it is not one command. It is the coming together of all Naval Aviation commands—and their partners— committed to active participation to drive readiness for the high end-fight. The NAE is cross-functional, transparent, accountable and committed to active participation to facilitate and align resources and processes to improve Naval Aviation readiness levels. 

The NAE ensures the effective management of critical readiness stakeholders across Naval Aviation, directly supporting the successful delivery of combat-ready forces while advancing future warfighting capabilities at the best possible cost.

NAE leadership is committed to these principles that act as a guide for everyday decisions:

Concentrate efforts on producing required readiness while sustaining Fleet wholeness and improving efficiency.

Exercise a bias for action.

Drive systemically cross-functional, cross-command practices.

Apply disciplined, process-driven, analytic methodologies.

Understand the Single Fleet Driven Metric: Naval Aviation forces ready for tasking in the numbers required to meet Navy and Marine Corps readiness and warfighting requirements.

Use consistent, integrated and hierarchical metrics focused on Fleet readiness and sustainment.

Ensure full and consistent transparency of data, information and activities.

Establish and maintain accountability for actions and results.

Commit to active participation.

Enterprise efforts are guided by the NAE Charter, which defines supported/supporting relationships within the Enterprise and emphasizes the NAE's focus on generating warfighting readiness.

Name: Hornet
Description:
Hornet is India’s first fully indigenous crypto and darkweb forensics platform, empowering both law enforcement and crypto businesses. Trusted by agencies like the Enforcement Directorate, Telangana Police, and West Bengal Police, Hornet offers real-time blockchain tracing, darkweb monitoring, and deep analytics.
Along with this, Hornet also works alongside various Crypto Businesses to ensure they are in full compliance with existing rules and regulations.

 

In acknowledgment of its contribution to the tech field, Hornet was privileged to take home the top prize at the STPI CHUNAUTI 5.0, a recognition that further boosted their credibility and subsequently helped raise pre-seed funds from Pontaq VC, an international cross-border Venture Capital firm. 

Hornet's scientific prowess was further validated when it won the NIDHI PRAYAS Grant given by the Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), Kolkata. This was awarded under the Department of Science and Technology of the Government of India, underscoring the organization's depth in scientific and technological expertise.

The initiative's uniqueness was further kid-gloved when it was presented with the Best Innovation award by the Lt. Governor of Delhi Shri Vinai Saxena. Subsequently, the outfit emerged as the Best Startup at ABP-INFOCOM 2023, a testament to its status as a leading innovator in its sphere. 

The global tech community also recognized its merits when Hornet was selected for a fully sponsored visit to one of the world's largest entrepreneurial conferences, TiECon, in 2024, in San Francisco, USA, Crypto Expo, Dubai, 2025.

Last but not least, Hornet was incubated at ISI, Kolkata – among India's premier institutions – rendering it the formidable platform it is today. 
Hornet recently raised seed round from Artha Ventures, solidifying their leadership globally.

Name: MKKN LLC
Description:
MKKN is the Saudi independent leader mastering production and services in the ammunitions field according to NATO standards.

MKKN’s capabilities span in manufacturing and development of aerial general purpose systems, medium and large artillery ammunitions.

The operations are performed in the production plant located in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where the state of the art numerical control machines have been installed.

Equipment at MKKN’s facilities includes horizontal and vertical forges, thermal induction heat treatments, quenching and annealing island, automated welding station, multi-axes numerical control machines, chemical treatment and coating island, assembly line and storage systems.

All devices and their parts are inspected and tested in MKKN laboratories to guarantee highest quality standards.

Equipment at MKKN’s laboratories includes tensile testing machine, hardness testing machine, XRF spectrometer, impact testing machine, high-pressure hydro testing station, 3D measuring machine, including devices for metallographic and coating quality control.

MKKN is constantly pursuing new ways to provide Clients with a competitive edge in land and air ammunitions, and offer precise and effective solutions against enemy threats.

Name: Naval Intelligence Professionals (NIP)
Description:
Founded in 1985, NIP is a nonprofit organization incorporated to enhance awareness of the mission and vital functions of the naval intelligence community, as well as to foster camaraderie among members of NIP.

The goal of the Naval Intelligence Professionals (NIP) is to further the knowledge of the art of maritime intelligence, and to provide a vehicle whereby present and former members of NIP may be kept informed of developments in the naval intelligence community and of the activities and whereabouts of past shipmates.

Our members consist of active duty, retired and reserve officers, enlisted personnel, and civilians who serve or have served within the Naval Intelligence community, as well as those in certain other categories who qualify as a nonvoting subscriber.

The goal of Naval Intelligence Professionals (NIP) is to further knowledge of the art and science of naval intelligence. This goal will be achieved through NIP’s sponsorship of publications, services, and events that provide education on the past, present, and future of naval intelligence. NIP will foster an active professional development program, provide a venue for networking across the naval intelligence community of interest, and offer career support services to accelerate the professional growth of its members. It will engage the academic community and the private sector to assist in reaching its goal.

Name: EDN - European Defence Network
Description:
The European Defence Network (EDN) is a private initiative and association aiming at promoting the European perspective in Defence and Security. Targeting on young professionals and students from the relevant domains (international relations, politics, military, academia, defence industry), we aspire to have a long term impact by connecting, building trust, educating and inspiring for action.

As we are independent we can do this without a specific political agenda. Moreover, we have a highly interactive approach involving our members in the design and development of our organization and the execution of projects.
For our concept we have been awarded with a price by the French Minister of the Armed Forces Florence Parly in 2020 and the Citizenship award at the Berlin Security Conference in 2022. 

To support our ambition, we organise seminars in different European countries to engage with experts and leaders of defence, both local and European: Paris and Madrid in 2022, Tallinn in 2024, Brussels in 2025. 
We also publish content on various topics: a monthly press digest on European Defence, interviews, position papers, ...

Name: Marine Innovation Unit

Name: Forum de Défense et de Stratégie
Description:
The Forum de Défense et de Stratégie seeks to provide exchange platforms for European actors who work in the defence sector or in international relations. Seminars are regularly offered to bring together high-quality speakers and participants from several professional backgrounds: diplomats, soldiers, politicians, employees from defence-related industries, university professors, PhD students, experts from think tanks. 

We believe that the Franco-German cooperation is the basis of any sound and viable project for the development of a European defence capacity (and thus for a European strategic autonomy). This is true despite the fact that there are disagreements between Paris and Berlin. Our events aim at favouring meetings and exchange between speakers and participants from France and Germany. They also associate people coming from other EU countries and the Atlantic Alliance.

Name: C.A.S.I. - Centro Analisi e Studi Italus
Description:
Decifrare il presente. Anticipare il futuro.

Il Centro Analisi e Studi Italus (C.A.S.I.) nasce per informare, formare e creare consapevolezza. Attraverso analisi scientifiche e approfondimenti su geopolitica, sicurezza, economia globale, cybersicurezza e intelligenza artificiale, offriamo strumenti critici per interpretare il presente e prepararsi al futuro.

 La nostra missione
Fornire un’informazione affidabile, rigorosa e indipendente, per una comprensione più chiara delle dinamiche globali. Con un approccio scientifico e analitico, supportiamo accademici, professionisti e istituzioni nel prendere decisioni consapevoli.

 La nostra visione
Vogliamo essere la fonte primaria di analisi strategica, combinando autorevolezza e affidabilità. Non ci limitiamo a descrivere gli eventi, ma ne analizziamo le cause e prevediamo gli sviluppi futuri. Il nostro obiettivo è incidere sul dibattito pubblico e offrire strumenti utili per chi opera in settori chiave.

 Se cerchi analisi strategiche e approfondimenti di valore, segui il CASI per restare sempre un passo avanti.

Name: CSIS Missile Defense Project
Description: The Missile Defense Project at CSIS looks at a wide range of policy, program, and strategic issues related to missile defense. Technological and geopolitical factors have driven increased global supply and demand for high-velocity, unmanned, missile-based weapons and their corresponding counters. Besides more recognized cruise and ballistic missile threats, global missile proliferation now consists of a spectrum including precision-guided rockets, anti-ship missiles, air defenses, hypersonic delivery systems, and counterspace weapons.

Name: Military Innovation by Doing
Description:
MIND means Military Innovation by Doing and you will find this way of working in our DNA. 

MIND is the innovation centre of Commando Materieel en IT (COMMIT) for the Ministry of Defence in the Netherlands, and supports COMMIT in strengthening her innovative capabilities. MIND focuses on both technological and social innovation, assigns budget to promising ideas, and strengthens and develops partnerships in the field of innovation both inside and outside of the Ministry of Defence.

MIND consists of a diverse team both with civilians and military personnel that have knowledge of and experience in technological and operational developments, working with large cooperations, knowledge institutes and startups.

Name: Comanche Armament Corporation
Description:
Comanche Armament Corporation connects the world of military manufacturers, and trainers and brings them to the doorstep of our clients providing critical training and expanding the capabilities of current defense organizations geared towards Strategic Defence Systems.

Comanche Armament Corporation’s Object is the empowerment of African governments. With our years of work in the African Defence forces and communities we believe the future of African Defence Lies within the ability to have direct access to the Manufacturers and the experts in their fields, we specialize in connecting Military, Police, and Civil Defence sectors direct access to manufacturers from around the world allowing our clients optimized and afford turnkey solutions.

With multiple manufacturers under the Comanche Armament Corporation umbrella, we are able to provide full turn-key solutions direct to our clients in a single and simple package with Comanche Armament management directing all our partner's involvement into a single project saving our clients months of waiting time when delivering products and services

FOC tarjoaa palveluita kyvykkyyksien arvioinnista kansainvälisten verkostojen rakentamiseen ja asiantuntijatuen komplekseihin kilpailutuksiin. Tavoitteemme on tehdä suomalaisyrityksistä globaaleja menestyjiä vaativalla alalla.

Together with university researchers, we mature technology for a specific use case and bring it to market through our commercial partners.

Rooted in armed forces values like professionalism, discipline, and loyalty, our passion for defence drives our business.

Name: ROK Forum for Nuclear Strategy
Description:
Co-founded in October 29th, 2022 by Dr. Seong-chang Cheong (Director of the Center for Korean Peninsula Strategy) of Sejong Institute with several experts, the ROK Forum for Nuclear Strategy (ROKFNS, 한국핵안보전략포럼) is a nonprofit, nonpartisan research organization specialized in South Korea’s future nuclear deterrence strategy.

The forum is composed of members including Northeast Asian security experts, professors, nuclear engineers, advisors, North Korean defectors, former diplomats, politicians, aspiring young professionals, junior and senior researchers, defense analysts, undergraduates and graduates. 

The yearly membership is open to a selected number of people from various background in order to promote active discussions and exchanges for the forum’s fields of interests such as proliferation, nuclear strategy, deterrence, ROK-U.S. alliance, military technology, etc. For more information, please contact via rokfns@gmail.com.

◯ 설립 목적

1. 북한의 핵과 미사일 능력의 급속한 고도화로 인해 한국 국민들의 안보 불안감이 갈수록 커지고 있고, 남북한 간의 세력 불균형으로 인해 한반도 정세가 계속 불안정해지고 있으며, 북미관계도 계속 악화되고 있는 상황에서 한국의 독자적 핵무장을 통해 한국 국민들의 안보 불안감을 해소하고, 남북 핵균형을 통해 한반도 정세를 안정시키며, 한반도 문제를 한반도화함으로써 북미 적대관계를 완화하고 동북아 및 세계평화에 기여 

2. 포럼을 통해 한국의 독자적 핵무장에 대해 국제사회가 수용할 수 있는 합리적이고 정교한 논리들을 개발하고, 핵무장 과정 및 이후 남북한 간의 핵균형 및 핵군축과 남북관계 발전으로 나아가기 위한 정책 방향들을 제시 

3. 한국의 핵자강에 대한 우려와 부정적인 논리들을 극복하고 설득할 수 있는 체계적이고 정교한 논리 제시 

4. 핵자강을 통해 외교와 안보 분야에서 한국의 자율성을 더욱 확대하고, 한미동맹을 한국과 미국이 책임을 균형있게 분담하는 보다 건강한 동맹으로 발전시키는데 기여

5. 한국의 외교안보와 한미동맹을 업그레이드시킬 핵자강 옵션에 대한 범국민적, 초당적, 국제적 합의를 이끌어내기 위해 외교안보통일 분야의 전문가, 핵공학자, 문화예술인(영화 감독과 소설가, 가수, 만화가 등), 청년, 여성, 탈북민, 해외 전문가 및 동포 등이 광범위하게 참여하는 네트워크를 구축

Name: CAFCYBERCOM | COMCYBERFAC
Description:
Canadian Armed Forces Cyber Command (CAFCYBERCOM) leads all Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) cyber operations, joint electronic warfare (EW) and signals intelligence (SIGINT). We help protect Canada’s digital systems, carry out cyber operations and support CAF operations at home and abroad.

More information: https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/corporate/organizational-structure/cafcybercom.html

Nous aidons à protéger les systèmes numériques du Canada, menons des opérations dans le cyberespace et soutenons les opérations des FAC au pays et à l’étranger.

AATC not only enhances the readiness and lethality of the air reserve component, we amplify the entire Joint Force's technological superiority, directly advancing National Defense Strategy objectives.

Name: TENET Intelligence
Description:
TENET provides the strategic intelligence that aligns defense policy with industrial execution, enabling credible deterrence and informed diplomacy.

The security of Allied nations depends on an ecosystem that operates with speed and clarity. Today, that system is fractured. We exist to close the gap between national security strategy and private sector execution by providing a shared, objective reality built on data-driven analysis.

Our intelligence is delivered through two integrated platforms:

DAWN │ Defense Industrial Intelligence
DAWN translates complex budget strategies into actionable market signals. We provide the quantitative market sizing, supply chain analysis, and capability assessments necessary for effective investment, M&A, and corporate strategy within the defense sector.

MERIDIAN │ Diplomatic & Geopolitical Intelligence
MERIDIAN quantifies the full spectrum of national power. We integrate soft power modeling with a hard power index of over 200 indicators to support precision negotiation, coalition-building, and evidence-based policymaking.

We empower leaders to prevent conflict by providing the intelligence for principled action.

Learn more at tenetintel.com

Description: Om te beschermen wat ons dierbaar is, biedt WJB hét platform voor de verbinding tussen actieve officieren en oud-officieren der mariniers.
