Name: NATO Centre of Excellence for Military Medicine
Description: The NATO MILMED COE assists the Alliance in its goal of continuous transformation in the medical field. The COE is also the Department Head for the NATO Medical Support Discipline. The NATO MILMED COE has four medical branches, the Support Branch and the Directorate and it is overseen by the Steering Committee, which is composed of the delegates of each Sponsoring Nation. Sponsoring Nations are NATO Member States who chose to join the Centre. They delegate Subject Matter Experts and contribute to the Budget of the COE. The four medical Branches are the following: Force Health Protection Branch, Interoperability Branch, Lessons Learned and Innovation Branch, Training Branch. The Force Health Protection Branch is located in Munich, Germany, the rest of the Branches and supporting elements are located in Budapest, Hungary.

Name: AOBP
Description:
Asociace obranného a bezpečnostního průmyslu České republiky (AOBP) sdružuje firmy zabývající se výzkumem, vývojem, výrobou, obchodem a marketingem s obrannou a bezpečnostní technikou, materiálem a službami. Je nevládní, nezávislou, nepolitickou, neziskovou a neobchodní organizací, která byla založena v roce 1997. Již od svého založení je respektovaným a uznávaným partnerem vlády České republiky, ministerstev průmyslu a obchodu, obrany, vnitra a zahraničních věcí, s nimiž má podepsánu řadu dohod o spolupráci. AOBP udržuje v rámci České republiky partnerské vztahy s dalšími profesními organizacemi jako je Hospodářská komora či Svaz průmyslu a dopravy. Asociace spolupracuje s vydavatelstvím MS Line na vydávání časopisu Czech Defence Industry & Security Review, jenž je mediální platformou českého obranného a bezpečnostního průmyslu.

Asociace prosazuje podnikatelské a obchodní zájmy svých členů v dialogu s Parlamentem, ministerstvy a ostatními orgány státní správy. Stala se plnoprávným partnerem a účastníkem legislativních procesů. Diskusí u „kulatého stolu“ či osobními jednáními s představiteli státní administrativy a zákonodárné moci se podílí na řešení problematiky českého obranně-bezpečnostního průmyslu jako celku. AOBP je základním kontaktním místem pro obdobné zahraniční instituce a pomáhá firmám z celého světa hledat v České republice vhodné partnery. Obdobně pak slouží českým výrobcům při vyhledávání zahraničních společností vhodných pro průmyslovou spolupráci.

Podporuje úsilí firem českého obranného a bezpečnostního průmyslu pronikat na zahraniční trhy. V rámci svého postavení se podílí na proexportní politice České republiky a snaží se působit na orgány státní správy při prosazování exportních zájmů svých členů.

Name: Armament Technology Incorporated
Description: Armament Technology Incorporated specializes in professional-grade weapon sighting systems, including Optical Combat Weapon Sights, Long Range Precision Rifle Sights and OEM Mounting solutions. Name: Inter Populum: The Journal of Irregular Warfare and Special Operations
Description:
Inter Populum is a peer-reviewed academic journal dedicated to advancing scholarship at the intersection of irregular warfare and special operations. A product of Arizona State University's Competitive Statecraft Initiative and Media Enterprise, the journal serves as a platform for scholars, practitioners, and thought leaders to examine the human domain of warfare. Topics range from historical lessons and current best practices to the evolving nature of future conflict.

Our mission is to foster meaningful discussion, debate, and collaboration on critical issues shaping 21st-century strategic competition. The name Inter Populum—Latin for "among the people"—highlights our focus on the human element of irregular warfare and special operations, where influence, legitimacy, and control over populations are central to modern conflict.

We invite readers, contributors, and collaborators to join the conversation. Explore, engage, and submit your work to Inter Populum today!

Description:
The NATO Centre of Excellence for Cold Weather Operations (COE-CWO) is to deliver recognized expertise and experience related to the cold weather environment within all aspects of military operations.

Name: IDF Medical Corps חיל הרפואה
Description: The Medical Corps of the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) serves as the preeminent medical authority within the Israeli military establishment. This specialized Corps is entrusted with a multifaceted mission encompassing several critical domains. The Corps provides comprehensive medical services in combat scenarios and during training exercises, while also overseeing and delivering regular medical care for all service members across the IDF. Additionally, it offers professional mental assistance to the entire military personnel. The Medical Corps is continuously engaged in the education and certification of medical staff through the auspices of the Military Medical Academy. Concurrently, it conducts extensive scientific investigations to advance military medicine and maintains ongoing partnerships and exchanges with both domestic and global medical entities. The Israeli Medical Corps is dedicated to a continuous cycle of data collection, analysis, learning, investigation, research, and improvement. This rigorous approach is singularly focused on the paramount goal of saving human life in every medical encounter. Through this commitment to excellence and innovation, the Medical Corps strives to remain at the forefront of military medical practice and emergency response capabilities. We will always appreciate any reference towards us, and be humble to establish new relations with anyone interested whom share the same values as we pursue.

Name: Combat Methods & Innovation Division (CMI), IDF
Description: Combat Methods & Innovation (CMI) is a division in the Israel Defense Forces’ Planning Directorate. CMI was established in 2019 by the IDF Chief of General Staff under the innovation archipelago concept and has been a leading triumph of innovation in Israel since. Key efforts of the division include the Chief of the General Staff's Innovation Awards, the first-ever International Military Innovation Conference (IMIC), The Defense College for Innovation,  Challenge - the IDF center for innovation and entrepreneurship, establishing R&D communities and more.

Name: Downrange Operations and Training, LLC

Name: Stockton Center for International Law
Description:
The Stockton Center for International Law (SCIL) is an internationally recognized research institute for the study of international law and military operations that produces original analysis for national decision-makers, senior military leaders, scholars, and legal practitioners throughout the world in order to better grasp the role of international law in naval, Joint and Combined operations.

SCIL is a department in the Center for Naval Warfare Studies that produces original research and analysis of both international law and military operations to educate and inform military leaders, national decision makers, and international law practitioners and scholars. The results of this analysis shapes legal interpretations affecting U.S. and partner nation forces throughout the world.

Our program provides international law research, analysis, and teaching to support the development of the Navy’s future strategy, the education and training of Navy leadership, and the conduct of fleet, joint, and combined operations. We also provide legal support to wargames and advice to the Fleet and other Department of Defense entities on a variety of international and operational law issues.

We serve as the Primary Review Authority for the Commander’s Handbook on the Law of Naval Operations, and have participated in the Tallinn Manual, the San Remo Manual, the Woomera Manual, the Harvard Air and Missile Warfare Manual, and other influential publications.

Annually, the Stockton Center hosts legal research workshops on emergent issues drawing many of the world’s leading international law experts, including the annual Alexander C. Cushing International Law Conference.

SCIL publishes the International Law Studies journal, the U.S. Naval War College's journal for international law. First published in 1895, it is the oldest international law periodical in the United States. Each year, the journal publishes faculty peer-reviewed articles and analysis from the world’s leading experts.

Name: The MERT Club
Description: A club for former members of the UK Military's Medical Emergency Response Team (MERT) comprising of tri-service medical personnel, RAF aircrew & force protection personnel who operated in Southern Afghanistan between 2006-2014

Name: Warrant Officer Historical Foundation
Description: We thank, and are extremely grateful, to the individuals and organizations who have supported our efforts to preserve the history and heritage of the United States Army Warrant Officer.  In 2018, construction of Warrant Officer Historical Exhibits commemorating the 100th Birthday of the establishment of the Warrant Officer Corps in the Army Mine Planter Service of the Coast Artillery Corps.  We are also purchasing Paver Bricks honoring Warrant Officer Medal of Honor recipients and some other distinguished Warrant Officers for placement on the walkways at the National Museum of the United States Army, and local military museums throughout the United States.  We have also made a grant to the Army Historical Foundation for the Veterans Hall in the National Museum of the United States Army (NMUSA). Name: U.S. Army Financial Management Command
Description:
Welcome to the official U.S. Army Financial Management Command page on LinkedIn. 

USAFMCOM enables the readiness of America’s Army by serving as the Army’s focal point for all finance and comptroller operations while providing FC capabilities that facilitate accountability, auditability and stewardship. USAFMCOM conducts enterprise-level financial operations and provides technical coordination for FC units and commands across the Army in order to ensure the effective implementation of policies and programs to support optimally resourcing the Army.

USAFMCOM is responsible for Army FC functional support in the areas of systems, audit and compliance, financial operations, and Enterprise Resource Planning business standardization. The command also provides Army-wide, unique actions such as operational oversight of field FC activities, FC unit technical training, electronic commerce and classified finance and accounting oversight. In doing so, the two-star command also provides FC expertise and coordination on the adequacy of finance policies, as well as systems and reporting requirements to units at all levels throughout the Army and to the Defense Finance and Accounting Service.

USAFMCOM is comprised of five directorates: Army Financial Services, Audit Response Center, Business Process Management, Systems Support Operations, and Military Pay Operations. Each directorate serves a distinct – but equally vital – task within both Army finance and information systems support.

Name: Naval Service Training Command
Description:
Naval Service Training Command transforms volunteers into service professionals who embody the Navy’s core values and warrior ethos. Through disciplined training and education, NSTC forges civilians into Sailors and into Naval and Marine Corps Officers. This is the Foundry that strengthens the Fleet.

NSTC ensures the Fleet is supplied with leaders ready to serve, strengthen, and fight on behalf of the Nation through Recruit Training Command, Officer Training Command Newport, and the Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps. 

NSTC also leads the Navy Junior Reserve Officers Training Corps, a citizenship development program that empowers high school students to grow as leaders, embrace civic responsibility, and pursue excellence in life.

Name: Partnership for Peace Consortium
Description:
The Partnership for Peace Consortium (PfPC) is a multinational governmental program. Its mission is to “strengthen partner defense education and security sectors while fostering a trusted global network of defense educators, research professionals, and security sector practitioners.”

The PfPC was established in 1999. Today, the PfPC network includes over 800 defense academies and security studies institutes, and it works actively in dozens of nations. The PfPC seeks to build intellectual interoperability, foster security sector cooperation, and support institutional capacity building. This work is carried out through various permanent and ad-hoc working groups, the Connections quarterly scholarly journal, and co-management of the Defense Education Enhancement Program (DEEP) in conjunction with NATO. 

Strategically, the PfPC is overseen by a Senior Advisory Council comprised of military representatives at the general/flag officer or executive level from the United States, Germany, Austria, Bulgaria, Canada, Poland, Sweden, Switzerland, and the NATO International Staff. Operationally, PfPC activities are managed by the Secretariat. It is situated within the George C. Marshall Center (Garmisch, Germany), a bilateral military facility sponsored by the US and Germany.

Visit our website to stay informed on the latest developments of the PfPC: http://pfp-consortium.org/

Name: Kampkraft
Description:
Kampkraft er podcasten, hvor vi styrker samtalen om Danmarks forsvar, teknologi og industri i en tid hvor Europas sikkerhed er under pres. Med udgangspunkt i aktuelle beslutninger og strategiske behov inviterer værterne Alex Mathar og Anders Havelund Krøyer nøglepersoner fra det politiske, militære og industrielle landskab til åbne samtaler om, hvordan vi bedst ruster Danmark til fremtiden.

Podcastens formål er at synliggøre det komplekse samspil mellem industri og forsvar, skabe gennemsigtighed i milliardinvesteringerne i dansk forsvar, og fremme forståelsen af, hvordan teknologi, innovation og samarbejde spiller sammen i opbygningen af et stærkt og modstandsdygtigt samfund.

Vi ønsker, at aktører fra både det offentlige og private kan lære af hinandens erfaringer og at startups og industrielle spillere får bedre mulighed for at forstå vejen ind i forsvarsøkosystemet. Samtidig vil vi give borgerne indsigt i, hvad der er på spil, og hvorfor det angår os alle.

Kampkraft skal være en platform for dialog, refleksion og fremdrift i forsøget på at styrke vores fælles resiliens og forsvarsevne.

Podcasten er sponsoreret af ARComply og produceres i samarbejde med TechSavvy.

Our engineers work across multiple disciplines – Marine Engineering, Weapon Engineering and Aviation Engineering – each offering unique challenges and requiring expert problem-solving under pressure. Whether deployed at sea, stationed onshore, or embedded in specialist support units, they play a critical role in sustaining the Royal Navy’s operational advantage.

Combining technical excellence, hands-on leadership, and resilience in demanding environments, the Engineering Branch is essential to delivering maritime power today and innovating for the future.

Name: 35e régiment d'artillerie parachutiste
Description:
L’action du 35e RAP consiste à appuyer par les feux de tous types (sol-sol, sol-air, air-sol, appui feu naval) l’action dans la profondeur tactico-opérative de la 11e brigade parachutiste, d’acquérir du renseignement et de surveiller le champ de bataille, de coordonner et conduire les feux au niveau brigade et d’assurer l’intégration et la coordination de l’ensemble des intervenants dans la 3e dimension.

 Unique régiment de l’armée de terre à posséder la double spécificité artillerie et parachutiste, le 35e RAP se caractérise par plusieurs spécificités : 
-  la capacité d’engagement dans l’urgence : spécialiste de l’action dans et par la 3e dimension, le 35e RAP est l’unique régiment d’artillerie à armer une alerte permanente à 12h et 48h (contrat opérationnel) dans le cadre de l’échelon national d’urgence des troupes aéroportées (ENU TAP).  
 - la capacité de mise à terre par aérolargage de tous ses matériels : des capteurs (observateurs, drones, radars) aux effecteurs (Mistral/Mortier). Le CAESAR est aérotransportable par A400M. 
- Un groupement des commandos parachutistes : spécialiste des actions commandos et de l’infiltration sous voile dans la profondeur, le GCP marque les zones de saut pour les troupes aéroportées et cible ou neutralise des objectifs à haute valeur ajoutée loin derrière les lignes ennemies. 
- Un détachement d’acquisition dans la profondeur : formé à l’infiltration à pieds ou en véhicule léger derrière les lignes ennemies, le DAP renseigne et est en mesure de participer aux actions de ciblage de la 11BP.

 Constitué de près de 900 combattants d’active renforcés d’environ 200 réservistes, le 35e RAP se compose d’une batterie de commandement et de logistique (B.C.L), d’une batterie d’acquisition et de surveillance (B.A.S), de quatre batteries d’appui sol-sol (B1, B2, B3, B5), d’une batterie de défense anti-aérienne sol-air (B4), d’une batterie de réserve (UIR).

Name: DetectaChem
Description:
DetectaChem is a privately-held company and manufacturer of the rapidly deployable, handheld, intelligent, and easy-to-operate detection system called the Seeker. The Seeker enables real-time safety and security threat detection throughout the world. DetectaChem products are simple to use and maintain, yet are sophisticated in function and design. The Seeker utilizes proprietary patent pending processes to deploy automated colorimetric analysis of extremely small traces of explosive and narcotic materials. The company has developed a proprietary card collection system that is used to acquire a sample and contains the necessary components for the colorimetric reaction.

The Seeker uses technology related to color reactions that have been commonly used in the field of molecular analysis. This liquid chemistry based process, enables the Seeker to rapidly perform on-site diagnostic detection of explosive and narcotic materials. DetectaChem products are multi-lingual allowing for global deployment.

Name: New York Air National Guard
Description:
The New York Air National Guard (NYANG) is the largest and most storied Air National Guard organization in the US. We're a dynamic team serving both state and federal missions. The majority of our members serve part-time, generally one weekend a month, while also maintaining full-time civilian employment or attending college.

As the Air National Guard's largest and most diverse state force, we're comprised of 5 Wings, the Eastern Air Defense Sector, and 3 Geographically Separated Units, operating 7 distinct weapons systems across multiple domains. Our 24/7/365 mission sets have a global impact, reaching all 7 continents. Our units include the 105th Airlift Wing (Stewart ANGB, Newburgh), 106th Rescue Wing (Gabreski ANGB, Westhampton Beach), 107th Attack Wing (Niagara Falls ARS), 109th Airlift Wing (Stratton ANGB, Scotia), 174th Attack Wing (Hancock Field ANGB, Syracuse), and the Eastern Air Defense Sector (EADS, Rome).

With a rich history dating back to 1915, the NYANG has a proud tradition of excellence and service. Our airmen have deployed worldwide, supporting operations and humanitarian missions. We're committed to serving our local community, responding to natural disasters and participating in outreach programs. Visit our website at https://dmna.ny.gov/ang/ to learn more about our units, careers, and how you can join our team!

Today, the Mighty Ninety operates Minuteman III ICBMs on full alert 24 hours a day, 365 days a year.

The 90th Missile Wing employs approximately 3,361 military members and 964 civilian employees. Family members of assigned military members add another approximately 5,445 to the local population. Also, some 5,000 military retirees reside in the area.
Location: F. E. Warren Air Force Base, Wyoming

Name: Combined Joint Operations from the Sea
Description:
The Combined Joint Operations from the Sea Centre of Excellence (CJOS COE), established in May 2006, serves as a global leader in supporting transformation through concept development, experimentation, and the creation of doctrine and standards. With 11 nations represented, CJOS COE is the only Centre of Excellence in the United States and one of 30 NATO-accredited Centres worldwide, offering a vast wealth of international experience and expertise.

Independent from the NATO Command structure, CJOS COE draws on the knowledge and capabilities of sponsoring nations, U.S. Fleet Forces, U.S. SECOND Fleet, and neighboring U.S. commands. This collaboration enables us to promote and implement "best practices" within the NATO Alliance, specifically in the realm of maritime-based joint operations.

Our close partnerships with Allied Command Transformation (ACT), Allied Maritime Command (MARCOM), other NATO commands, maritime COEs, and national commands help shape NATO’s transformational goals and address the complex and emerging maritime security challenges of today’s global landscape. We are committed to advancing the ability of NATO and its partners to effectively conduct Allied combined joint operations from the sea, enhancing maritime security worldwide.

● Deliver structured, time-sensitive OSINT assessments to a curated base, combining imagery analysis, strategic forecasting, situational assessments, thought pieces, and escalation monitoring.
● Maintain strict source validation protocols, uphold posting integrity under operational uncertainty, and ensure output meets professional analytic standards.
● Coordinate on dissemination priorities, tone calibration, and situational framing across military, political, and strategic layers.
● Integrate open-source imagery, maritime tracking data, and media feeds into concise, high-reliability briefings.
● Focus areas include military asset and defense posture, conflict zone mapping, and regional normalization or escalation trends.

Recognized for maintaining analytical composure, judgment, and discretion under pressure.

Need sniper sequences with bite? Room-clearing scenes that feel raw and right? Dialogue that sounds like it was torn from a debrief? We’re embedded from pre-production through post to make sure your story hits hard and lands true.
 
We don’t fake it. We train it, live it and film it.

Name: BE KIND RESCUE
Description:
BE KIND RESCUE provides professional front-line conflict support solutions. A volunteer and donation-based venture where excellence exists across every facet of our brand. We provide highly trained professionals, deploying them to save lives in high-risk environments, and supporting them with the very best equipment available.

When it comes to conflict between nations, BE KIND RESCUE will always locate our bases within the territory of the defending party. However, when it comes to the provision of our support and rescue services, we provide the same professional level of care to all those who need our support, regardless of their nationality, culture or views. Every person we assist is a human being.

This organisation is the brainchild of Gareth Minor, a UK-based 53-year-old Management Consultant and business troubleshooter. Having followed the crisis in Ukraine closely, and having observed the sometimes amateurish approach employed in front-line search and rescue, Gareth realised an improved approach to such operations would benefit all involved. So, as we approach the one-year anniversary of the Russian invasion of Ukraine, BE KIND RESCUE intends to launch its first mission to Ukraine in February 2023. A detailed plan has been formed containing an essential equipment list and training requirements, and we now wish to push forward at pace to bring this mission to life through public and corporate donations.

In order to deliver this humanitarian mission, BE KIND RESCUE must generate income urgently. This income will fund the purchase of professional equipment, professional training, and will cover all mission-based expenses. Once the model is tested and refined during the Ukraine conflict, we may investigate the feasibility of bringing on additional teams and moving training in-house to save unnecessary expenditure.

Name: MARA Defence
Description:
MARA Defence is a Polish defence technology company specializing in the development of autonomous C-UAS (Counter-Unmanned Aerial Systems). 

Our mission is to deliver innovative, effective, and cost-efficient solutions that counter the most urgent threats on the modern battlefield.

The primary threat we address is the proliferation of small, fast, and low-cost FPV drones, which have revolutionized tactics and created a critical gap in existing defence systems. In response, we created WARTa, our flagship product.

WARTa is an ultralight, portable, and fully autonomous kinetic system that provides point and area defence. 

Its key advantages are:
Autonomy: Our proprietary AI system independently performs the full defence cycle: scanning, detection, tracking, and neutralization, freeing the operator for other critical tasks.

Portability: The system can be transported and deployed by a two-person team in under 5 minutes, providing protection anywhere it's needed.

Cost-Effectiveness: Using standard ammunition (5.56/7.62mm) dramatically lowers the cost per engagement, solving the critical issue of cost asymmetry.

Modularity: WARTa is a platform technology, not a single-function device. Its modular architecture opens multiple markets, including public security and civilian applications.

We are a team of engineers, designers, and managers with deep, cross-industry expertise in AI and robotics. 

We are applying our proven technological capabilities to solve critical challenges in the defence and security sector.

Contact us to learn more about our technology and partnership opportunities.

Name: 2nd Battalion, 9th Marines
Description:
2/9 was an infantry battalion of the United States Marine Corps. Formed during World War I, the unit played an instrumental role in the defeat of the Japanese forces in the Battles of Guam and Iwo Jima during World War II. The battalion distinguished itself in the defense of Khe Sanh during the Vietnam War, and later participated in an ill-fated invasion of Koh Tang Island in Southeast Asia, with the intention of rescuing the crew of SS Mayaguez. During Operation Desert Storm, the battalion served as the lead battalion for the III Marine Expeditionary Force .

2nd Battalion 9th Marines served until September 2, 1994, when it was deactivated to make room for one of three light armor reconnaissance battalions. It was part of the 9th Marine Regiment and the 3rd Marine Division. On July 13, 2007, 2nd Battalion 9th Marines was re-activated again as part of the 6th Marine Regiment and replaced the Anti-Terrorism Battalion.

 On April 1, 2015, it was deactivated as part of a post-war drawdown.
